title: Knowing What Matters. 😉
caption:
Those that follow me and have read the Knowledge Transfer Series know that news released on Fridays after the close is never good.

Why? Because this is the best TIME to legally comply ..... but shield unfavorable company news from the market. Friday? after 4 pm?

Wolves can't trade on it. Most are at a bar by now anyway. Best chance for them to forget about it anyway.

Sneaky SOBs.

I immediately sold my stake in Verde Clean Fuels after the news today. 😂 (Still holding 2028 warrants).

This company was working with Diamondback Energy to use flared gas (excess gas present during oil extraction that is burned into the atmosphere) for a green project.

The project just ended and Diamondback pulled out.

Here's the news:

https://finance.yahoo.com/news/verde-clean-fuels-inc-announces-211500499.html

Note why the project ended:

"....announced today the suspension of development of its Permian Basin project primarily as a result of changing market conditions driven by increasing demand for natural gas in the Permian Basin."

PRIMARILY DRIVEN BY INCREASING DEMAND FOR NATURAL GAS IN THE PERMIAN BASIN.

Ohhhhhh Shit! For those not versed in O&G, that means they need every molecule of natural gas they can get!!!! 🤑 🤑 🤑

In other words.....there will be no more gas flared.

This is incredibly bullish for any company that touches natural gas. 💰 🤑 💸 💲 🪙 💶 💸

Knowing what matters and what actions to take. Membership opens early next week. This is going to be fun! 😁

I hope you'll join. 🏆

This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️ 🌊

title: Anyone Doubting Who Holds the Cards Anymore?
caption:
India Weighs Cheap Russian Oil Against Costly U.S. Trade Commitments


Russia’s flagship crude, Urals, is being offered in India at a widening discount to Brent, with the differential now at $11 per barrel and testing the appetite of Indian refiners amid the trade deal with the U.S. that calls for limited purchases of Russian oil.

Sellers are offering Urals at an $11 per barrel discount this week, widened from about $9 a barrel ten days ago, anonymous traders involved in the offerings told Bloomberg on Wednesday.

Typically, such steep discounts would have Indian refiners rushing to lock in purchases.

However, the U.S.-India trade deal and lower tariffs for Indian products in the United States depend on India slashing imports of Russian crude oil.

Refiners in India are reportedly waiting for government guidance on how to proceed, if at all, with Russian oil purchases.

Following the trade deal announced by U.S. President Donald Trump, Indian refiners seek clarification about Russian oil imports from their government, with some pre-emptively halting purchases, sources with knowledge of the matter told Bloomberg earlier this week.

President Trump broke the news of a deal with India on Monday, saying the U.S. would reduce tariffs on Indian imports in exchange for a commitment on the part of New Delhi to stop buying crude oil from Russia and boost purchases of American oil instead, along with other goods and commodities.

The deal would also grant Indian energy buyers access to Venezuelan crude and maybe even Iranian crude, as suggested by the U.S. President, providing alternatives to Russian crude.

India, the world’s third-largest crude oil importer, dramatically raised its imports of cheap Russian crude following Moscow’s invasion of Ukraine in early 2022.

For nearly four years, India imported so much Russian crude that Russia became its single biggest oil supplier, accounting for about a third of all imported crude.

But now refiners are temporarily halting Russian oil purchases and seeking clarification. Regardless of the cheap Russian crude currently on offer, India will have to take into account the U.S. deal and its implications.
https://oilprice.com/Latest-Energy-News/World-News/India-Weighs-Cheap-Russian-Oil-Against-Costly-US-Trade-Commitments.html

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #91 - Price vs. TIME
caption:
O Beskar, Beskar! wherefore art thou Beskar?
For those that care, I have been running around trying to get my laptop fixed!  The processor fan (and apparently the entire heat sink failed) started grinding then abruptly quick working. A quick CTRL+ALT+DEL forced shutdown and then my computer was beeping critical error warnings without startup!!!! All while working to launch the upcoming Beskar membership!
But I am back now…..and as we cross 9,300 followers, I owe you another KTS post!  So let’s get right after it!  Pour that Sunday morning coffee, cuddle up, and turn on the neurons!
I haven’t caught up with Blossom yet but I’m certain the collapse in the precious metals market on Friday is the hottest topic (Beskar’s portfolio dropped -5.9% while $GLD and $SLV dropped -11.5% and -28.5%, respectively !).  So we’ll use this KTS as a TIMELY one to address what to expect going forward.
Price vs. TIME . 
Many of you that follow me know that this is a favorite topic of mine.  How many TIMES does a new follower ask, “is this still a good buy?” ….. or …… my favorite …… “what price target do you have?”
What’s the response??? 
Wolves control prices.  I invest in TIME within the context of the real estate/banking crisis cycle.
So what’s that mean, Beskar? 
Great question!  First, we have to accept that wolves are fast moving, greedy, beady-eyed, carnivorous predators that thrive on price movements.  They don’t care if a security goes up or down.  It just needs to move …. for them to find a kill to eat. Price goes up….. they short it.  Price goes down, they buy it.  The quicker (velocity of price change), the better….so they can move on for more and more meat!  Were you a wolf meal on Friday? (aka …. did you sell?)
But when you invest in TIME within the context of the cycle, you willingly absorb these price fluctuations in exchange for compounded returns over the secular trend by stepping back and observing the longer-dated charts (KTS #15).  For newbies, that means buying low and holding through these wolf oscillations…….AS LONG AS THE SECULAR TREND IS IN PLACE.  Get it?  Read this paragraph again.  Again.  Again.  And re-read KTS #66.
You have seen those compounded Beskar CALL LEAP trades rolling off like a printing press over the last month, right? You get those when you make moves on TIME, not PRICE.  Get it?  Good.  Let’s move on.
See, the important part of this approach, as Kenny Rodgers would say, is “knowing when to hold ‘em, when to walk away, and when to run.”  So let’s take a deep dive into this question:
IS THIS THE END OF THE SECULAR TREND IN PRECIOUS METALS?
First, welcome to 2026. We knew that this will be the year of extreme volatility … and here in the first month starting off with a BANG!
To break this down, I took the TIME to mark up the 1-year $GLD chart.  Here’s some observations:
*Did you forget about the -11.3% drop over 12 days in GLD from 10/20/25 to 11/4/2025? (See chart).
*Did you forget about the -3.9% drop over just 4 days in GLD from 11/12/25 to 11/17/2025? (See chart)
*Did you forget about the -5.2% drop over just 4 days in GLD from 12/26/25 to 12/31/2025? (See chart)
Of course you did!  Now, this -11.5% drop in 1 day is likely more memorable than those -1% per day grind lower for the above periods of TIME. But, will you be cursing this drop with regrets later this year when we look back?  The answer is no.  And here’s why:
(1)   You are not a wolf! You can’t run with this pack. None of us can.  That’s why we pluck ‘em from the tree stands! The pack coordinates as a group and you aren’t invited. They decide how to jerk price movements and they ain’t sharing that with you! 
 
So is this the classic end of the month rebalancing pack? Is it those that know the script of “how goes January is how goes the year” pack and knows that 2026 is most volatile and play their part?  Or is the collusion of the banks and the government pack of wolves? Those that follow me know that banks and governments are ONE AND THE SAME – they work together to control the earnings.  You know how CME (bank) keeps upping the margins on gold, silver, palladium, and platinum and we get drops?  Or isn’t it particularly TIMELY that TRUMP out of nowhere announces his new Fed Chair appointee Kevin Warsh on Friday way ahead of schedule? Governments and Banks. Or is it just a reaction by wolves – a temper tantrum of sorts – that Kevin Warsh is more hawkish (cares more about inflation than unemployment) and will resist rate cuts to help stocks climb higher? Or is it a great “squeeze” event like the late 1970’s Hunt Brothers?  Maybe it’s just wolves feasting on your latecoming weak-handed retail peers rotating from Tech/Bitcoin to Metals?  A flush ‘em out?
 
Oh, the noise, noise, noise, noise! They'll bang on tong-tinglers. They'll blow their floo-flounders. They'll crash on jang-jinglers and bounce on boing-bounders! Then Whos young and old would sit down to a feast, and they'll feast and they'll feast.  And they'll feast, feast, feast, feast! They'll eat their Who-pudding, and rare Who roast beast!
 
Instead?  Recognize that none of this matters.  Skip the latest YouTube video on the complexities and particulars of this move.  Instead…stay focused on secular trend within the context of the cycle.
 
(2)   Study the Beskar four pillars (KTS #80) of investing:  Technicals, Fundamentals, Money Flows, and TIME.  First up, let’s look at the Technicals – study the ticker tape.  Reference the $GLD chart below carefully.  I added each of those events (that you forgot about – red, blue, and yellow circles) below along with the current move (orange circle) that we’re currently experiencing.  The secular trend is marked with that green line in the chart. Looks to me like $GLD should come down to $420.00 in the short-term? I won’t be surprised.  And I certainly won’t panic.  Will you?  Probably!
 
I want you to also look at the volume – the column chart - just beneath the price chart.  Notice when the volume spikes over the past year, so do the price changes.  The more volume, the more price change.  That’s why this current move may feel like a gut punch if you are invested in metals. 
 
(3)   Next pillar?  Fundamentals.  Look at the stick figure I drew.  That’s the rolling EPS for Gold. Obviously, this chart keeps getting revised upward as the price of gold climbs. But go ahead and look at miners in gold, silver, and other metals.  Remember KTS #17 and #75 A/B/C?  That stick figure is going to keep climbing?  You know why?  Because KTS #28 told us that miners are basing estimates on a gold price of $3,500 and a silver price on $50.  The miners are just getting ready to report and those that did – skyrocketing moves (e.g. $TRX).  So have no fear!  Onward we go!
 
(4)   Next pillar?  Money flows.  KTS #25.  Re-read so as not to miss this one!  But you know already that since 2022, the central banks around the world are buying up as much gold as possible. You heard Tether continues to stockpile for gold-backed currency amongst the world wide debt.  At the virtual moment of this “crash”, JP Morgan adjusted their price target for gold to $8,000 !?!?!  And CIBC adjusted their price target on proxy silver miner, Hecla Mining, from $27 to $32.  We’re looking at a ~50% return on this miner from current prices if that target is hit.  And with the volatility in 2026, that could be any day now!?!?!?  What’s on our side is that the dollar broke and closed below the lows of 2025.  That violation is enough to convince virtually every market participant that despite near-term fluctuations, there is only one direction for $DXY to continue to trend.  With the average hedge fund owning just 2% of their portfolio in precious by the end of 2025, a simple doubling of this allocation to 4% means massive future inflows into it.  There isn’t enough gold in the world to satisfy this……so the money will really start seeking out the profitable miners.  So here’s an opportunity, eh?
 
(5)   The last pillar, TIME?  The secular green line in the chart.  This most neglected financial parameter of them all is the crystal ball.  We know that at this Winner’s Curse stage of the meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle that commodities just continue to climb.  We know also that historically, Gold does well in fear about the banking system, a declining dollar, inflationary pressures, and geopolitical conflicts.  Every day the case for Gold just keeps getting stronger and stronger.  And don’t forget Silver.  While silver tends to better when the market is humming rather than safe haven searching, the increasing GDPs and interest in it is just now starting.  You can do the same for the SLV as I did for GLD here to see the same knowing that historically Silver is way more volatile than Gold.  As a fun exercise, I also included a max chart of gold/silver miner proxy, $CDE - going back to the late 1970’s.  For two reasons.  One, I suggest you download the image and mark a circle on this chart where you see Friday’s steep -16.8% drop.  And realize the context within the secular trend that just broke out of a 13-year hibernation!?!?!?  Context is everything.  Oh yeah, the second reason I chose $CDE?  I like to plant nuggets in posts for reference later on.
 
Oh, be still my beating heart?
So what now for you, Beskar?

title: Tale of Two: JAPAN 🇯🇵 vs CANADA 🇨🇦
caption:
KTS #13. 🔮

It's two very different approaches.

While it doesn't capture the headline news with the flood of daily events, the United States of America 🇺🇸 is undergoing an Allied Asian Invasion! In a welcome way! 🙏

TRUMP'S call to friendly nations around the world to invest in the United States and the re-industrialization of it is attracting massive foreign investment from allies.Taiwan, South Korea 🇰🇷 and Japan 🇯🇵 .

Let's take Japan as an example. The United States loves and welcomes Japanese investment. Perhaps our most favored partner. And Japanese companies have contributed massively to our productive economy and their presence has greatly improved our standard of living.

So what are they doing these days?

* Back in June 2025, Nippon Steel bought out iconic steel maker U.S. Steel and committed to investing $11 billion into the USA and a new facility.

* And recently, Japanese power and gas companies pursued direct upstream ownership of the Haynesville LNG (liquefied natural gas) heavy resource basin near the Gulf of America.

- In April 2025, Tokyo Gas bought a 70% interest in Chevron's shale gas for $525 million.

- In October 2025, JERA, Japan's largest power generator, purchased $1.5 billion of Haynesville shale gas assets from Williams Companies.

- In June 2025, reported negotiations between Mitsubishi Corporation and Aethon Energy Management for an $8 billion purchase of Haynesville shale gas assets. That deal closed today for $7.53 billion.

Japan is investing in America in a big way - as they always have! 🇺🇸 🤝 🇯🇵

Canada 🇨🇦 is seemingly taking a different approach. Instead, Canada is looking to expand their relationship with China 🇨🇳, citing that China is "more predictable" than it's neighbor to the south.

New prime minister Mark Carney just completed a high stakes meeting with Xi Jinping yesterday. First meeting with China since 2017.

So what came of that meeting?

PM Carney agreed to rollback tariffs on the first 49,000 Chinese electric vehicles from Trudeau's 100% tariff to a now mere 6.1% tariff. "For Canada to build its own competitive EV sector, we will need to learn from innovative partners, access their supply chains, and increase local demand", Carney said. He went on to say the EV pact would drive "considerable" Chinese investment into Canada's auto sector, create good careers in Canada and speed it towards a net zero future.

(Opinion interjection: Un huh. 😂 A green scam snake in the grass!🐍 Is anyone surprised that Brookfield's Carney is ready to take aim at the oil and gas industry demand in Canada? Where exactly does he think those equalization payments originate from? Canadian cold and EVs. 🤪 😜 😂)

Under the new deal between 🇨🇦 & 🇨🇳, Carney expects China will lower their tariffs on its canola seed to 15% from 84%.

(Don't you just love how there's no bitching about ridiculous tariff levels between these two countries! 😂 🤣 Imagine that - Liberal hypocrites! 😁)

Carney said Canada will double it's energy grid over the next 15 years, adding that there are opportunities for Chinese partnership in investments including offshore wind.

But perhaps the most noteworthy meeting takeaway is that Carney 🇨🇦 also had discussions with Xi 🇨🇳 about Greenland saying "I found much alignment of views in that regard."

So as the world ramps up defense spending amid ever rising geopolitical conflict, Canada 🇨🇦 is choosing to woo China 🇨🇳 ...... while relying on the 🇺🇸 to protect them. (How much of a hit would that national Healthcare spending take if 🇺🇸 forces Canada to shoulder more of it? 🤔)

Meanwhile, the United States of America 🇺🇸 woos Japan 🇯🇵 .

Canada is making a bold move without having the cards to support it.

"Every nation deserves its government. "
- 26th U.S. President Theodore Roosevelt

Staying abreast of macroeconomic events can help an investor stay ahead of the pack ..... of short-sighted wolves.

title: Knowledge Transfer Series#90-Steadfast Discernment
caption:
Added another 700 followers since the last Knowledge Transfer Series .... I definitely owe you another one! 😁 👍

I have been out over a 3-day weekend having made a road trip to BuffaLouie's in Bloomington, Indiana to witness the destruction of the Oregon Ducks on the national stage with my daughter and her friends while my son attends a soccer camp. Hoo! Hoo! Hoo! Hoosiers! 😄 🏈 🏟 🏆

So let's lock in!

Every day ..... from now until market 🤮 , you will hear the word TRUMP. TRUMP this, TRUMP that. The news will just take the bait and regurgitate.

And if the media dares to stop talking about him, he'll do something to make sure they talk about him. Like it or not, get used to it. Not a single day of pardon now.

So what are the headlines?

-TRUMP proposes 50-yr mortgage!?!? That's crazy. 🤪 😜 That would mean most will NEVER become a true homeowner in their LIFETIME. Buy at 30. Die at 80. Your last breath will precede your final mortgage payment. Yikes!!! Many will die owing the bank and having your offspring on the hook or lose all that lifelong equity!!!

-TRUMP proposes a 1-year freeze on credit card interest at a max cap of 10% vs. the 25%+ in most cases!?!?! That's crazy too! 🤪 😜 TRUMP's lifelong democratic tendencies to fuel further spending by finding more money for tapped out consumers can turn what would be a financial hangover into an immediate overdose! 😫 😥

-TRUMP initiating a massive buying program of mortgage-backed securities in a futile attempt to lower interest rates. This crazy can't stop!!! 🤪 😜 He's in every corner trying to find morsels of money to give to those that live paycheck-to-paycheck ..... so they can leverage their life away to the banks ..... in a shop til you drop ...... permanently. 💀 ☠️

-TRUMP borrowing indigenous (😂 🤣) US Senator Elizabeth Warren's long-standing policy proposal to ban institutional investors from buying residential homes to increase supply and reduce prices of existing stock. And yes ..... of course Senator Warren now thinks it's a bad idea ( 😂 🤣 .... those Libs! ). Crazy can't stop! 🤪 😜 And they can't solve the problem. The problem is that these kids can't afford these prices. Their credit sucks with an average at 40% of their annual income allocated to bank interest, they can't afford the down-payment amounts, and most importantly ..... they can't afford the mortgage ..... period.

-TRUMP in Venezuela and Iran as a daily news flow. Is it oil? Is it critical minerals? Is it drugs? Is it democracy? Is it war on Communism? Is it nation building? Or is it to save the petrodollar to sustain American greatness and reserve currency status?

-TRUMP to occupy Greenland? 🇺🇸 has occupied Greenland since 1946. Nothing new. Previous presidents tried to buy it twice before.

-TRUMP suponea of Jerome Powell to force the interest rate cuts. Crazy on steroids!!! 🤪 😜

-TRUMP going to cut citizen checks from all of the tariff income. Some $2,000 - $10,000 disbursements to taxpayers from international sources. This one is not so crazy as there's more than 90 countries have a sovereign wealth fund to benefit their citizenry. 🤑

-TRUMP BBB to create the largest tax cuts in history! Also, not crazy. But a sign of the TIME!

This can go on and on.

It will be EVERY DAY you hear his name until your portfolio tanks. Per cycle, per script.

So what's the post about exactly, Beskar? 🤪 😜

😂 🤣 Great question. It's all about the real estate/banking crisis cycle. And the tool you must develop now - Steadfast Discernment.

put simply: Continued focus on the big picture of what matters.

You cannot survive what's coming and manage your portfolio successfully through it without knowing what to filter and what signals what's coming.

Here in 2026, we are getting very near the peak. ALL of this news flow is confirmation. At the same TIME......NONE of this news flow is new. None of it. It's all happened before. Many STIMES. Just different characters. Different ideas. All as an attempt to avoid the crisis.

This is par for the Winners Curse stage of the meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle.

Those that follow me know that we were expecting the massive liquidity injections via government interjections and interventions.

Well, it's here.

And we just need to step back and lump all of this news (and the details of it) into ONE BROAD AND GENERAL clarity of insight ..... liquidity injections and desperate government intervention TIME.

TRUMP is doing his absolute best to do what's never been done before - breaking the cadence of the real estate/banking crisis cycle.

And he won't succeed either.

It doesn't matter who is president. It doesn't matter what new methods or policies are brought. It doesn't matter who is in what position and what their intentions are.

All debt is eventually captured by the banks through the ownership of land. Period.

The cycle must reset. And it will. And we must recognize the GOOD in this. Young people will be able to afford homes again. 😃 😁 😄 👍

But the ugliness we will go through to get there? It can absolutely CRUSH your portfolio. To the point that I bet half of current Blossomers will never again want to talk about stocks. 😫 🤢 🤮 😠 😡 🤬

So let's lock in and think about how to prepare.

(1) TIME to eliminate as much debt as possible. Get it out of here!!!! The banks will be offering easy credit. At this stage of the cycle.....that's equivalent to a fat, juicy worm on a hook! 🪝 🐟

(2) While the masses of retail rush to open margin accounts (have you seen the growth in these !?!?! 🤪 😜), it's likely TIME to do just the opposite - get rid of any margin loans. It's tough ..... because liquidity injections, easy credit, and lower rates is a positive catalyst for stocks to go parabolic. But you WILL get burned if you are not in the right stocks! This is tricky.....because if you are in the right stocks, you can keep that portfolio going in the right direction. 😉 🏆

(3) Magnificent 7. It's early .... but it's TIME to check out. Everyone is on to the AI overinvestment story now ..... including you! 😂 Where are the revenues to support this capital investment for double-digit profits?? 🤪 😜 Sure, eventually......but not for a LLLLOOOONNNNGGGG TIME. The CEOs of each of these companies and the billionaires have been consistently selling their stakes in these companies for 2 years now (they can't sell at once!). With the average 401k 40% in these 7 names and an upcoming slower growth trajectory, these innovators know the fate of their stocks. They know the cycle completes - as it ALWAYS does. Sure, the next couple of quarters can see earnings beats....but those that follow me know the market trades 3 to 9 months ahead of events. So that's baked in now!!!! It is way too much risk at this point.....while these basic Vanguard ETF holders are so smug in a perceived safety. You WILL LIKELY GET CRUSHED if you are in these Mag 7 weighted investment vehicles.

(4) Private markets and IPOs. Now everyone going to be piling in. Are you an accredited investor yet? 😂 Are you going to make bank now ahead of the IPO? Not likely. All new investment types are designed by wolves to find the bagholders. This territory is off limits at this point in the cycle. There are always exceptions. But you need to give yourself an honest assessment of your ability to navigate that. My guess is that 90% of Blossom needs to just say NO! 👎

(5) Start learning about trailing stops. I plan to post a Knowledge Transfer Series post on this soon WHEN IT'S TIME , but starting to learn about it can't only help you be a better investor to protect your portfolio. Of course the masses use them inappropriately - in my opinion - with a basic fixed or arbitrary parameter. It's more differentiated than that.

I want you to win! 🏆 And it gets super tricky now to navigate. Some just leave and say hey, I can check out now and come back in a couple of years. You'll look like a genius when you curcle the date on a 20-year stock chart when you show your kids in 2040. 😂 There's nothing wrong with that either. Buffett effectively did. And we know how history will grade that move. 😉 Precision vs. Timing, right? Then there's others that will want to stay the course to surf the most fantastic waves in history. After all, greed index is only ~50 right now and we need everyone to believe it can't fail before we take a knee, remember? And geez ..... who wants to miss the run up in stock prices of the RIGHT STOCKS!?!?!? 🤑 🤑 🤑 🤑

So start lumping all of this daily news into cycle milestones to understandwhere we are in the cycle and successfullyread the markets. Read the Knowledge Transfer Series in full to learn what all of those are. 👍

Now? I keep buying the RIGHT STOCKS at the RIGHT TIME .......... while also increasingly buying PUTS for the future. Tricky business indeed as we head up to the inflection point. 🎢 📈 🤑 📉 🤮

I always give you my best.

This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️ 🌊

title: Seeking Efficiencies? 🇨🇦
caption:
The Trump administration has signaled plans to revive Venezuelan oil production and direct large volumes to U.S. refiners. Many of these are already designed to process heavy crude similar to Canada’s oil sands blends. A resurgence of Venezuelan barrels into the U.S. Gulf Coast would benefit American refiners while increasing competition for Canadian crude, potentially pressuring prices and export volumes.

Canada’s long-running debate over how to get its oil to market took an unexpected turn this week after British Columbia Premier David Eby argued that Ottawa should prioritize building refineries rather than new export pipelines, reframing a discussion that has dominated Canadian energy politics for years.

Eby’s comments come as U.S. actions in Venezuela add uncertainty to global oil markets and renew attention on Canada’s reliance on the United States as its main crude buyer. Instead of pushing raw oil to tidewater for export, Eby has suggested Canada should invest in domestic refining capacity to capture more value at home and reduce dependence on foreign processors.


Or?..... we just merge and save billions. 😁 🇺🇸 🍁


"Every nation deserves its government."
- Theodore Roosevelt

https://oilprice.com/Energy/Crude-Oil/Why-Canada-Is-Rethinking-Pipelines-and-Rediscovering-Refineries.html

title: Tetra Technologies is Everywhere
caption:
🇦🇷 Argentina, in a surprise development, overtook Colombia to become South America’s fourth-largest oil producer. The country is undergoing a once-in-a-generation unconventional hydrocarbon boom, which began with Buenos Aires nationalizing integrated energy major YPF in 2012. Since then, Argentina’s oil and natural gas output has kept soaring higher, regularly hitting new monthly highs as volumes of shale oil and gas production grow. It is Argentina’s national oil company, YPF, which is at the forefront of the boom with it responsible for this strong production growth. $YPF

For November 2025, Argentina’s crude oil production, despite falling from the October 2025 record of 849,646 barrels per day to 844,386 barrels per day, was still an impressive 12.5% higher than a year earlier. This was the first month out of the last six where output did not rise to a new record high. Rapidly growing shale oil production in the Vaca Muerta shale, Spanish for Dead Cow, is driving Argentina’s stunning production growth. For November 2025, shale oil output hit a new monthly record of 578,461 barrels per day, a 30.68% year over year increase, which saw it responsible for 68.51% Argentina’s total production.

The 8.6-million-acre Vaca Muerta is a massive shale formation, roughly the size of Switzerland, located in the Neuquén Basin in northern Patagonia. It is among the world’s largest unconventional hydrocarbon resources and is frequently compared to the prolific Eagle Ford and Permian shales. The Vaca Muerta is estimated to contain 16 billion barrels of light tight oil and 308 trillion cubic feet of tight gas, making it the world’s fourth-largest unconventional oil and second-largest unconventional gas reserve. According to analysts, characteristics such as superior shale thickness, greater quantities of biological material, higher reservoir pressures, and increased well productivity make the Vaca Muerta superior to major U.S. shale plays.

Since 2011, TETRA Technologies has been involved in the production of unconventional oil and gas in the Vaca Muerta formation located in the Neuquén Basin, Argentina.

Water Management
Our fully automated and integrated water management solution helps meet your increasing water requirements by recycling, treating, and delivering an optimized fluid for frac operations. All while yielding significant cost savings and reducing operational and HSE risks.

Production Testing
TETRA provides all aspects of surface production testing services, from front-end engineering and design to execution of services, from surface well testing to early production facilities. Our equipment is designed and built to meet or exceed the industry standards of API, ASME, ISO, DNV and NACE (other regional requirements available).

Calcium Chloride
TETRA is a global producer of a variety of calcium chloride grades. We produce premium quality calcium chloride products thanks to our proprietary manufacturing process and high-quality raw materials.

https://oilprice.com/Energy/Energy-General/Argentinas-Shale-Boom-Propels-It-Past-Colombia-in-Oil-Output.html

title: Anybody Up for a Game of Clue During the Holidays?
caption:
Markets are climbing into the end of the year! Santa Claus is here! 🎅🏿 🧑‍🎄 🤶 🤑 💸

And those that have been following the Knowledge Transfer Series are positioned ahead of the retail money and can enjoy the daily grind higher in the RIGHT stocks at the RIGHT TIME. 😎 ⛱️ So let's kick back and keep juicing these mutha fukin' markets! 📈

But when the euphoric mania is the story of the day/week, that's the best TIME to put on your detective hat and start seeking out the truth! 🧠🕵️‍♀️ 🕵️‍♂️

Why? Because this is the TIME in the real estate/banking crisis cycle when the Bigs start to seek out Bagholders. 😄 👉 💩 🤢

Yesterday's positioning news from $APO didn't slip by me.

Yes, these are the stories that map out the undercurrents of this fantastic surf. Because these stories forecast events that will come to fruition ......... and take those that surf the market indicies down after the liquidity measures are eventually pulled. IT ALL TAKES TIME. First we need massive liquidity injections and easy credit. 🤑 But then? 🤮

First, private companies.
Then public banks.
Then the markets.

In summary here's the key takeaways:

(1) Apollo Global Management (APO) is moving into an aggressive defensive shell, stockpiling cash and purging high-risk assets as the private credit market faces a “reckoning moment.” On December 22, 2025, CEO Marc Rowan informed investors that his top priority is building “the best possible balance sheet” to ensure the firm is ready to profit when “bad things happen.”

(2) Apollo is currently operating in a state of high alert, with internal executives describing the atmosphere as “waiting on the edge” for the next major market surge or upheaval. This defensive posture is a direct response to Rowan’s belief that long-term interest rates will not fall as fast as the market expects, making high-priced equity and debt increasingly dangerous.

(3) Apollo is aggressively cleaning its books by divesting from sectors that appear vulnerable to technological or financial bubbles. The firm is specifically reducing its exposure to software industry loans, fearing that rapid developments in artificial intelligence could disrupt borrowers’ business models and their ability to repay debt.

(4) Simultaneously, Apollo’s insurance arm, Athene, is drastically scaling back its presence in the collateralized loan obligation (CLO) market. Rowan noted that spreads on these low-rated loan portfolios have become “completely and utterly compressed,” prompting the firm to cut its CLO exposure by half to $200 billion while reallocating tens of billions into safer government bonds.

(5) Beyond market prices, Apollo is sounding the alarm on a brewing crisis in the insurance sector caused by regulatory loopholes. Rowan has been a vocal critic of insurers shifting operations to the Cayman Islands, stating: “What people are doing is shifting operations to the Cayman Islands, where there are fewer rules and lower capital requirements.” He warned that offshore defaults could trigger a “contagion risk” for U.S. insurers who lack a federal backstop.

Key Takeaway:

The bottom line is that Apollo is calling the top. While retail investors and some Wall Street firms are still chasing the AI and “soft landing” rallies, one of the world’s most sophisticated credit investors is retreating to the bunker.

Whew!!!! The momentum is building. The positioning is taking place now. I feel great about my PUT OPTION on $APO and inspired to add to the position over TIME. 📉

While the wolves initiate a game of hot potato 🔥 🥔 ....... we play Clue. 🕵️‍♂️ 🕵️‍♀️

I'm trying to save you. But natural selection rules this universe. We have plenty of TIME in the RIGHT STOCKS but ......... it's also TIME to start scooching over to a seat a little closer to the plane door and check to make sure there's a parachute underneath that seat.

This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️ 🌊

title: Knowledge Transfer #88: Chronological Indifference
caption:
As we blow through the 7,000 followers mark with another +300 since the last Knowledge Transfer Series post, I am well overdue for the next one. So today is the day!

To be fair, this is a TIME of the year that is extra busy enjoying friends and family! And I also have been working on some special Beskar projects. 😉 😂

Thank you to each and everyone of you for continuing on this journey to save your financial soul and fast forward your financial investment acumen. 😀 👍

Now go pour that hot cup of coffee, nestle in to the corner of the couch with a blanket, and get ready for a Knowledge Transfer Series post that you'll want to make sure you bookmark.

Get ready, set, GO! 🔫 🏃‍♂️ 🏃‍♀️

A week ago, we posted a poll on MARKET TIMING! We posed three different entries into a stock. One by Thomas, one by Emma, and one by Olivia. Additionally, we offered an option for I can't tell! 😂 😃

Here is a link to the poll and a recap of the results:

https://link.blossomsocial.com/7uYa/apiccgrv

"Survey says?"

193 participants.
38 (or 20% of you) voted for Thomas
31 (or 16% of you) voted for Emma
117 (or 61% of you) voted for Olivia
7 (of 3% of you) voted for "I can't tell"

Let's break down why first.

Maybe those that voted for Thomas picked it because he sat out a period of consolidation before entering at the same low price point before a climb into a new high? KTS #43/43A? 🤔

And maybe those that voted for Emma saw a break on KTS #23 and entered on a breakout? Or maybe because it was a lower stock price?

And what about Olivia? The far and away most popular choice. Clearly a KTS #47 "The trend is your friend" channel pattern entry into an ever new high generator?

And last. Those 7 that can't tell!?!?! They looked at each chart and felt they didn't have enough information???

C'mon, Beskar....you always string this stuff out! Can't you just get to the point already?!?!?! What's the answer??? 😠 😡

No patience, huh? 😂 Not surprised. 👍

Because THIS is what I would guess that ~90% (this poll suggests 97%) of investors (as defined by KTS #11) simply do not get. Not now. Most not ever.

It is the most disregarded, underutilized, abandoned investment parameter.

TIME. ⏰️ 🕑 🕙 🕚 🕖 🕘

None of those stock charts in the poll had an x-axis. They only showed stock prices and movement to different prices over a price range.

But it was enough for you that you were able to grade their entry. 🤪 😜

And it's this obsession with RETURNS ...... combined with an equal complete and total disregard for RETURNS OVER TIME ..... that kills most INVESTORS. I see it all the TIME in posts on this site. Most report returns only. TOTALLY OBSESSED with price movements.

So Thomas, Emma, and Olivia? Those charts? You had no idea if those are 1-year, 5-year, or 20-year charts, did you? Those could have been daily charts, right? Maybe even hourly?

Makes a difference doesn't it?

So kudos to the 7 of you that didn't have enough information. 👏 I can't either ...... until I add the x-axis of TIME (see below graph attached).

The gig is RETURNS over TIME. Learn this. Ingrain in the membrane. 🧠 Repeat it 50x until it becomes your mojo. 🔮

Especially as we head into a major inflection point in 2026, you need to really step back and start understanding both TIMEly entries AND exits in order to make the next few years as successful as this one. Those that have been following me since I came onto Blossom knew to expect that 2025 would be the year of highest returns in the 2020-2030 decade.

Now more than ever, the RIGHT STOCKS at the RIGHT TIME can outperform year 2025 while most all on this site are set for severe casualty. 😔 😟 😟 🤢 🤮

Stepping back to learn the role of TIME in successful market TIMING is critical now as the real estate/banking crisis cycle cracks are becoming more and more prevalent, the initiation of massive liquidity measures in a futile attempt to thwart the cycle reset are underway, and some sectors have already peaked and are already on a continual downward path ..... for years. 😫

title: ExxonMobil Will OWN the Energy Transition
caption:
$XOM

Any and all energy molecules. As soon as they become profitable, it will be brought to you by ExxonMobil. 🤑

ExxonMobil Unveils Graphite Invention to Extend EV Battery Life by 30%

Exxon Mobil Corp. (NYSE:XOM) has invented a type of graphite that can extend the life of electric-vehicle batteries by as much as 30%, pushing the oil major further into advanced battery materials as automakers look for longer-lasting cells and supply chains less reliant on China.

“We’ve invented a new carbon molecule that will extend the life of the battery by 30%,” Chief Executive Officer Darren Woods, adding that it’s a “revolutionary step change in battery performance.”

According to Woods, several EV manufacturers are currently testing the new invention. The synthetic graphite is designed to be used on the anode side of the battery, with the intention of allowing for faster charging, a longer driving range and a longer lifespan for EV batteries.

The graphite is created from oil refining byproducts like petroleum coke, aiming to reduce reliance on mined graphite (mostly from China). It's part of the company’s larger strategy to enter the battery supply chain, leveraging its chemical expertise for a more sustainable, lower-emission alternative to traditional mining. Exxon recently announced the acquisition of assets from Superior Graphite, with a goal of to scale up manufacturing and commence commercial production by 2029.

The graphite invention is part of the company's new ventures, which include plans to aggressively grow its Low Carbon Solutions (CCS, biofuels, hydrogen), high-value chemicals (Proxxima™), and advanced materials (graphite for EVs). The Oil & Gas giant is doubling down on its strategy to leverage its unique competitive advantages, driving higher profitability and cash flow without increasing capital expenditure, positioning itself for enhanced investor returns while meeting growing global energy demand responsibly.

ExxonMobil's new corporate outlook significantly raises its 2030 targets, aiming for $25 billion in additional earnings and $35 billion in cash flow growth versus 2024, an increase from prior goals, driven by its ongoing transformation, cost discipline, and focus on advantaged assets (Permian, Guyana, LNG). The company expects >17% Return on Capital Employed (ROCE) with no increased capital spending, growing Upstream production to 5.5 million barrels/day (mostly advantaged), and maintains a strong focus on shareholder returns via share buybacks and dividends.

No matter what you buy.....ExxonMobil will be behind it. 🏆

The housing industry isn't going to recover. Too late. 😟

The housing stocks are on a path of Lower High, Lower Lows. 📉 Toll Brothers is a luxury builder that wolves think is insulated from the industry trend because they serve the wealthy that are less impacted or not impacted by interest rates.

Everything else? Margins, backlog, earnings, and most important......their outlook. 🤮

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #87 - Bubble Mechanics
caption:
As we push through the 6,700+ follower mark, these Knowledge Transfer Series posts cannot come fast enough. I have been busy though in preparing the KTS for an official rollout. Today's post is likely to get passed over with the excitement in market moves and anticipation of the rate cut decision this week. They will cut by the way - so you can take the TIME to read this one all the way through!

Today's post is one to bookmark and check back in whenever your stomach is grumbling while watching the market - especially for those that choose not to continue following @beskar_capital . So I give you this overview as a final guide for all (inclding Libs and Conventionalists) to monitor the continuing Winner's Curse stage of the meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle. It is always interesting to me how many more questions come when the market is going down vs. the sparse few when the market explodes. Blossomers going from "I got this!" - almost instantly into - "What are we getting so wrong?" with the whims of the wolves!

So let's get started.

But first.....did you pass the first test? You know..... The Waiting is the Hardest Part (KTS #83)? Those that did will recognize why that was particularly TIMEly if they haven't already. Those Compounding Rights (KTS #66) are in full swing now and going forward will give you constant reminders of your TOTAL WIN in doing so. Those that trimmed? Remember, no regrets looking back ......... because there is ALWAYS (strong word) a new opportunity right in front of you each and every day.

So there's debate about whether there's a bubble forming? Maybe......but if jits are the only ones on one side of the table, it's not really a debate, right? Forget that debate and accept that of course there's a bubble forming. I literally have over 50 securities that I have realized/unrealized gains that have returned triple and quadruple digit returns in months......some in just days. THIS IS NOT A NORMAL STOCK MARKET!!! But, that doesn't mean that the best move is to just flee from it now ahead of the collapse.

Why not, Beskar? Great question. Because of TIME. Bubbles (which is what the meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle is) have distinct milestones along the way before the spontaneous collapse. And in order for collapse to occur, the bubble must pass each milestone along the way to ......... popping!

So let's look at the mechanics of how a bubble forms. At first, tiny gas particles form within a liquid and start to aggregate and form a larger core (the beginning investment of a thesis in a secular trend). This is called nucleation. Next, gas outside the liquid barrier starts to diffuse into the nucleus of tiny bubbles, which is now forming as a single, unified bubble that continues to grow in size. Think of this additional gas diffusing from outside the bubble across the bubble barrier and into the nucleated bubble as liquidity (easy money flows into the market). Naturally, the bubble gets bigger! Last, it reaches the stage where the Stability of the bubble becomes dependent on a few driving factors like surface tension, temperature, impurities, and surfactants that regulate whether the bubble will reach a point of stabilization ......... or collapse. Think of this as government intervention through stimulus, regulation, and intervention.

Come on ....... that's easy enough to understand, right?

Can you quit with the science, Beskar.......and just get to the point already !?!??!?!

Ok, ok. I'm drawing the parallels for you to research and understand how past real estate/banking crisis cycles go through these stages. I'll go ahead and be more direct in my explanation in a moment. But first, I'll give you the reason of why this must be. The late 1990's internet bubble is just one recent example that is readily relatable as a "Tech" bubble. So let's go there. Companies were increasing their capital expenditures at a furious pace - like today - and STOCKPILING inventory. It was a wide open race with no rules to see who would become the dominant players in the innovation of the internet. Winners like Cisco and Corning KNEW it would all collapse ....... as all bubbles eventually do. But - the business decision made was to outrun the competition so remain relevant when the trend recovers from the collapse. Corning was losing share to JDS Uniphase but had the scale to weather the storm. Following the collapse JDSU went bankrupt and Corning nearly so. Stock traded under $1.00 in 2001. But ........ Corning is the clear winner a decade later with their inventory of fiber optic. You should care about this. Why? Because these companies today.......will POP, crash, and then take decades to recover.

Did you know that $CSCO still hasn't taken out it's previous high ($82.00 per share) in 2000. At $76 today........it only has another 8% before it does. WTF!!! That's 25 years !?!??!?!? Does that Conventionalist Bullshit of "TIME in the market is more important than TIMING the market" settle in with you yet? Think about the parallels today. That's a risk you may not want to take!

Geez......never thought about that, Beskar. That's why we like to step back and look at those long-dated charts to figure out what's really going on!

So let's look at the stages of the bubble in a clear, concise, direct, and actionable way, shall we?

(1) The first is when an exciting new innovation STARTS to see excitement. You know, the tiny bubbles start to form in an independent way. This is clearly the trend in AI. ChatGPT gets early use. Kids start using in school. Workers start probing and see benefits. Companies mention AI in earnings calls to garner interest. Companies see the potential for efficiencies and point out how they are doing what no other companies can do for the trend. EARLY INSIDERS pour in to everything AI and AI related industry while most investors watch from afar.

I think you recognize that this was occurring in late 2023 and into the first half of 2024.

(2) Further investment begins inflating prices. This is where the microbubbles start aggregating into a bigger nucleated bubble. It becomes apparent to even non-market participants that this is a new and exciting technological development that can impact how we do things going forward. Speculation grows into stocks large and small and futurism appears ever more tangible to even the lay person about what's going to happen.

This was the back half of 2024 and into 2025.

(3) Next? Prices continue to rise without regard to rules (like valuation metrics). This euphoric stage just seems like an ever-lasting climb with only healthy ~10% pullbacks. Easy money is available. Easy gains. This is where you literally can make decades worth of returns in less than 2 years. Cracks in the economy begin to show with frequency but don't seem to correlate with the stock market. Governments begin to respond in a futile attempt to stabilize the bubble. They TRY stimulus (checks to citizens to chase ever increasing inflation), regulation (e.g. - tariffs for revenue, relax rules on banks, environment, etc.), and intervention (e.g. - rate cuts, QE, privatization, nationalization, new banking products like 50-year mortgages, etc. to try and "grow" out of the problems brewing).

Sound familiar? It should. Because this is where we are at the moment - the Winner's Curse stage of the meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle. AMAZING returns!

(4) Next, we start to see some profit-taking. EARLY INSIDERS (as defined by KTS #11) are first. But it takes TIME - so much so that it overlaps with adjacent stages of the meltup phase. The ultrarich go first. Then the super rich. Then the rich. But then it becomes a widespread practice that even the common retail investor begins doing it as it. People start thinking they can just do this as a "job" instead, make daily calculations on their retirement date, and/or plan to build a spending fund for college, trips, or senior living.

(5) Then? Out of nowhere......triggered by a banking event.....PANIC. Panic selling. Fear becomes so great that selling becomes fast and furious. You wish you didn't have so many stocks that you have to liquidate because you are literally fighting the clock to sell out. Margin clerks start selling without asking......furthering the pace of decline to that of an elevator drop. The chase to the very bottom is on. Most stocks 50% down. Many stocks 100% down. This is similar to the bubble mechanics when the temperature changes, external pressures are applied to the bubble from impurities and surfactants. The bubble collapses.......as in the market crashes. You wished you got out earlier.

(6) Last? Prices return to the pre-boom phase........and linger in a sour couple of years to complete the real estate/banking crisis cycle........before the next one begins. Valuation metrics become relevant again. We can talk intelligently about P/E ratios and fundamentals having more weight in investment decisions again. People are scared. Investors are burned. They're mad, pissed, angry. Many will be slaughtered. Most will be permanently damaged to some extent.

And - after a couple years - the sun peeks through to start a decade long climb to get back to those prices again - but this TIME - based on appropriate valuations.

So in conclusion, this is the bubble we are in. It's not debatable. No matter what TRUMP does - no matter how noble the effort - the real estate/banking crisis cycle will go through these bubble mechanics. But it while portfolios will suffer, there will be some GREAT news. You just need to survive it to realize it. The bubble popping is a very necessary and WELCOME event. While the casualties will be everywhere.......it must be recognized as a necessary evil to make things affordable again. House prices will become more affordable. Young people can once again realize the dream. Food, insurance, everything becomes more obtainable at reasonable price levels........including stocks! WE WELCOME THE COLLAPSE.

But.........we need to start prepping for survival of this event. Even though, our poll from two days ago shows 55% GREEDY and 45% still on the FEARFUL side of the gauge:

https://www.blossomsocial.com/posts/Market-Sentiment-Poll-for-Blossom-Users-on-Fear-and-Greed-Index__POST-1764431424586-05ezFtE8_qoQV3QbaHcPIAvML

For those that have been following me for awhile, you know that we need just about everyone on the GREEDY side before we worry about the Stability of the Bubble. Not there yet.

And what you don't get in the above post is the length of TIME of each stage. They can be many years llllllooooonnnnnggggg or just a couple of months. This is where most throw up their arms and say "Nobody knows". But think of it as more of a Precision vs. Accuracy instead. Remember KTS #75 A/B/C ? Good TIME to re-read. We know what's coming. And we know when ........ in the context of stepping back to see it all.

So actionable steps at this point in the bubble development that follow the laws of logic are to continue to invest while watching the stage milestones to realize opportunities that present themselves every 20 years. Paying down and off.......all short-term debts like credit interest, car loans, personal loans, bills, etc. And of course, exercise and eating well. Personal health habits are absolutely critical to start practicing in a scheduled routine of some sort.......NOW!

So I share with you with a recipe that will both fortify your gut in market swings and make you feel absolutely amazing every day!!!! - which you will need in order to prep for this environment. Welcome to the big leagues, Blossomers!

Beskar Market Maker : a start-of-the-day, every morning drink (before coffee, of course):

1 grapefruit sqeezed
1 lemon squeezed
1 tablespoon of high phenolic olive oil

Even if you pucker up with lemons and/or don't like the taste of grapefruit, you will find this perfectly digestible (P.S. - I've served to others that were hesitant that agreed it was ACTUALLY good tasting).

I always give you my best. You didn't think you were going to get a healthy recipe today, did you? Well get used to it......because more are coming.

Until then, let's get back to juicing these mutha fuckin' markets!

This is the Way!

title: Why is the IEA ALWAYS LAST to revise outlook?
caption:
Lib-bias.

https://oilprice.com/Energy/Energy-General/A-Price-War-Is-Looming-for-Electric-Vehicles.html

The global electric vehicle sector is facing massive uncertainty. Policy whiplash, the end of a leasing era, and a crowded market are creating a complicated landscape for EV makers, and a price war could be coming down the pike. Times are very tough for EV makers, but may be shaping up to be better than ever for EV buyers.

A record number of EV leases will be returned in the United States in 2026, causing a surge in used EV supplies and therefore making the used vehicles more affordable than ever before. At least 243,000 EV leases are set to expire, and a recent study from J.D. Power projects that this figure could reach 330,000, representing a 3-fold increase over 2025 levels.

Accordingly, Auto Blog reports that an “EV Avalanche” is in store, and with incentives expiring and inventories rising across used car lots, a price war is likely to make the vehicles even cheaper than gas cars. But will consumers embrace the newly affordable technology? Experts aren’t so sure.

EV policy is undergoing a massive shift in the United States as the Trump administration pulls the plug on industry incentives. The $7,500 federal tax credit for electric vehicles expired on September 30 of this year. “With EV tax credits expiring, and automakers continuing to prioritize hybrid powertrains, drivers may choose to ditch electrification,” says Auto Blog. “Though EV sentiment remains high, the electrification bubble is showing some stress.” Currently, EVs represent only six percent of new vehicle sales, down from 12.9 percent in September, when sales surged as consumers rushed to buy before tax credits ended.

As a result, automakers including Ford, Kia, and General Motors are cutting back on their EV manufacturing for U.S. markets. “They're explicitly delaying when they're going to bring out new models, or in some cases, pulling back models,” Elaine Buckberg, senior fellow at Harvard's Salata Institute for Climate and Sustainability and former chief economist at General Motors, recently told Marketplace. “They're just not moving as ambitiously into the market as they had been planning to six months ago.”

“They're looking at this landscape where they're facing multibillion dollar tariff hits, so they really need to think about their profitability,” Buckberg went on to say.

Those tariffs are causing turmoil on the other end of the trade route as well. When the largest economy in the world is facing market uncertainty, global markets feel the impact. China’s Xiaomi corporation, which produces EVs and smartphones, among other technologies, has seen a remarkable crash in recent months, going from being the market darling to the nation’s worst-performing tech stock seemingly overnight.

Meanwhile, Beijing is fighting hard to pressure Western powers to reverse their 100% tariffs on Chinese EVs by targeting farmers in Canada, Europe, and the United States. “Beijing has levied billions of dollars of agricultural sanctions against the U.S., the European Union, and Canada, after they imposed duties on Chinese EVs to protect their own automakers,” says a Rest of World report from this week. “China has said it will lift farm tariffs only when Western governments drop EV duties.”

Despite extreme volatility in the global EV sector and a “grim” outlook going forward in the United States, there is still hope for the future of EVs worldwide. EV sales are still going strong in many regions around the world, and will continue to pick up as EVs continue to get cheaper – brutal price wars within the Chinese market ensure that prices in markets without tariffs will remain low. The International Energy Agency’s Global EV Outlook 2025 projects that “despite uncertainties in the outlook, the share of electric cars in overall car sales is set to exceed 40% in 2030 under today’s policy settings."

The green scam unwind continues. And if you can see it, you can profit off of it. 🤑

This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️ 🌊

title: Green Scam is Ending Right Before Your Eyes
caption:
Peak oil consumption.

All along, Darren Woods - CEO of the greatest company in the world $XOM ExxonMobil, and the company that will OWN any energy transition of energy molecule consumption - told us.......oil will continue to grow through 2050 and beyond. Simple math. The growth comes as poverty-stricken 3rd world populous countries are climbing the standard of living ladder. 🪜 😊

It was supposed to be 2019. Then 2022 following a COVID rebound. Pushed back to 2023. Then 2024.

2025 will be another record.

And now? The world's energy forecasting agencies just folded their cards. 🤑

IEA Drops Peak Oil Production. 🛢 📈

The International Energy Agency has dropped its predictions that oil demand growth will peak in a matter of a few years. In the latest edition of its World Energy Outlook, the IEA said oil and gas demand could continue growing until 2050.

The IEA said that the coming years and decades will see a consistent increase in demand for energy across industry, households and, notably, information technology. Investments in data centers this year could reach $580 billion this year, the IEA’s secretary-general said, which exceeds the expected $540 billion in oil and gas industry investment.

As demand for energy grows, so will demand for the traditional sources of that energy. In a departure from its predictions of peak oil demand and peak natural gas demand before 2030, the IEA now expects oil demand to reach 113 million barrels by 2050, under the stated policies scenario that the outlet reintroduced this year after dropping it for five years to focus on aspirational scenarios focused on net zero.

The IEA also emphasized the return of energy security as priority number one for many governments, replacing climate change and emission reduction. “With energy security front and centre for many governments, their responses need to consider the synergies and trade-offs that can arise with other policy goals – on affordability, access, competitiveness and climate change,” Fatih Birol said.

Even with oil and gas demand on the rise over the long term, the IEA hailed what it called the Age of Electricity, estimating that electricity demand will grow much faster than overall energy use under all of the scenarios the IEA included in its forecasting. It then went on to predict that energy generation capacity will be growing in response to the demand surge and that solar PV will be the fastest-growing form of generation capacity. Nuclear, however, will also accelerate, with capacity growing by as much as a third by 2035.

https://oilprice.com/Latest-Energy-News/World-News/IEA-Drops-Peak-Oil-Predictions.html

This is an announcement of an inflection point. KTS #43/A.

These companies are cannibalizing themselves with share buybacks, slashing leverage ratios, increasing efficiencies, increasing dividends, massive growth in free cash flows, and now ....... the financial world is accepting increased demand.

The Green Scam is over. Stocks tied to it can still perform as they are growing too. But when you are such a small sliver of the pie supply, the easy bet is on traditional energy molecules.

Know the purpose of this post, Libs. 🤑 🤑 🤑

It's TIME to get really, really rich. 💰 💶 💸

title: Beskar Unmasked!
caption:
While we wait for the next Knowledge Transfer Series #85 tomorrow morning 👍, I posted a poll on a frequent questioning I get in DM along the lines of who are you exactly? 😂 🤣

https://link.blossomsocial.com/7uYa/lg3tdk55

So VERY interested in anyone that selected the right answer of which statement IS NOT TRUE about Beskar! So after this post, I would greatly appreciate your honesty in identifying yourself. 🏆

First up was 3-term elected official for 12 years?

Survey says? .......This is TRUE. 13% of you thought this wasn't true. Yes, as a proponent of public school education and with five children in a district that failed their first levy in history at the TIME our #4 and #5 were born, I got involved and ran for our local school board, which I served on for 12 years. 👍

Next.....Founded/grew 7 businesses?

Only 6% of you doubted Beskar did this one. This too is TRUE. 👍 6 profitable businesses and 1 non-profit business were founded and /or grown from scratch. Very proud of the success in these. All 6 profitable businesses continue today. The non-profit was a community youth sports business that enabled economically-disadvantaged youth to participate in competitve club sports. It was eventually transferred into a larger regional/national youth sports club to enrich student athletes to realize their full potential.

Next up? ....Practicing Civil/Environmental Engineer with Masters degree and published research in peer-reviewed journals?

Only 4% of you thought this one was not true. But it is TRUE! Since 1995, I have been working in groundwater modeling for contamination clean up, water resource exploration and protection, and asset management/GIS in several projects with utility companies, the EPA, governments, and commercial clients. I continue consulting today in both engineering and education industries. Undergrad in Civil engineering. Masters in Environmental engineering. I have a few papers in scientific journals of which I am an author or co-author. 👍

Next? Completed coursework certificates in both Business Analytics and Data Analytics from Cornell University? This also is TRUE! Only 5% thought this one wasn't true. Yes, you got it....continuing education is important to me to continously improve everything I do. I LOVE LEARNING!!!!! I have always been known for being a fantastic listener and learning from others with an open mind. 👍 So when you see what appears as an overconfident - even arrogant post or statement from me 😳 - just know that I'm knocking back an equally arrogant/overconfident statement made by a jit headed in the wrong direction! 😂 A shock and awe is sometimes needed to get someone back on track. A spanking of sorts. 😂 When it's something I don't understand, I ask questions. I'm old though and have learned a lot. And very opinionated - like YOU. The only difference is that I just share mine with others! 😂 Still NOT a Boomer!!! 🤪 😜 Anyway, I am.....and will always be......engaged in continuing education. Strong recommendation to all to embrace that mindset as well to realize an amazing life! 😍

So that brings us to the final 2. Did Beskar vote for a liberal for President of the 🇺🇸? Or did he drop out of business school at UPenn?

63% of you thought NO WAY Beskar voted for a liberal for US President. Only 9% thought Beskar wasn't a dropout.

Survey says? ........... Beskar DID NOT drop out of anything! No way, no how! 😂 🤣 I am a particularly motivated and competitive soul that goes until we 🏆. I think most of you have seen plenty of examples of that. 😂 In fact, dropping out is a one way phenomenon..... that would go from Engineering into Business. NOBODY drops out of Business into Engineering. 😂 🤣 After all, that's the degree that Conventionalist wolves obtain. 😂 NOT saying everyone with a Business degree is a Conventionalist stupid wolf. In fact, so many of my heros that have taught me so much are Business and Finance gurus with AMAZING knowledge and insight - and our children are just killing it with their Business education. So the respect is earned by the individual. A lot of dummies in that industry though!

So does that mean they are ALL TRUE????

No. I did actually vote for a liberal for US President. As a fierce proponent for a multiparty system, I voted for Ross Perot (NOT a liberal - he would have been great!) in 1996 and later in 2000 for Ralph Nader. Ralph Nader was a liberal political activist who focused on consumer protections, environmentalism, social justice, and government reforms. He was awesome!!!

So I was blamed by Republicans for electing Clinton over Dole in1996. And then four years later, I was blamed by Democrats for electing Bush over Gore. 🤪 😜

When all along, it was just UNConventional. 💪

I thought @late.start 's detective work was some solid sleuthing in the comments of the poll post. Impressive!

We are all shaped by our life experiences. ❤️ And my path is now here to help others 🏆.

This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️ 🌊

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #84C- The Observer cont.
caption:
Next up, Speculative Investors.

Running up total bullshit stocks.

For example, Rigetti Computing, $RGTI . Man does this stock drive Conventionalists nuts!?!?? Sorry Convens……your valuation metrics don’t mean shit right now. 😂 Yes, yes, you are 100% correct, they eventually will, but not now!

Rigetti has a market cap of just over $15 billion. Yet the company’s projected fundamentals over the next year? Revenue: $21.5 million. Net Income: -$51.4 Million. Free Cash Flow: -$59.8 Million.

Can I say WTF!?!?!? Good….because WTF. 😫 Stocks like these are set to slaughter your fellow Blossomer when it’s TIME - which is not yet - so hard to press on those that are riding this wave successfully. But beginning investors? If you don’t develop these navigation skills quick……..this is not a type of stock to put in your portfolio to hold. It will WIPE YOU OUT. And there are many, many more examples.

So we understand that some stocks are going to come crashing to the ground. It won’t surprise me one bit to see this $46 Rigetti Computing stock fall all the way into penny range (<$5.00) within the next 2 years.

Counter this with speculation into commodity stocks. Some of these penny miners are returning quadruple-digit returns on derivatives now over the past year……triple-digit returns on the underlying stocks.

Are these in the same camp as Rigetti?

Great question! Some are. Some aren’t. So next TIME you see my bros @osmel86 post an article on yet another penny miner, you need to start making an assessment on it’s future profitability. Sure, you can pick a few and call them lotto tickets and plan to deal with the consequence if it doesn’t work out……but at this point……if you are going into this space……that any significant amount of capital you are plowing in is finding a home in a company that is already producing or soon to realize profits. All miners are selling their story now. But if you aren’t pulling the metals out of the ground, you aren’t benefitting from the rising price action in the metals themselves. Just a caution…..because those without profit…..will come to market and issue more shares as their market cap climbs from all of the speculation. THE HANGOVER ON THAT PARTY WILL BE BEYOND WICKED!

So the takeaway, miners are fantastic right now. But let’s make sure they are going to turn a profit before this economic cycle peaks if we are going to put significant (as defined by YOU) money into them.

Speculative Investors will rule the day and continue to rule for someTIME. Just know the relative risks when you totally abandon fundamentals (KTS#17).

Last for now, the Consumer.

Us. Nobody watches the economy better. We are the consumers. We are the fuel that runs it all. So I recommend at this point in the Winners Curse stage of the meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle, that YOU start observing your fellow consumers to establish a baseline that we will measure against later on as the cycle is peaking.

Two games. First the Grocery Game.

Start peeking in your fellow consumer’s grocery cart as you roll past. Look at the shelves. Find the discounted mystery meat section. How are the stock levels on the offbrands? Are they cleared out while the name brand equivalent is at full stocking levels? Are the endcaps loaded with name brands practically giving away all of their profits? You know those specials…..4 bags of Frito Lay for $8.00 ?

Bottom line. Establish the base level now. Watch for changes each week. Grade it. About the same? More off brand? Less off brand? Record it. Mentally is fine. But record it. Always measure against what you observe today.

The second game is a fun one too. The Gasoline Game! ⛽️

As you know TRUMP only gets credit for rising energy prices……not falling ones, right Libs?

But prices at the pump are really low right now. Play this game everyTIME you go to the pump. While pumping, take a walk around to the other pumps (not occupie) and give a glance on the final bill of the previous pump. Run a tally system on the number of final purchases that end on a dead even number like $10.00 or $15.00. Also tally the ones that end on a totally random number too. You know like $46.71 ? $23.32?. So 8 pumps you checked. 2 of them were on those nice, fixed numbers….like $15.00, $25.00, etc. The other 6? Whatever the pump stopped on. So tally it up 2 out of 8, of 25%. These people are on a budget. Let’s observe the observers and see how this proportion changes over the coming year.

It's not work!!!! It’s actually FUN!!!! 😁

I will save the second half of observers that we need to observe for another post as we continue onboarding to the Knowledge Transfer Series.

The Winners Curse phase is where everyone realizes what’s going to happen…..yet it is their human instinct to try and stop it someone. This is where the massive liquidity enters the system to try and re-stimulate – which it will do – but in a way that makes the collapse even greater. Rate cuts from the Federal Reserve. Tax cuts from the US Government. Momentum chase by Boomers money market withdrawls. Inflation checks from the Governors. The money will be coming from every orifice of the government to throw gasoline on this fire……..higher! Learn to observe it. 👀

But the tool here that you need to develop…….Observing the observers. It’s key to successfully identifying the point within the economic cycle to TIME markets to consistently realize absolutely amazing double-digit returns over TIME. And now…….despite the cracks beginning to appear…..that are even recognized by the average investor ….. it’s TIME to full bore juice these mutha fukin’ markets even more! 🤑 🤑 🤑 🤑 🤑

I always give you my best! 👍

This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️ 🌊

P.S. – If you want to develop your instincts for monitoring the crosscurrents and observing the observers/economy further, I strongly recommend going back and reading – in this order – KTS #38, #39, #40, #44, #71, and #71A.

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #84B- The Observer cont.
caption:
*** This post has an A/B/C. Read all 3! 😊 ***


Next up, Andrew Ross Sorkin

You know him. He is the brilliant (albeit Lib 😂), open-minded, steadfast CNBC host since 2011 that nearly every week day is reporting on the economy, analyzing markets and conducting interviews of CEOs and key past/present economic decision makers. 👍

During this past week, he surprised 😱 his co-hosts with a new book he authored and released: '1929: Inside the Greatest Crash in Wall Street History--and How It Shattered a Nation'

What TIMING eh? 😂 He disclosed that he had been working on it on his vacations and days off for the past 8 years ?!?!? 🤪 😜

And he released it this week. It’s almost as if he knows something about the real estate/banking crisis cycle. 🤔 But alas, he states the obvious, ““We will have a crash; I just can’t tell you when, and I can’t tell you how deep.”

https://www.msn.com/en-us/money/markets/we-will-have-a-crash-why-andrew-ross-sorkin-thinks-this-market-bubble-will-eventually-pop/ar-AA1OkDgj?ocid=BingNewsSerp

Let’s just remember TRUMP and his kids aren’t the only opportunists at this TIME! 😂 🤣

Next up, Jamie Dimon. 😫

How can we forget? The man who wants to be known for the biggest bank (currently here in 2025 building the JPM/Chase HQ building to be within the top 25 in the world 😉) and the all-knowing of everything said some things during JPM’s earnings call about private credit.

What happened? A subprime auto lender and dealer went bust last month in a crash fueled by plenty of risky loans and, allegedly, “pervasive fraud” of “extraordinary proportion,” a lawyer said in court. 🤮

First Brands, a privately owned auto-parts supplier, filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy. 📉 First Brands turned to the private credit market for help acquiring competitors. In a recent court filing, newly appointed directors of First Brands revealed as much as $2.3 billion in unpaid loans. Creditors now allege that First Brands essentially used the same invoice multiple times to access funds from private lenders unaware of the double dipping.

If you recall from my earlier posts about private credit, companies that were on the rocks started going to private credit when the interest rates were climbing after COVID. Private credit companies, in turn, would then go to the big banks and secure lower rate loans….and then pass that money on to the companies in trouble. So essentially, the private credit companies (you know who they are!) served as a middle man to give unworthy companies access to lower rate loans.

And now? Private credit has reached all-TIME highest levels.

Can you say YIKES?!?!?!? 😱

So what exactly did Jamie Dimon say about this situation? 🤔

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #84A - The Observer
caption:
As we cross over 6,200 followers, I owe you another Knowledge Transfer Series post!

And what TIMING? A huge runup in the markets into mid-October with some pause this past week in recent headline news. 📈 Nonetheless, those that have been following @beskarcapital know that we will see calendar year 2025 record the highest returns of any year in the 2020-2030 decade, per script. That includes December 31st (and beyond into 2026 ). 🤑 💸 So if you haven’t by now…… you may want to consider to 🎶 🎵 Get Your Motor Running! 🎶 🎵

Remember, we need every average retail investor man, woman, and child to believe that markets are launching into a new bull market that will last for many years. How wrong they will be. 😔 How devastating it will be. 🤮 😡 🤬 But the takeaway here is that these markets are going up, up, and up until full acceptance by all …… that this cannot and will not end anyTIME soon. 🤑 🤑 Then we deploy the parachute.

So today’s post is to teach you to start taking the steps …… to become an observer ……… of the observers of the economy.

First, before we begin this post, I need you – I REALLY NEED YOU to read, or re-read for those long-TIME followers, KTS #22 Charts Map the Future from May 5th, 2024. If you are going to be any good at this….you’re going to have to work away at it……and ingrain in the membrane key learnings that repeat over TIME. So in typical Beskar fashion, we step back…..and observe the past……to learn the future.

Did you read KTS #22 yet?

https://link.blossomsocial.com/7uYa/zo6qthox

Seriously……..I’m not going any further until you do. GO NOW AND READ IT!

Ok…thanks to those of you that did. Those that didn’t? No bitchin’ later on. 😂 🤣 Now, let’s develop our ‘observation from above’ abilities, step back, and look at some of the key characters in the play. Afterall, they are the ones that study the script and perform for us! 😄

First up, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell:

First, for beginning investors, we don’t normally key in on Fed speak. Instead, we follow the cadence of the real estate/banking crisis cycle. ⏰️ But during transitions of stages within the phases of the economic cycle, we like to check in with macroeconomic events to verify. So let’s go!

No rate decision meeting this week, but some key commentary was made this past Tuesday, October 14th amid the euphoria of the markets.

So what did he say?

For beginning investors, don’t feel intimidated. Learn this. The Fed operates for just two reasons: employment and price stability goals. They take action to keep employment as high as possible. They also take action to keep inflation as low as possible.

Easy enough? Again - Employment high. Inflation low.

So let’s pull out some gems 💎 from his Tuesday speech that help us see – how this key observer of the economy – sees current economic conditions and monetary policy outlook.

(1) “Some signs have begun to emerge that liquidity conditions are gradually tightening”

(2) Available data shows “that growth in economic activity may be on a somewhat firmer trajectory than expected.”

(3) “While the unemployment rate remained low through August, payroll gains have slowed sharply, likely in part due to a decline in labor force growth due to lower immigration and labor force participation. In this less dynamic and somewhat softer labor market, the downside risks to employment appear to have risen.”

(4) “Near-term inflation expectations have generally increased this year, while most longer-term expectation measures remain aligned with our 2 percent goal.”

Conclusion:

Jerome Powell THINKS Employment Down, Inflation (after tariff one-time increase effect) Down.

“Rising downside risks to employment have shifted our assessment of the balance of risks. As a result, we judged it appropriate to take another step toward a more neutral policy stance at our September meeting.”

So what’s next? He just told us that looser monetary policy is coming. 💰 💲 💶 💶 💸 🤑

That means lower interest rates……which means more money in the hands of consumers and companies……which is the FUEL for the markets to climb ever higher. ⛽️ 🔥 🤑

Check out our KTS# 68 FRED chart attached. What happens whenever the growth slope in the Payrolls levels off?

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #83 – The Waiting
caption:
Oh baby, don't it feel like heaven right now?
Don't it feel like something from a dream?
Yeah, I've never known nothing quite like this
Don't it feel like tonight might never be again?
Baby, we know better than to try and pretend

I said yeah, yeah (yeah, yeah)
