The waiting is the hardest part
Every day you see one more card
You take it on faith, you take it to the heart
The waiting is the hardest part
 
Ahhh.  Tom Petty.  A true rock n’ roll icon.  Only the good die young!
 
We just blew through the 5,700-follower threshold so it’s TIME for another Knowledge Transfer Series (KTS) post.  For those new to the KTS, I recommend starting with my first post in March 2024, “They are telling us so what are you waiting for?” and then move into the Knowledge Transfer Series posts #1 thru #83 and counting!  It will fast forward your financial life and give you a toolset that will work in any market to successfully TIME markets within the context of the real estate/banking crisis cycle!

So let’s jump in.  This market is moving just as we thought.  The first half of the year choppy while the wolves tried to figure out the winners and losers.  Then, the second half of the year climbing to give us the calendar year of highest returns in the 2020-2030 decade. Check!
 
So why are so many exiting now? 

This is the beginning of the Winners Curse.  Sure the undercurrents are dangerous.  But the waves?  Kowabunga Dude!  And we are just starting to see the M2 Boomer money market and CD funds come pouring in to chase what we already own!!!  And don’t forget the biggest tax cuts in history on the menu.  And I know you didn’t forget about more rate cuts! 

No…..this is not the TIME to grab and release a parachute – the plane is taking off with an elevation of 20 feet above the runway!!!   I know, I know.  You have triple digit returns…….so it must be TIME to sell with a smug look on your face, right?  WRONG ANSWER!

Why?  We just listed 3 sources of MASSIVE LIQUIDITY that is on tap to flood this market and BUY THE STOCKS THAT YOU ALREADY OWN (if…..big IF…..you are in the RIGHT STOCKS at the RIGHT TIME!).  No, this market is full bore until every man, woman, and child has bought in to the euphoria.  We need you and everyone else talking stocks at your mechanic, your hairdresser, and your department store.  Until then?  It’s pedal to the metal, baby!!!!  That’s right, if the little old lady next door isn’t telling you about Nvidia $NVDA yet, then we are enjoying the ride!

I want to see a book titled DOW 80,000, or even 100,000!  I want to hear how this TIME is different.  And that AI investment and productivity means that we can’t learn from history anymore because this revolution is so completely different.  No more bubble comparisons to the late ‘90s.  No more crisis comparisons like 2008.  Throw in the towel on the roaring 1920’s.  I want to hear there is just no historical comparison to what this market is.

Ok, Beskar.  I’ve heard this before.  So what are YOU doing now?????

Great question.  I’m doing what must always be done in the Winners Curse stage of the meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle.

And that’s ………… WAIT!

It’s the hardest part.  Everyday you look at your portfolio now.  Amazed with the results.  Thinking about how this can change your everything.  And it can.  And if you play it right…….it will.
These miners and select commodities have been asleep for over a decade and are just waking up. 

So where are you going !??!?!?!?!

Sure….you can use the popular Beskar Capital technique by selling out your original capital in each investment…..effectively guaranteeing that you CANNOT lose.  But that’s not my gameplan…..yet.
Instead, you need to think really, really, really, really hard about hitting SELL buttons.  Ever type a nasty draft message and stare at the SEND button knowing you’re out of bounds?????  That’s the feeling you will should have!

And why?  Because these meltup phases in the real estate/banking crisis cycle occur only 4x in the average human investment lifespan.  You’ve waited this long……..so why leave now!??!?!?

Let’s look for guidance from a respected trader:

“After spending many years in Wall Street and after making and losing millions of dollars I want to tell you this: It never was my thinking that made the big money for me. It always was my sitting. Got that? My sitting tight!”

-Jesse Lauriston Livermore
 
…..and how about words of wisdom from a respected investor?
 
“The big money is not made by buying or selling, but in the waiting.”
-Charlie Munger
 
Let’s pull up an example.  See the two charts attached below. 
The first is Northern Dynasty, the largest copper reserve in the world.
From 1997 to 2003, Northern Dynasty stock traded in a range from 23 cents to 93 cents.

It took 6 years to break $1.00 !?!??!

It then took 2 months to move from $1.00 to $2.00.

2 more months to get to $3.00

2 more months to cross $4.00

1 more month to $5.00

2 more weeks to $6.00

.....then 3 more years to $14.00

Do you see a similar pattern in any of the stocks that you own? (#MELTUP)

Had you bought $NAK at 50 cents somewere in say 2006…….and exited when it doubled……you would declare a total win!  And you should!  Afterall, that’s 100% return!?!??!  2x bagger!
That means a $3,000 investment at 50 cents (6,000 shares) would have turned into $6,000 in just ~3 years would kill any Conventionalist returns at an average ~33% return per year!

But what if you stuffed your hands under Ole Charlie’s fat ass instead? - and waited until the real estate/banking crisis cycle peaked?

Those 6,000 shares would be worth $14.00 a piece.  That’s $84,000!?!?  …… or 2,700%.  For you liberals out there……2,700% is very different than 100%.  You would knock out a college education for your child……..by NOT buying and selling…..but just WAITING!

Big difference, eh?

TIME to re-read KTS #66, Compounding Rights.  Here it is again:
https://www.blossomsocial.com/posts/Knowledge-Transfer-Series-%2366---Compounding-Rights__POST-1741260754838-9ywNnVvr_qoQV3QbaHcPIAvML

So think carefully before you give up your compounding rights by hitting that sell button!  Because the euphoric stage can convert gains that can buy you a car ………. Into gains that can buy you a college education……….in virtually NO TIME!

I included a chart of $TGB Taseko Mines to give you a modern example.  For Taseko, it took 12 YEARS  TO CONVINCINGLY BREAK $2.00 per share.  And the last 4 MONTHS TO CONVINCINGLY BREAK $4.00 per share. 

Still……if you think $NAK isn’t setting up for a repeat performance, you’re dead wrong.

So don’t be afraid to step back and see what’s really going on by looking at the 20-year chart (KTS #15).  This is a TIME to enjoy the surf and - Ride with it, ride with it, see where this thing goes!

Can you learn from someone older than you that has experience and knowledge that you don’t yet?  I did.  I learned from the best.  And we will roll those names out when it’s TIME.  But for now?  You need to answer this question for yourself. 

Start by showing your respect and listening to an all-TIME great all the way through!

title: Liberal Policies Create Investment Opportunities
caption:
The Net Zero Consensus Is Breaking Down in Europe

In June 2019, Prime Minister Theresa May passed a law – with near unanimous parliamentary backing and pretty much no debate – committing the UK to achieve Net Zero by 2050.

And where has it taken us?

The UK has the highest cost of energy in the developed world – for households and businesses – and we’ve been putting wind farms faster than our energy infrastructure can handle them – leading to a situation where we – taxpayers, bill payers – will pay £1bn this year to wind farm operators to turn off those wind farms when it’s too windy.

High energy costs are forcing chemical, cement and manufacturing plants to close. Energy investment and associated manufacturing are heading for the US. The North Sea is viewed as an awkward relic rather than a natural asset and the combination of green levies, subsidies and taxes are making a mess of the entire system.

She said that the Conservatives would make the North Sea the “cornerstone” of UK energy policy by ending a ban on new oil and gas exploration, and that as Prime Minister her priorities would be “growth, cheaper energy” and a renewed commitment to protecting the environment.

Meanwhile, our energy demands are going to skyrocket – not least given all the hungry new data centres unveiled in recent weeks.

Policies change. They evolve. They adapt to meet circumstances. They encounter problems and breakthroughs at unexpected moments. They do not always conform with political aspirations and timelines and badly-written law, and it seems that the penny is beginning to drop.

https://oilprice.com/Energy/Energy-General/The-Net-Zero-Consensus-Is-Breaking-Down-in-Britain.html

Let's peak into the Liberal HIVE and check in on California...... 😂 🤣

California trying to keep oil and gas firms from leaving the state

Following 25 years of what oil and gas executives categorize as hostility to the industry, the state is now making a play to keep those companies from leaving.

Concerned with the exodus of oil and gas companies, refinery closures and the expensive price of gasoline in the state, California Gov. Gavin Newsom signed legislation last week that fast tracks the approval of 2,000 new wells per year over the next 10 years in Kern County, a significant oil-producing region.

Californians are paying $4.65 for a gallon of regular gasoline while the national average is $3.17 per gallon, according to AAA.

There are 13 refineries currently in operation in the state. When Valero and Phillips 66 shut down theirs, that number will be down to 11. The state had 40 refineries in 1983.

"Millions of Californians will soon start saving billions on their energy costs," Newsom said in a press release. "We’re stabilizing the state’s gasoline supply to avert severe price spikes at the pump and we’re making it easier to build the abundant clean energy we need to keep bills lower."

Even Pro golfer Phil Mickelson is commenting:

"An Important Open Letter to All Californians — Especially Santa Barbara Residents. The reason I became involved with Sable Offshore $SOC is simple: the data shows it’s a true win-win for California — especially our environment."

"Santa Barbara is home to one of the largest natural oil seeps in the world. Every day, over 100 barrels of oil seep uncontrolled through the ocean floor — creating tar on our beaches, sheens on the water, and constant stress on marine life."

"If you believe in cleaner beaches, lower gas prices, local jobs, and reduced dependence on foreign oil, now is the time to act. A short email, tweet, or phone call can make the difference. Let our leaders know that restarting Sable is the right thing for California."

It's a complete unwind of the Liberal Green Scam. So are you watching? Listening? Learning? Do you know how to successfully TIME this unbelievable reversal in policy?

title: Wall Street Rediscovers Oil & Gas! ⛽️ 🤑
caption:
Encouraged by the U-turn in the U.S. energy policies under President Trump, investors are back to backing oil and gas majors as the self-righteous ESG hype is waning, especially in corporate America.

For all the environmental, social, and governance craze of the past few years in markets, ESG has done value investors one big favor—it has helped sink the valuations of oil and gas companies to bargain levels, alongside the oil price crash of 2020, of course.

These super-low valuations of Big Oil and other oil and gas firms have encouraged bargain hunters and contrarian investors to continue betting on fossil fuels amid a major market and ideological drive against these.

The Big Pivot

For example, for most of 2020 and 2021, ExxonMobil, the biggest U.S. oil firm by valuation, saw its share price dive to less than the per-share value of all its assets. The price to tangible book value per share slumped to below 1, compared to about 3 in the previous two decades, according to data compiled by Bloomberg and cited by Bloomberg Opinion columnist Javier Blas.

However, the Russian invasion of Ukraine, the subsequent spike in energy prices, and the energy crisis were the beginning of the end for ESG and for super-bargain valuations.

The specter of $100 a barrel oil for longer in case of supply shortages helped the narrative of the U.S. supermajors. Exxon and Chevron never backed down from their position that the world will need oil and gas for the foreseeable future and investments in solar and wind aren’t a good fit for a company chasing high returns and payouts to shareholders.


Europe’s Big Oil, especially Shell and BP, had to retract strategies that promised in 2020 a decline in their oil output by the end of the decade, to resets in 2024 and 2025 with renewed focus on the upstream and pledges to boost oil and gas production to meet the world’s growing energy needs.

BP and Shell also scaled back, significantly, their pledges to invest in renewable energy sources—they have ditched projects for biofuels plants in the Netherlands and channeled more cash to core oil and gas operations at the expense of investments in renewables. Clean energy solutions, including offshore wind and green hydrogen, have stumbled upon high interest rates and supply chain issues in recent years to make projects much more expensive and less lucrative. That was a deal breaker for Big Oil firms, which hate to lose money when oil prices are down in the cyclical business of energy commodities, and don’t need additional drags on finances with losses in their low-carbon energy divisions.

Europe’s Big Oil found out firsthand that the renewables business isn’t bringing the profits that the core oil and gas business is generating. Faced with the difficult task of rewarding shareholders with attractive yields and payouts and stopping the investor outflow from the industry, and with an energy crisis with soaring oil and gas prices, Shell and BP drastically scaled back their ambitions in renewables and shifted their focus on oil and gas again.

Shell’s CEO Wael Sawan has said that reducing global oil and gas production would be “dangerous and irresponsible” as the world still needs those hydrocarbons.

Equinor of Norway, where electric vehicles hold an enormous market share and power comes from hydro and wind, has also acknowledged that market conditions in the clean energy sector have changed and the energy transition is going forward with an uncertain and uneven pace.

Equinor, which now produces a large part of the gas going to Europe via pipelines, expects to keep a high level of oil and gas production in Norway “all the way to 2035.”

The Big Rebound?

The return to the core business of the European majors and the doubling down on the Permian and global exploration hotspots such as Guyana for Exxon and Chevron have restored some of the market valuations lost in the 2020 carnage. Exxon now trades at 1.8 times the value of its assets, per Bloomberg’s estimates.

BP, which was the center of speculation of a takeover earlier this year, has now seen its shares gain 30% since the early-April low.

At higher – but not too high – valuations, Big Oil still looks like a bargain for investors, especially compared to the other S&P 500 sectors and Big Tech.

The investors that have stuck with the oil industry over the past five turbulent years in markets, geopolitics, and the U-turn of U.S. energy policies now expect the increased valuations to lead to looser financial discipline at some point in the future, Bloomberg’s Blas argues.

However, oil prices below $70 per barrel and expectations of a glut in the oil and LNG markets in the near term are keeping Big Oil anchored to prudent investment policies, with priorities firmly geared toward raising production and returns for investors.

https://oilprice.com/Energy/Crude-Oil/Wall-Street-Rediscovers-Oil-and-Gas.html

Take a few sips before we go further because we are going to need the brain working at full optimization. 😂 Added 300 new followers in just the past week (mostly bots 😂 🤣 🤖) and thus Beskar delivers another absolute 💎! 😉

No more dilly dally, Beskar - let's get after it!

Whale watching. 🐋 🐳 We all love it. We'll even pay for it. This summer we paid $100 a head for a box lunch and whale tour in Port Angeles, Washington .....worth every penny! These beasts of the underworld OWN the oceans. With every move, the cameras click in rapid fire mode to learn as much as we can about them.

Wall Street is no different. We want to learn their every move. THEY really the EARLY INSIDERS (as defined by KTS #11) that stand to make the most. They are the ones that control 78% of the daily trading volume. They are our reason for barnacle to whale (KTS#19). 🐚 to 🐋

Before we go any further, KTS #11, KTS #19, and KTS #20 are mandatory reads and/or re-reads. Mandatory. Here is a link to one of them. Purposely making you search out the other two. A natural selection of sorts that keeps adversaries at bay. 😂 😉

https://link.blossomsocial.com/7uYa/j2kaoi1d

From KTS #20, we learned that these beats must break the water surface so we can see what they're doing 4x per year through the SEC Form 13-HR. We also learned that the legal filing deadline is 6 weeks after the quarter ends. And.....we also learned that the whales try to keep all moves hidden so they can capture lower prices as they build accumulations in the stocks they are interested in. Many institutions accumulate or distribute shares over periods of 8 quarters or more.

Why is this important now????

Because we are in the Winners Curse stage of the meltup phase of the real estate/ banking crisis cycle....period. This stage can last a llllloooooonnnnngggggg TIME. But this is the stage that will wipe most retail investors clean. 😥 😞

Yes, the Winners Curse stage is loaded with all kinds of stocks rising. In other words, both contenders......and pretenders. From recent posts, (KTS #76A, #76B, and later), you know we've been focusing on rising earnings estimates. Those are the contenders. But anything speculative will rise now too. Why? Those Baby Boomers money market and CD rates are dropping in a rate cutting cycle that the Fed is signaling. That means.....their jealousy in seeing the unrealized/realized gains their kids are logging is going to flood into this market and latch like a hookworm onto anything with any kind of momentum pulse. Yikes!

So what's the gameplan, Beskar?

For me, I learned over 25 years ago (about 10 years into my investment "life")....is to ALWAYS......ALWAYS......watch the money flows and WHALE ACTIVITY to successfully TIME markets. It shows up in the ticker tape and they can't hide it. Those that have been Beskarized have proven this to themselves and now know this to be fact.

So what to do now? We are approaching the end of the 3rd quarter, September 30th. That's Tuesday. So we have 2 trading days left. This is a key TIME to observe what's going on. Why? About 10 days prior to the end of every quarter, whales typically make their positioning moves. So those that follow me know.....we watch like an eagle for any surfacing of whales in the 10 days leading into quarters end and then again as soon as the 13-HR is released to the public 6 weeks later.

So last week's markets told us quite a bit. Pretty much a down market (for indicies) all week. But we want to focus on what was going up on above average volume. 🤔 Why? Because that's where the whales are increasing/decreasing their positions. Observe. Analyze. Go back and look at your portfolio last week. Anything rise from the dead? Anything breaking out to new highs? These are whale accumulations. I saw $XLE , $XLRE , and $XLU all up. 🤔 Everything else down. Sector ETFs help us read the market (KTS #2), but we prefer indivual stocks for investment.

So let's get out there and observe and analyze.

Now here's the recommended homework NOW. 😂 This is a market where some stocks are legit.......and some are TIME to quit.

First step, go back and look at the stocks that rose this past week on above average volume. Circle those. If whales are accumulating.....then so am I. They will keep building positions. So will I. 🤑

Nest step, record the % of instituional ownership of each and every stock in your portfolio. ALL brokerage accounts havr this information and update it quarterly. You have about 6 weeks left to run this exercise (remember they don't release until 6 weeks following the end of the quarter on this upcoming Tuesday 👍 ).

Third step, on the Thursday/Friday of the 2nd week in November, repeat the recording of the % of institutional ownership in each of your stocks.

Fourth, do the simple math to see if institutional ownership is increasing, decreasing, or roughly stayong the same. We want to hone in on stocks that are increasing in institutional ownership! 🤑 🤑 And conversely, start scaling OUT of stocks that the whales (instituions) are. Makes sense? Of course it does!!! 😂 🤣

Now this is always a great KTS exercise to perform.....but we just entered the Winners Curse stage of the meltup phase of the real estate / banking crisis cycle. And that means sorting out the retail investors running up stock prices versus instituional investors running up prices. Know which one you want to follow. NEVER FORGET......the Winners Curse can last a llllllooooonnnnnggggg TIME. As the daily news flow continues to highlight the crosscurrents of an economy reaccelerating/slowing, we follow what matters.

For thise that don't want to manually record each stock's institutional ownership, I have an option where you can upload your portfolio tickers and it shows you in a sortable table! 😁 👍 You can open a trial account at Finviz. Have your tickers ready to copy into the Ticker window. When inside, click on the Ownership tab and note the 4 columns, Insider Ownwership, Insider Transactions (updated in realtime as SEC forms are filed), Instituional Ownership, ansd Instituional Ownership Transactions. This last column is the one to focus on on the Winners Curse. 🏆 👏

Also, when Insider Ownership is greater than 30%, Finviz highlights it in green. When the 2 change in transactions fields are greater than 20%, they also highlight those in green. Green is good! 💚 Conversely, When the change in instituional ownership is negative 20% or more, they highlught in red. Red is bad.

So you can see if the insitutional ownership increased in the 2nd quarter.....but remember, that's the past. We always look forward to learn the future while studying the ticker tape to learn FROM the past. So it's the change in ownership come Friday, November 14th that we will anxiously await. So record what's there now. And then again later. 😉

What if yoy see a stock being heavily accumulated by Insiders AND Instituions??? 😂

What if both are heavily selling? Can we learn from all of this? You bet! 😅

I included some screenshots of Finviz with some of Beskar's stocks to show you the interface. I think I see why Beskar's portfolio has been climbing for some TIME now. 😂 🤣 🤑

I always give you my best with real meat to chew on. Now get out there and identify which stocks climbed last week and the upcoming next 2 days as we end the quarter. And record the institutional ownership so we can see the change on November 14th. Whale watching! 🐋 😍

title: A Different Perspective on SMRs.
caption:
You can feel the buzz: nuclear is back. Or so we’re told.

From Brussels to Washington, a new wave of enthusiasm for so-called Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) is sweeping through policy circles, think tanks, and energy startups. These compact, supposedly plug-and-play nuclear units are being hailed as the perfect solution to power data centers, feed artificial intelligence’s growing hunger, and backstop our energy transition with clean, stable electricity.

There’s just one problem. Actually, there are many. None of them small.

The hype cycle is in full spin



SMRs are currently being marketed like they're the iPhone of nuclear energy: smarter, smaller, cheaper, scalable. A miracle solution for everything from remote grids to decarbonizing heavy industry and AI’s server farms. Countries like the U.S., Canada, and the UK have announced ambitious deployment plans. Major developers, including NuScale, Rolls-Royce SMR, GE Hitachi, and TerraPower, have painted glossy timelines with glowing promises.

Except the fine print tells a different story.

There are currently no operational commercial SMRs anywhere in the world. Not one. NuScale, the U.S. frontrunner, recently cancelled its flagship Utah project after costs ballooned to over $9,000 per kilowatt and no investors could be found. Even their CEO admitted no deployment would happen before 2030. Meanwhile, Rolls-Royce’s much-hyped SMR factory hasn’t produced a single bolt of steel yet.

So, we’re betting on a technology that doesn’t yet exist at commercial scale, won’t arrive in meaningful numbers before the 2030s, and would require thousands of units to significantly contribute to global energy demand. That’s not a strategy. That’s science fiction.

Big nuclear hasn’t exactly inspired confidence either

Even the large-scale projects that SMRs claim to “improve upon” are struggling. Take the UK’s Hinkley Point C, once heralded as the future of nuclear energy in Europe. It’s now twice as expensive as originally planned (over £46 billion), at least five years late, and facing ongoing construction delays. The French-backed EPR reactor design it’s based on has already been plagued with similar issues in Flamanville (France) and Olkiluoto (Finland), where completion took over a decade longer than promised and costs ballooned dramatically.

Let’s be honest: if any other energy technology was this unreliable on delivery, we’d laugh it out of the room.

Price floors for nuclear, and price ceilings for reason

In France and Finland, authorities have now agreed to guaranteed minimum prices for new nuclear power, effectively writing blank checks to ensure profitability for operators. In Finland, the recent deal sets the floor above €90/MWh for 20 years. Meanwhile, solar and wind regularly clear wholesale power auctions across Europe at €30–50/MWh, with even lower marginal costs.

Why, exactly, are we locking in decades of higher prices for a supposedly “market-based” energy future? It’s hard to see how this helps consumers, industries, or climate targets. Especially when these same nuclear plants will also require major grid upgrades, just like renewables, because any large-scale generator needs robust transmission capacity. So no efficiency win there either.

The SMR promise: too small, too late

Back to SMRs. Let’s suppose the best-case scenario plays out. A couple of designs clear regulatory approval by 2027–2028, construction starts in the early 2030s, and the first commercial units are online before 2035. Even then, the world would need to build and connect thousands of these small reactors within 10–15 years to displace a meaningful share of fossil generation. That’s a logistics nightmare, and we haven’t even discussed public acceptance, licensing bottlenecks, uranium supply, or waste management.

For perspective: in the time it takes to build a single SMR, solar, wind, and battery storage could be deployed 10 to 20 times over, for less money, with shorter lead times, and with no radioactive legacy.

And unlike nuclear, these technologies are modular today. They’re scalable now. They’ve proven themselves everywhere from the Australian outback to German rooftops and Californian substations.

The elephant in the reactor room: waste and risk

Nuclear fans love to stress how “safe” modern designs are. And yes, statistically speaking, nuclear energy is relatively safe per kilowatt-hour. But it’s also the only energy source with a non-zero risk of catastrophic failure and waste that stays toxic for thousands of years.

Why, exactly, would we take that risk when we have multiple clean energy options with zero risk of explosion and waste streams that are either recyclable or inert?

You don’t need to be a nuclear physicist to ask this: how is betting on high-cost, slow-deploying, risk-bearing, politically toxic infrastructure a better idea than wind, solar, and storage?

A footnote in the transition, not the headline

Let’s be clear: nuclear power will likely continue to play a role in some countries’ energy mixes. France and Sweden have legacy fleets. New projects may go ahead in China or South Korea, where costs are contained and planning is centralized. But for the majority of the world, especially countries trying to decarbonize fast, new nuclear is not the answer.

SMRs, despite their branding, will not save the day. They will be at best a niche, possibly a small contributor in specific applications like remote mines, military bases, or industrial clusters where no other solution works. That’s fine. But let’s stop pretending they’re some kind of energy silver bullet.

Final thoughts

We are in the decisive decade for climate action. Every euro, dollar, and yuan we invest must yield maximum emissions reduction per unit of time and cost. By that standard, SMRs fall flat. Nuclear power, small or large, is simply too expensive, too slow, too risky, and too narrow in its use case to lead the energy transition.

So let’s cool the reactor hype. Let’s focus instead on the technologies that are already winning: wind, solar, batteries, heat pumps, grid flexibility, green hydrogen. These are not dreams. They’re deploying by the gigawatt, today. SMRs are fascinating, yes. But when it comes to decarbonization, we need workhorses, not unicorns.

https://oilprice.com/Energy/Energy-General/Small-Nuclear-Reactors-Will-Not-Save-The-Day.html

Last week, Exxon announced it had invented a new graphite molecule that could extend the life of an electric vehicle battery by as much as 30%. “We’ve invented a new carbon molecule that will extend the life of the battery by 30%,” chief executive Darren Woods said, as quoted by Bloomberg. It’s a “revolutionary step change in battery performance.” Woods added that several electric vehicle manufacturers were testing the new molecule.

The news came just days after Exxon said it had taken over a synthetic graphite maker, Superior Graphite, “to complement our planned entry into the battery anode graphite market.” The company said synthetic graphite is used in both EV batteries and battery storage systems. Exxon added that synthetic graphite was “a game changer” since it was easier to produce than mining natural graphite, which made it cheaper and more consistent in quality, which meant that “we can scale faster and smarter, all while producing a better graphite than what is available in the market today.”

The acquisition and the newly announced invention follow Exxon’s foray into lithium mining in 2023. The supermajor said it had plans to produce lithium in Arkansas ($TTI 🤑) and become a leading EV battery supplier by 2030 two years ago, despite stumbling EV sales in its home market. Exxon is targeting first lithium production for 2027 and is also evaluating growth opportunities globally. By 2030, the company aims to be producing enough lithium to supply the manufacturing needs of more than a million EVs per year.

https://oilprice.com/Energy/Energy-General/Exxon-is-Shaking-Up-the-EV-Battery-Market.html

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #81 - As The World Turns
caption:
G'day, Mate! 🇦🇺

Another 100 followers and another Knowledge Transfer Series post! 🤑

Conventionalists struggle these days with their constant promotion of index ETFs, an aversion to acknowledge that markets can be successfully TIMED, and DRIPPING into ultra dilutive securities........because they too are observing this stock pickers market and are becoming increasingly interested in deviating a portion off their portfolio in an exploratory FOMO way. 😂 🤣

Of course the indicies will continue to climb in the meltup phase of the real estae/banking crisis cycle - as they ALWAYS do. These years are +20% instead of the historical average of +9%. 😂

But, these index funds don't hold a candle to the returns being realized in individual stocks. Even the talking heads are now uttering ho-hum sentences without a beat about select stocks "400% return over the past 3 months" or even "1,300% return over the past year". 💰 🤑

But.......today.......we are going into the Conventionalist world......and study their security of choice - the ETF.

Why? Because focused, yet dilutive securities are what Conventionalists like to buy. ......and......most investors (as defined by KTS #11) are trained to be Conventionalists. So......monitoring changes in these ETFs can bring us incredible insight to inflection points in the cycle playbook! 👀 🤑

Today, we are going to study three key ETFs:

(1) $EWC 🇨🇦
(2) $EWG 🇩🇪
(3) $EWA 🇦🇺

Why? Because these are representative DEVELOPED world countries that help us successfully TIME markets. Not to neglect emerging market countries.....after all the playbook for those is NOW at this stage of the meltup phase of the real estate/babanking crisis cycle. 👍 But with emerging markets, the number of influential factors are so numerous and so potentially impactful as to whether an individual country starts growing.....or starts slowing.

A look back at the history of recessions, you can learn that there is a progression of both POSITIVE AND NEGATIVE growth from the Western world to the Eastern world. While these inflection points among countries can vary from weeks to months to years.......it is extremely informative for taking profitable action.

Check out the history of recessions bar chart below for the three countries from the TIME period of 1960's to 2019. (Hint: maybe check out through 2025). Find the 3 countries and note the red periods (recessions). Been awhile for Australia, eh, mate? 😂 What other insight can we glean? I'll leave that to those that are more curious. 😉

Let's move on. Next, let's pull a Beskar bread and butter move by stepping back and observing these ETFs performance over a 10-year period. Look at the relative performance. What do you see?

Me? I see Canadian stocks break through it's ceiling and retest as a new floor in December 2020. It bumped it's head on the ceiling in late 2017, and again in late 2019........before busting through in late 2020.

Nobody signaled this to you, did they? 😂 🤣 But had you made a pivot? ...... I think you would know how amazing your performance would be today, eh? 🤑 🤑 🤑

Of course Canadian investors missed all of the gains since this breakthrough (and continue to do so! 😂) as we have repeatedly learned from Beskar polls that Canadians prefer investing in California rather than their own home growns. North America generally runs together....but California has been getting tired.....and the stocks that pull the positive performance require more and more selectively. But look at today.....and 🇨🇦 is finding a new trajectory!!! 🤑 🤑

Now, let's turn our focus to Germany. A representative of developed world zone #2. I see German stocks - in a general, Conventionalist cohort 😂 - bump it's head on the ceiling in January 2018....then again in April 2021.......before breaking through and coming back to test as a new floor in February 2025. This relative lag to the Canadian market was a 5-year difference!?!?! But it's pretty clear now to all that European stocks are in a new place. A Beskar favorite, $RYCEY has no signs of quit!!! 🤑 🤑

Last, let's turn our attention to developed world zone #3, Australia. Here, I see the head bumping in February 2021.... again in August 2024......and now? I'm sitting on the edge of my seat to see if the 3rd head bump.....is a break though! 🤑 🤑 🤑

So now you know why @beskar_capital has been starting to build into 🇦🇺. $WDS , $TBN , $IMDXF, on and on....

And let's think about the why this should break through? Those that follow me know.....inflation amid a rate-cutting environment benefits commodity stocks trmendously. You know, gold, silver, oil, gas, etc.

And how about the lag from Canada to Germany to Australia? We know what's coming, don't we? 🔮 😂

And where does most all of the world's drilling and mining occur? In companies from 🇦🇺 and 🇨🇦. 🤔

caption:
We crossed over 5,000 followers so I owe you a new Knowledge Transfer Series post.  I originally had a different topic……but I am pushing that one to the next one because of what I am starting to observe in Blossom investor trades and with 5,000 followers being a significant milestone, I think we need something special.  But good news, we’re only 30 more followers from 5,100 so that G’day, Mate! post is just around the corner.  Ok, let’s get on with it!

The 4 Legs of the Active Investor Stock Picker’s Chair.

So you want to be a stock picker?

Of course you do.  You like to dig in.  You find it kinda fun to “root” for your stocks, don’t you?  You generally like the world of finance to an extent – after all, you’re here frequently spending precious TIME on Blossom, aren’t you?  You’ve figured out that having more of a role in your investments can lead to higher returns……and an easier life, right?  This meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis is yielding some incredible returns to talk about, eh?

Yes, picking stocks just before they go up is exciting.  You are rewarded for being…….right!  They become part of you in a way.  I don’t think I can ever separate certain people on this site whenever I hear a stock ticker.  You know what I mean.  Like @wilonda1999 and $AXON ?  How about @sirdudebroman and $AVGO ?  @sonofrely and $DUOL ?  @beskar_capital and $FTI ?

Each of these stock pickers are tied to their “favorites”, aren’t they?  I just couldn’t imagine ever seeing any of the above stock pickers sell out of these names, can you?????  But what if the general economy suddenly tanks?  Most stocks will drop with it.  But some will start (or continue) to climb higher.  So how do you know which ones will go up…….and which ones will go down? 

Answer?:  The ticker tape.

If you want to be a successful in navigating (TIMING) the stock market, you have to accept this very basic fact:

The stock market isn’t the economy……period.

Instead, the stock market is a measure of where the wolves THINK the economy is in 3 to 9 months.
And stock prices of individual securities?  They too……..are barometers of expected company performance 3 to 9 months in the future.

So some stocks are always going up.  Some stocks are always falling.  And some stocks are always going nowhere.  Did you hear me???  I said ALWAYS!!!

Put simply, stocks are not correlated with the general economy. 

We also learned from KTS #17 (worth a re-read), that, instead, over TIME, individual stocks are correlated with their future earnings within the economy that they operate.  In shorter periods of TIME, irrationality, FOMO hype, momentum markets, and panic trading by wolves can send stocks to levels unheard of………for a SHORT period of TIME.  But then, stability will set in, and the pendulum swing back to valuation calculations become popular again.  But step back and look at stocks over long TIME periods and you’ll recognize that they just move with their earnings.

We also learned that the INSIDERS (as defined by KTS #11 – TIME to re-read) know things about companies that you don’t.  And you won’t…until after the stock has moved on that knowledge – either up or down. 

Seems not fair…..even illegal. But…it happens.  So can we learn where a stock (or any security) is going to go alongside of these INSIDERS (KTS #25 / #50 – worth a re-read)? 

The answer to this question?…… More often than not.

How? We learned that we track the money flows as a stock begins to outperform the index (KTS #25 / #50 – worth a re-read…..NOW).

And the last leg……is TIME.  The most neglected parameter by the Wall Street wolves and your fellow investors…….yet the most important of the successful active investor stock picker’s chair. This involves an understanding of major cycles that impact markets with significant inflection points in direction and magnitude.  An understanding of their duration that gives us insight to the playbook on knowing when to hold ‘em…..and knowing when to fold ‘em.  This isn’t a trader mentality pocketing profits on the routine ebbs and flows or even the healthy 10% pullbacks after runups.  Instead, we ride a much bigger wave of secular and cyclical trend until it changes course for a long………TIME.

After all, how can TIME not be the most important factor in successfully TIMING the markets?
Yet virtually no one talks about it.

And this is where the macroeconomic data (KTS #13) within its cyclical behavior DOES play a key role in the broader understanding of WHEN the broader cycle will tsunami most everything down or rocket everything up……again.  A cycle of repeated behavior is ideal for those on Wall Street.  A playbook.  If we all whisper a set playbook, then we can take lead retail in…….and take their money out.  After all, if Bill Belichick knows what the Jets are going to do…….then the Patriots will never lose to them!

And it’s this parameter, that we will now begin to focus on in upcoming KTS posts.  Not price targets.  The wolves decide where they want to take prices.  We can make educated guesses.  But it’s completely up to them.  Don’t forget, they have (relatively) all of the money to move stocks. So they get to decide. 

No, we focus on TIME now.  And we do this with a closer monitoring of the clock.  Accuracy >>>>> Precision.  There’s a lot noise.  There’s a lot of emotion.  It’s easy to get caught up in meaningless data.  But the meaningful data?  These are the macroeconomic markers that repeat in cycles - relative to each other - that tell us roughly how many ticks are left on the timer.  Knowing which ones matter ……. and which ones don’t……. is key.  The Knowledge Transfer Series posts now will increasingly become updates on macroeconomic indicators of how much TIME is left.  I wouldn’t miss them for the world (and I won’t since I write them!  ).

Ok, Beskar.  I’m trying not to get lost here.  Can you summarize this for me?

Of course.  Here’s the takeaways:
(1)    We study the ticker tape to successfully TIME markets. (TECHNICALS).
(2)    The stock market isn’t the economy.  They can move in opposite directions. The direction of a stock’s price will follow the direction of its forward earnings (FUNDAMENTALS).
(3)    Stocks move on information that will impact forward earnings BEFORE it becomes publicly available (MONEY FLOWS).
(4)    Macroeconomic events and data serve as markers for taking action......or not yet taking action.... within the context of the real estate/banking crisis cycle (TIME).

These are the 4 legs of the active investor stock picker’s chair to successfully TIME markets.

I always give you my absolute best.  Read this one carefully.......again!

This is the Way!

title: While Canadians Invest in California....
caption:
.....we use our Knowledge Transfer Series toolkit to chunk away with the INSIDERS (as defined by KTS #11). 😂 🤣 👍

https://oilprice.com/Energy/Energy-General/US-Deepwater-Production-Is-Set-for-a-Record-High-in-2026.html

The Gulf of America (GoA) has witnessed an outstanding 2025 in terms of startup activity. Three new floating production units (FPU) are set to begin operations by year-end, with the potential to drive the basin’s deepwater output to an all-time high of nearly 2.2 million barrels of oil equivalent per day (boepd) in 2026.

The next five years will see significant new commissioning activity with the likes of FPU-based projects such as Sparta, Kaskida and Tiber – all of which target more challenging Lower Tertiary reservoirs. More regular leasing and the initial results from multi-client ocean bottom node (OBN) seismic surveys may also set the stage for new discoveries as US deepwater players refill their exploration hoppers. For now, though, 2025 is shaping up to be a standout year for the Gulf, with few parallels in the recent past or near future.

Staying ahead of the pack watching the lemmings cliff dive! 😂

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #79- TIMING Confirmation
caption:
🔮

They said it couldn't be done. You can't successfully TIME markets. After all, everyone knows that "TIME in the market is better than TIMING the market"! 😂 🤣

For those that subscribe to the B.S. statement above, they probably haven't been around for the final two years of a real estate/banking crisis cycle that follow the peak of markets in a meltup (KTS #10/#10A). Sticking around for a 7-year period before your portfolio takes out previous highs after suffering a 60%- 100% drop is NOT a win! Don't let those Conventionalists get to your brain. 😂 It's NOT A WIN!

Let's recap the key TIMING moves Beskar made since joining Blossom in March 2024:

(1) In March 2024, while everyone was certain of a May 2024 rate cut....we started positioning knowing there would be no rate cuts for the foreseeable future (minus political one before election). ✔️

(2) A portfolio overhaul into Commodities while everyone continued on more of the same. ✔️

(3) Following the EARLY INSIDERS (KTS #11) into stocks that would benefit from a TRUMP election. We follow the money. ✔️

(4) No buy in on the idea of a soft landing. Instead, we know that persistent inflation would rule the markets. ✔️

(5) A sector rotation into unloved sectors/industries that have been left for dead (KTS #2 / #74). ✔️

(6) Broadcasted that we are entering a period in which you can make 10 years of returns in just 2 years. Ridiculed by the Conventionalists......but they don't come around here no more, do they? 😂 🤣 😂 ✔️

(7) Reiterating that calendar year 2025 would be the year of HIGHEST RETURNS in the 2020-2030 decade.....even in the heart of the Liberation Day fallout. ✔️

I can go on and on.....but I need to get to the content of today's post as we just crossed 4,900 followers. 👍

The point is that you actually CAN TIME markets successfully if you know how to read the real estate/banking crisis cycle. It's a script like the NFL playoffs that those in control pretend they don't know! 😂 .....and it's NEVER too late to start! 🤑

Before we begin today's KTS post, you must read KTS #78. You can find it here:

https://link.blossomsocial.com/7uYa/lg8stsep

So let's jump in!

At the end of KTS #78, I encouraged you to monitor the relative performance behavior of 5 indicies: S&P500, Dow Jones, NASDAQ, MidCaps, and (MidCaps little brother) SmallCaps.

The conclusion was for @beskar_capital to continue to scale aggressively into Mid and Small Caps. Now, this doesn't mean exclusively.....but it does mean prioritizing in investment candidates. And here's why:

First, let's look at the chart below from August 7th, 2025 thru last Friday. It's the one with a lot of red markup! 😂

Careful examination and we see on August 11th-12th, a significant change in these 5 indicies. This is when the CPI data jumped higher than expected (for those that don't know how to read the cycle 😂). On that day, we saw the S&P500 jump 1%. And NASDAQ? 1.75% 🤑 The Dow Jones was even better....2% jump!?!! But MidCaps? +3.75% . Jesus. I mean...Geez. 😳 Last, MidCaps little bro.....SmallCaps? +5.75%. 💥

Ok, ok.....that's ridiculous gains, Beskar. But what happened following this?

Great question! After the big jump.....the markets naturally pulled back. But the insight to glean? Is how each pulls back RELATIVE TO EACH OTHER!

For those that have been following Beskar for awhile? You know the routine. Read that previous paragraph 3x in a row to ingrain in the membrane. 🧠 😄

So for the following week, all 5 dwindled lower......BUT.......one crossed over the performance chart of others! That's right, the NASDAQ went from the top performer following "jump day".... crossing below Small Caps......then crossing below the Dow Jones......then crossing below the S&P500. 😔 😟 🙁

It managed to hold over MidCaps. 👍

But then, Powell's Jackson Hole speech forecasted rate cuts coming sooner than later. As early as September. It seems imminent now......BUT even if those rate cuts don't even come in 2025 as Bank of America predicts......it doesn't matter now.....because the wolves are MONEY FLOWING INTO MidCaps and Small Caps now at a rapid pace. We have now transitioned from EARLY INSIDERS (KTS #11) into OTHER INSIDERS (KTS #11) phase. Retail INVESTORS (KTS #11) will be last......as always......and I want to be in before them.....because that's when the ridiculous money is made. 🐚 to 🐋

So, reference that same chart on Powell Speech Day at Jackson Hole. What happened in our Great Race? This TIME.....S&P500 jumps 1.25%. ✔️ NASDAQ? 2% 💰 Dow Jones? 2% 💲 MidCaps? 3% !?!?! 💸 And Small Caps? +4.26% 🤑 🤑 🤑

It's not even close! Do you see the pattern here? Again, even if rate cuts dont come immediately....the message is clear.....they WILL be coming......and the wolves are positioning now.....which will make these MidCap and SmallCap stocks climb! 📈

Now, let's do the Beskar step back....and look at these 5 indicies on a 3-year relative performance chart. Why? Because inevitably....someone will comment below ..."Did I miss the move in SmallCaps?" 😂 These market transitions (growth to value, large to small, small to large, stocks to bonds, domestic to international, etc......can take a llllloooooonnnnngggggg TIME!) So no....you didn't miss anyting! 😂. Looking at the 3-year.......the NASDAQ is in a class by itself. The S&P500 is rockin' with a solid unthreatened 2nd place. Then the Dow. Next, MidCaps. And in the wwwwaaaayyyy back? Small Caps.

Not even close. But we know how to read this transition, Conventionalists! 👊 💥 We see the closers coming from the back of the pack! 🏇 And we know the institutions are making their moves ....... NOW! 🔮 😂 👍

Let's move on to the next chart. $SPXA200R . A proprietary Breadth indicator (If you are 🇺🇸, highly recommend Schwab account with Think or Swim platform. It will positively change your financial life! 😉) . This chart goes back to COVID. What does it tell us? It tells us the percentage of S&P500 stocks above their 200-day moving average (KTS #47 worth a re-read ...... now 😁 ). That number has been rising since April 7th (remember that day 🤔 😂) from a low of 13% to today.....it stands at 68% !?!?!? 🤪 😜 🤪 😜

Why is Breadth important? Because it shows a willingness to look for stocks that are deserved to run......but haven't yet. The Mag 7 conversion to Lag 7 has slowly been transitioning......and these rate cuts before the peak in the real estate/banking crisis cycle will be the catalyst! 🤑

But let's get some more insight out of this Breadth check, shall we? I see that on June 30th, the indicator ......itself......broke it's 200-day moving average....effectively converting a previous ceiling into a new floor. 👍

In fact, I put GREEN and RED ARROWS on these key 200-day MA crossovers of the Breadth indicator onto the chart. Now, let's see how the SmallCap 600 behaves when the $SPXA200R rises above it's 200-day MA. Those are shown in those green windows on the white chart below of the index.

And what's particularly interesting this TIME? This isn't the normal ebbs and flows above and below the 200-day MA.....we have the run up into the peak of the meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle on our side. But make no mistake......this winner's curse will catch victims of ALL CAPS following the peak. But for now? We surf these tasty waves! 🏄‍♀️ 🌊 🏄‍♂️ 🌊

Last, some points I consider in SmallCaps and MidCaps.

(1) I don't invest in ETFs of these indicies. ETFs are diversified.....thus guarantee a diversified result. I prefer to search for the workhorses that carry these ETFs. After all, they go up hundreds of percent to offset the dogshit in the bottom of the fund. Especially SmallCaps!

(2) Small Caps and MidCaps have been so unloved.....for so long.......that many of them are sitting at or near as low as they can go until the peak of the cycle. How do we know this? Jump on KTS #28 and check out KTS #19/#20. So many of these companies are retiring shares through buybacks and management teams are loading up on shares for personal gain. Forget Congress! Follow the real money! 🤑🤑🤑

(3) Conventionalists will convince you that the lower price of Small Caps justify their assertion that they are risky. Don't believe the hype! 😂 That alone is not enough to make such conclusions. Dig in on these companies and find out. Ask yourself, how risky is it to invest in a company raising earnings estimates, beating those estimates, raising again while raising dividends, buying back shares, and reducing leverage ratios towards no debt......ALL WITH INCREASING FREE CASH FLOWS!!! Those companies are out there. 😉 🏆 So don't let a low share price alone convince you otherwise. The Conventionalists will tell you that markets can be irrational. And I'd characterize decade plus of out of favor TIME for rationality to creep back in....justifiably! 😂

(4) All of this said, many small caps are not profitable. Find those that are....or very soon to be. For example, $MIND just crossed above their 200-DEMA for the first TIME since 2013!?!?!? And why? A return to profitability! 👍 🤑 Those that follow me know we've been pyramiding into this one for some TIME! 🤑 When rate cuts come, less capital goes to bank interest and goes into the balance sheet instead.....which is good for company stock prices. But do your homework!

I always give you my best! Thanks to the 4,902 followers that decided this is the way and that it'sworth lending an ear to how to TIME markets! After all, no matter what anyone says......most everyone of us actually attempts to do so whether they recognize it or not! If you don't believe that, than think about your rationale the next TIME you make a trade! 😂 🤣 😂 🤣

Next up? 5,000 followers. I have a special one for you. 😉

This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️ 🌊

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #78- TIME for Small Caps
caption:
Another 100 followers and here we go into KTS #78! 🤑 👍

Yesterday, we took a poll to see which of the following category of stocks does our Blossom following think will outperform in the near to midterm. Here's the results and these were our choices:

https://link.blossomsocial.com/7uYa/mcmnp4sk

(1) S&P500
(2) Dow Jones Industrial
(3) NASDAQ
(4) MidCaps
(5) SmallCaps

As I expected, the S&P500 narrative of the Conventionalists ruled the day by collecting the most votes. 🙄

But pleasantly surprised that the group had - as a close second - was SmallCaps! 😃

Finding points in TIME when these indicies relative performance cross over each other are ALWAYS noteworthy.......especially if the crossover holds for more than a couple of weeks on a longer-term chart. 👍

Wait.....why, Beskar? 🤔

Because it just doesn't happen that often........and when it does......it signals longer-term rotations by the whales. 🐋 🐳 And it typically takes a llllloooooonnnnngggggg TIME for a whale to turnaround and head in a new direction.

Note: Read the previous two paragraphs above 5x for your financial health. 😂

So in typical Beskar fashion, let's look at some charts to learn more!

First up, we step back and view the MAX chart of these 5 indicies, which is the 32-year chart. What can we learn?

I see since February 1993 through today....that investing in the NASDAQ absolutely crushed the other categories with a return of 2,700%+ !?!?!? Wow!!! I also see that MidCaps blew the S&P500 out of the water with a return of 1,705% ! 💥 Next, even SmallCaps edged out the S&P500 with an impressive return of 1,295%. The only laggard that the S&P500 could eclipse? The Dow Jones. Still, the Dow Jones delivered 1,095% over the past 32 years compared to the S&P500's nearly 1,300% over the past 32 years.

What else can we observe?

On this scale, these lines don't cross much do they? 😂 I see that when the Dotcom bubble burst in late 1999 that MidCaps made a move from 3rd place to 2nd......then capturing first place in 2001. MidCaps continued to lead the pack......aaaaaallllllll tttttthhhhhheeeee wwwwwwaaaaayyyy until COVID hit and NASDAQ regained the pole position !?!?!?! What!?!?!? That's crazy, Beskar !?!?!?!? 🤪 😜

It's true. And notice that MidCaps little brother, SmallCaps, also outperformed the S&P500, NASDAQ, and the DOW JONES for that same period of 15 years.

All of these "indicies" are dilutive by carrying boat loads of qualifying stocks. As in qualifying to be included in the category. But within each, there are ALL STARS, plenty of winners and losers, and of course.....complete dogshit stocks.

But what does make sense? From KTS #17, we expect stock charts to move with earnings. So does it surprise us that the highest margins are in companies in the fast growing NASDAQ? No, that makes sense. And that MidCaps are next with an average growth rate that rivals the NASDAQ since they haven't tired into a behemoth sloth large cap? And does it surprise us that small caps, some with ridiculous growth rates 🤑 with new products/services .....mixed with some trying to achieve profitability.......average into the same atmosphere as those two. No, it shouldn't. All while the heavy loaded S&P500 and the old value stocks bring up the rear? Again, no it shouldn't surprise anyone.

Nonetheless, we'll keep this in mind - with a grain of salt 😂 - the next TIME we read the warnings on investing in small caps. 😂 (and we won't invest in any index of small caps! 😂 🤑)

Now, let's move in and look at the 10-year chart. 👍

Aaaaahhhh. Different picture, eh? Sure, NASDAQ is still flexing 💪 as we see that breakout from the COVID drop. But where is Big Bro and Little Bro? Last place! 😂 Both are woefully trailing - by a wide margin - NASDAQ, S&P500, and the DOW JONES! Not even close.

Other observation? I don't see much line crossing, do you? 🤔 These are really different from their long term trend. SmallCaps and MidCaps still run together. The DOW and S&P500 hold their relative position similar to the 32-year chart. And NASDAQ is outta this world! 🌎 🚀

Let's go to the 1-year chart now.

What do we see? Hmmm. NASDAQ doesn't have quite the lead it does in the other charts 🤔 .....but make no mistake, it's still #1 over the past year! 💯 Again, not much crossing of lines.....but note that I saw DOW JONES run above the S&P500 in late 2024 and hold it until May 2025.....during a period of uncertainty.

Remember that TIME period? 🤔 👍

Last, let's look at the 10-day chart.

Any observations?

I see a much tighter range because it's a shorter-dated chart.....but more importantly, I see an abrupt crossing in relative performance, do you? (see red oval) What happened on August 12th that caused the SmallCaps to giddy up? 😀 Anticipation of interest rate cuts. Why? Less dollars going to bank interest and more dollars going into company balance sheet. 🤑 🤑 🤑 🤑

https://www.cnbc.com/2025/08/13/small-cap-rally-magnificent-seven-megacap-tech-slide.html

I also see the DOW JONES making a move to close the gap with S&P500. 🤔 And is that NASDAQ continuing to tire in this race?

It's been 5 days now since the SmallCaps have taken a clear lead. Every day forward it's worth watching this to see if it continues to hold. Yes, this is a typically weak period for stocks....back to school and tuition money to university! 🤮 😂 But it's RELATIVE performance that we need to tune in to now. So a quick check today......let's see.....SmallCaps are taking a hit (down -0.78%).......yet EXTENDING it's relative performance lead over the NASDAQ, which is down -1.34%. And the DOW JONES? UP +0.04% vs. The S&P500 down -0.55%. Relative 🏆. 😉

There are many other measures that are screaming for SmallCaps (and MidCaps) now. And please don't mistake this post as getting caught up in the day-to-day. Because that's not it. 😂 Instead, can we learn to spot inflection points and longer-term crossovers of whale rotation? 😉

Remember, KTS #11, #19, and #20! 🐚 to 🐋

As I currently sit on a 1,305.26% return on my CALL LEAPS in $MP MP Materials in less than just over 1 year.....comfortable that that money has outperformed the S&P500 over the past 32 years 😂 🤣 🤑......I know it's worth taking the TIME to see the game with a bird's eye view! 🐦

I see a lot more support for Small and MidCaps.....do you?

We'll investigate those more when we get our next 100 followers. 😂 👍

Stay tuned!

This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️ 🌊

title: Gold on the Ceiling
caption:
Do you see what's going on?

According to a 2025 report by the World Gold Council, Central banks have accumulated over 1,000t of gold in each of the last three years, up significantly from the 400-500t average over the preceding decade.1 This marked acceleration in the pace of accumulation has occurred against a backdrop of geopolitical and economic uncertainty, which has clouded the outlook for reserve managers and investors alike.

And who are the main buyers? BRICS nations. And why? A race to replace the US Dollar as the world's reserve currency.

Forget drones, missiles, and bombs. This level of warfare is the kind that can take a country down. So how is the 🇺🇸 going to counter? The US government and the banks (remember from previous Beskar posts that these are one and the same) are rolling out a new cryptocurrency in "digital tokens". These token coins were pioneered by the company Tether who thought it could leverage the benefits of the block chain with instant transactions....but with a currency pegged at a reserve ratio of 1:1. So they have digital tokens for currencies like the yen, yuan, and the dollar. Behind each of these, the company secures - in their reserves - a corresponding amount on a 1:1 ratio for a fully-backed currency.

This is important. Because in the USA, we threw out the 100% reserve ratio long ago. In fact, today, banks on average only have a ~14% reserve ratio. This is a problem when fear and panic hit and there's a run on the banks. If everyone rushes to line up and pull their money out....the banks only have 14% of the deposits they have in the books to actually dispense!?!?!

And the conditions are on the horizon for fear and panic. What are you talking about Beskar?

Currency volatility ✔️
Geopolitical Uncertainty ✔️
Rising/unsustainable worldwide debt ✔️
Peak of real estate/banking crisis cycle ✔️

Runs on banks are a common occurrence after markets peak in the real estate/banking crisis cycle.
https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_bank_runs

And as we head into that peak, the conditions above make a rush to hard assets like precious metals a high probability of a low risk, high return bet.

We are not there yet. But you can see that the Central Banks have been preparing for it for the past 3 years. Even Costco has with gold bars selling out like their hot dog bait! 🌭 ✨️

Meanwhile, the 🇺🇸 just passed a law in Congress that they will be issuing "stablecoin" tokens ..... and that ALL stablecoins issued by US banks..... will be backed 100% by US Treasuries. A sneaky way to get the world to buy US treasuries while they try to abandon it, eh? 😂

But that doesn't help if people think the currency is in trouble and will lose value. 😨

THERE SIMPLY IS NO REPLACEMENT FOR HARD SPECIE WHEN FEAR RULES THE DAY. It won't be crypto or any other fiat currency. No one is going to enter into philosophical discussions about whether these alternatives are hedges, commodities, or anything else when they are worried their lifeTIME savings are in danger of disappearing. They will simply run towards gold.....like the Central banks are.....for 3 years and running now.

But what I want to share is what Tether just did recently.......so YOU can do your research on potential investment ideas. 😉

Tether Gold is a new token.....fully backed 100%, pegged 1:1 to gold fine Troy ounces. It is quickly gaining popularity behind the scenes:

https://tether.io/news/tether-gold-grows-with-more-than-7-66-tons-of-gold-backing-xaut-as-of-q2-2025-confirming-continued-strength-in-tokenized-gold/

On June 10, 2025, Tether Investments acquired 78,421,780 common shares of Elemental (the “La Mancha Shares”) from La Mancha Investments S.à.r.l., representing approximately 31.9% of Elemental’s issued and outstanding shares. This transaction was completed offshore via private agreement.

$ELEMF is a proud Beskar holding in the precious metals royalty space. So I noticed the jump in the stock following this news from ~$1.00 USD to $1.45 USD. A little digging and you learn that Tether bought the shares for $1.55 CAD. That's a floor folks. And we love price floors. 🤑

https://tether.io/news/tether-acquires-strategic-stake-in-elemental-altus-to-deepen-push-into-gold-and-hard-asset-backed-financial-infrastructure/

Even more digging and you learn that they have options to accumulate more and more and more shares of the company. And they prefer the safer royalty model to source their reserve gold! This is the company pioneering it all. And you know the 🇺🇸 will come with their gold tokens as well. The gold reserves CAN STAY IN THE GROUND and it counts as reserves!!!!! Oh shit... 🤑 🤑 🤑 🤑 🤑

This is early stages, folks. PRECIOUS METAL ROYALTIES ARE ON THE VERGE OF EXPLOSIVE DEMAND AND CONSOLIDATION. 💰 🤑 💸 💲

Those that follow me, you know the names,
$ELEMF, $GROY, $WPM, $SAND, $TFPM,
on and on.

Energy transition and AI transition are phenomenal themes to invest in. But when you completely overhaul how the financial system functions? THAT is an investment idea! 💲 While the world runs to find winners in new cryptocurrencies and technologies.......the ultimate winner - when fear rules the day - will be the hard assets that stand the test of TIME! ✨️

I always give you my best and help you stay ahead of the Conventionalists. 😂 👍

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #77- Step Back 3-Pointer
caption:
As we quickly cross the 4,700 follower mark, it's TIME for another Knowledge Transfer Series (KTS) post. 👍

For those new to the group, the KTS is a toolset that will fast forward you along the lifeTIME learning curve of investment knowledge so you can outperform your peers. There are several followers routinely demonstrating KTS principles and realizing incredible annualized double-digit outperformance of the indices. You too can join them! 🤑

Today's tool demonstrates an assembly of KTS tools that I have used over the past 2 months to accurately read and listen to markets to successfully TIME them. So think of it as a HOW TO GUIDE to find stocks that are ready to run. 🏃‍♀️ 📈

The Step Back 3-Pointer. 🏀

If you don't know what this is, just search up a video of NBA basketball player James Harden's highlights. No one does it better. Just a step move like you're going to drive the lane and then pullback to give you all the TIME and space you need for an uncontested shot. SWISH!!!!! Nothin' but net!

Here's a primer: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=w6p-UJJntgE

So it's a move to find TIME and SPACE to improve your probability to score!

How does this relate to stocks? For those that remember reading KTS #15, you know Beskar likes to start by constantly step back to study longer-dated stock charts to see what's going on within the context of the point of TIME within the real estate/banking crisis cycle. By identifying secular trends and outperforming sectors/subsectors/industries (KTS #2, KTS #74), we can identify stocks that are getting ready to take off! 🚀

Then, we can hone in on shorter-dated charts (5-year, 1-year, 3-month, etc.) for confirmations using our deep KTS toolset.

Once you identify a stock candidate that satisfies your KTS toolset, there's no need to wait to start a position and then pyramid accumulate it (KTS #46).

So today's KTS tool is a step back to observe the ticker tape to use TIME and SPACE as an advantage to pick winning stocks.

Just two months ago, I didn't own a single share of Hecla Mining, $HL. Those that follow me saw me start an initial CALL LEAP positions with multiple contracts on multiple strikes/expiration (KTS #30). (For a good overview of options, see KTS #30 thru KTS #36A).

Soon thereafter, @beskar_capital started a stock position in Hecla and accumulation plan through a series of 17 buy trades as the stock went from 0% of my portfolio to being a rostered player at 1.5% at the moment (KTS #26) - in just 2 months. The latest purchase was in after-hours following their earnings report on Wednesday. And it's not done being accumulated by me yet either.

So what is it that you see in Hecla, Beskar? I mean.....how do I figure something like that out??? 🤔

Great question. So let's jump in.

Attached are 3 stock charts of Hecla Mining and some of the key KTS tools that you are familiar with if you follow me. I can't show them all because you can't see the screenshot if I do! But I'll talk about them along with the observations of the KTS tools in the attachments!

So, first, look at the one labeled MAX stock chart for Hecla. What are the takeaways?

First, we know that commodity stocks are ripe for investment in the meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle. If you don't know that, you need to start with my first post in March 2024, "They are telling us so what are you waiting for?" and then read the Knowledge Transfer Series in its entirety.

Here's the link:
https://link.blossomsocial.com/7uYa/tztr0whg

Let's move on. We started into mining stocks in the Spring of 2024 with the EARLY INSIDERS (as defined by KTS #11) knowing the stage of the meltup phase. It's no secret - even to retail anymore - that precious metals, food commodities, oil/gas, rare earths, etc. are all climbing. But Hecla? The stock hasn't been going anywhere since the Spring of 2024. 😑

So let's get back to MAX chart. Here's what I see. A long-term wedge with a ceiling going back to 1989 that has had it's price decline from $10.00 to $5.00 just this past May 22, 2025.

Next, the floor of this stock chart has been steadily climbing since 2000 from 50 cents up to $3.50. As we know from KTS #29, this convergence will lead to a stock price to make a break - one way or the other. But what gives us the confidence that it breaks higher.......and now????

Next, look at the volume chart beneath the MAX stock chart, you can see that the rising floor that started in 2000 coincides with an increase in volume (KTS #70). The green columns are periods where there's net more buys than sells and the red columns are vice versa.

Next, we look at the Rolling EPS and EPS trend (KTS #76 A/B). I see over the past 1.5 years that the earnings are trending higher......and that the company is coming back in to profitability. 💰 🤑 💸 💲

So what about KTS #28? A quick listen of the past few events and you learn that Hecla has a new management team focused on shareholder returns! Whoa......I like that! And KTS #19? What do you know........insiders are buying. 🤑 That's good. What else can we learn? Hecla is already mining 4 mines in geopolitically safe North America.......and 3 more mines have been hand picked by the government's FAST-41 projects where the typical 10-year long permitting process is expedited within one year!?!?!? 😂 What else? Silver is breaking out of a 13-year high right now......and Hecla's stock $HL is most leveraged to the price of Silver $SLV. And for those that study cycles......silver is ready to run. It's current gold:silver ratio is 3x higher than just the historical norm. That means a reversion to the mean is a 3x rise in the price of silver!?!?!? Oh geez. This case for Hecla $HL is getting good. 🤑 🤑 🤑

Next, still on the MAX chart, looking at the A/D line (KTS #49).....it appears to be in decline over the past year but bottoming and consolidating. At the same TIME, the OBV line (KTS #70) is rising......and rapidly!!!!! This setup is a golden setup: A/D declining, OBV climbing, Price chart flat. THIS IS A MAJOR BUYING SITUATION!!!! And the longer this goes on?......the longer the run in TIME when the coiled potential energy releases into a kinetic liftoff! (KTS #48) 🐍

Let's move to the 10-YEAR CHART. Let's look for those observations here too. It becomes crystal clear, eh? 🔮 Pattern break out with increasing net buying volumes while A/D has been declining and leveling off while OBV climbs. This is how you identify EARLY INSIDER behavior!
And what about the 1.5-year chart? It becomes even clearer. Earnings trend is higher and steppin' out (KTS #76 A/B), Net buying Volumes picked up even more in early 2025, didn't they? Yet......the stock goes nowhere!?!?!? 😑

Following the earnings call (KTS #28), the cat is out of the bag. We would like to see the stock come back down to $6.50 and test that 35-year+ ceiling as a new floor and bounce off and above it. That's a pyramid marker to BUY MORE hand-over-fist. $HL

And what about our other KTS tools? KTS #17? KTS #25? KTS #50?, KTS #75 A/B/C? They all matter. Imagine if you had a KTS screener like the one I developed many years ago? 😉

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #76B-Moving Off the Puck
caption:
** THIS POST IS A CONTINUATION OF KNOWLEDGE TRANSFER SERIES POST #76A.  BE SURE TO READ THAT ONE FIRST BEFORE READING THIS ONE! 
 
You know what, Beskar? Your kids are right.......no one wants all of this introduction.......you run on too long! 😂 🤣

Can you just tell us the tool already?!?!!

Ok, ok. 😂 Let's get right into it then.

Smart movement and anticipation is a silent skill of looking forward. But this always requires being able to read the cycle. As you know, we anticipated the market volatility in the first half of the year with the new administration. And you also know that with a new administration we heavily rely on our macroeconomic tool (KTS #13). But the tools we need now in this second half of 2025 (the calendar year of highest returns in the 2020-2030 decade, remember?) come back to KTS #17 Fundamental Analysis. Whoa! I wasn't expecting that, Beskar. After all, you've been correctly suggesting that Meme stocks, crypto, profitless SPACs, 0DTE options, penny stocks, etc. are back in vogue! 🤑 💸

Ahhhh yes.......that is true.....very true! 👍

And they will continue to run in this final winner's curse stage of the meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle.

BUT......the crash and burn will take most down when it eventually comes.........and it will destroy most all that didn't grab the parachute. It will take a long TIME for the speculation event to put in the inflection point so no need to run yet........BUT when it does......you'll wake up and realize there's nothing you can do as it elevator drops.

So again, it's reading the cycle. What's going on right now? The Baby Boomers......you know, the ones born in the late 40's to early 60's with relatively ALL of the money........they've been sitting in money markets for years now......watching the rest of us routinely locking in triple-digit returns over the past year. 😂

Now that tariff policy and geopolitical events are finding a relatively settled down status. And......though not yet.......the collective pressure of the crosscurrent economy is finding more support for cutting interest rates. Yesterday, 2 federal reserve board members dissented from the Fed decision to hold rates for the first TIME since 1993!?!?! 🤪 On top of that, TRUMP floated that he's going to start cutting checks to the American people as a tariff dividend!?!?! 💰 🔥

So lower rates, tax cuts, and citizen dividend payments? 💥! We are on the verge of the final runup which comes with MASSIVE LIQUIDITY INJECTIONS and lower rates will see those (jealous 😂) Boomers start transferring it from the ever-lower interest bearing money markets into a hot market on 🔥 that is about to be doused with an unheard amount gasoline to take the flames to levels you can't imagine! 🛢 🔥 🛢 🔥 🤯 💥 🤑 🤢 🤮

But as these Baby Boomers come in.......many are seasoned enough to baby step in by looking for companies that are doing well. So we can expect movement from speculative shit to continue to rocket......but we can - at this TIME - expect to get in ahead of the push for companies with an increasingly bright outlook.

And that tool is the FORWARD EARNINGS ESTIMATE TREND that is climbing constantly. 🪜

It's the tool of choice to use now for Beskar! Why? Because the collective analysts can't keep pace with the growth. In other words, the average analyst forward estimate continues to climb. How do we identify those candidates? Look into your platform for a Rolling EPS or EPS trend indicator. Your platform should have that. If it doesn't.......it makes sense to me that you find one that does!!! Remember, not all stocks are winners. It's a search for the RIGHT STOCKS at the RIGHT TIME!

Find companies that show a clear and distinct stair-stepping climb in earnings estimates ....which translates into earnings themselves. The economy is about to re-accelerate in a final push (remember this can last a llllloooonnnnngggggg TIME) and stocks that analysts can't keep up with are set to explode 📈 .....because this is where most of the Baby Boomers inflows will go. They can't risk the speculative names to the extent that you can as they are retired or in the process of retiring.

So love finding stocks just breaking out in their price pattern charts (KTS #29) or have been FLAT all the while having AT LEAST THREE EPS TREND STAIR STEPS! 😉

The higher the step increment, the better!

The shorter the step increment, the better!

The more consecutive steps, the better!
