title: Knowledge Transfer Series #76A-Moving Off the Puck
caption:
We eclipsed the 4,600 follower mark in a little over one year on the Blossom platform earlier in the week so I owe you another Knowledge Transfer Series post! 👍

For those that are new to the series, I encourage you to go back and read my first post from March 2024 "They are telling us so what are you waiting for?" and then begin reading the Knowledge Transfer Series in order. 😉

Had you done so over a year ago, you could have your portfolio positioned perfectly for this moment in TIME of the final stages of the meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle. Absolutely amazing double-digit returns (in several cases........a decade worth of Conventional returns in well less than a year 🤑) to blow away the performance of Conventionalists and their index ETFs! The great news is that the market is dynamic and always changing. So what's valued today will change like the weather. Is it a momentum market? Growth? Midcap? International? Certain sectors?

So you can either learn how to read and listen to markets to TIME it successfully to continue to win despite sentiment, sector favorability, etc. by developing a toolkit that can handle all market conditions........OR ................. you can simply give up and buy the index for average, Conventional performance. 🤔

I'm guessing that since you're here reading this.......you are seeking ways to grow your money faster? 😂

This series is meant to transfer my 35+ years of investment knowledge in an easily digestible way to those that want to fast forward along the learning curve by building a toolkit that can handle any market. After all, I've already made the mistakes, backtested methods, developed models, and learned from them for you so no need to reinvent the wheel! 😂 🤣 😅

So let's get started with today's post! The beginning of Volume II, post #76 - Moving Off the Puck . 🏒

Certainly - at least the Canadians on this site - have heard the phrase that 95% of the game you skate without having the puck. That's right......95% of the TIME, no puck on stick! So how do the greatest players (your aspiration 😃 👍) differentiate themselves?

By creating opportunities that others can't see.

How do you do that, Beskar? 🤔

Well.......sports is a fantastic analogy for it. In all team sports, the objective is to create TIME and space advantages that provide your team higher percentage opportunities to score. In basketball, we quickly make 2-3 swing passes around the 3-point arc to give that final player a great, uncontested look at the rim! In soccer, we can pull the defender out to man mark us, creating a passing lane for a teammate. And in hockey, we can join the rush to outnumber the opponents to create more TIME and space options to improve our chance to score!

Smart Spacing and anticipation is what separates the greats. I mean who doesn't like the way Leon Draisaitl plays??? Do you think he's lucky that he seems to always be in the RIGHT PLACE at the RIGHT TIME? Or what about Brad Marchant 🐀? I can't stand him.....but I can respect that he knows how to create opportunities!!!

So how can we be the best we can be in investing in markets? Well.....it starts with smart movement off the puck. Constantly repositioning in anticipation by observing the game from the rafters (that's usually where my seats are anyway! 😂). It's game awareness and IQ that will set us apart from the lemmings that just regurgitate the message they want you to follow (which of course......none of them actually do themselves! ).

So for those that follow me, do you ever wonder how @beskar_capital comes up with some of these amazing tickers???? Out of the blue.....and you think......geez, will I ever be able to source these kind of opportunities on my own??? The answer is YES you can.....and if you continue to follow me, you'll see I how navigate the cycle........which is always changing.....and never ends. So lesson to log now......there is ALWAYS a new opportunity right in front of you at this very moment!

So Don't look back with remorse and regret......we'll always be moving on!

🎶 🎵 Don't look back
A new day is breakin'
It's been too long since I felt this way
I don't mind where I get taken
The road is callin'
Today is the day 🎶 🎵

Golden Beskar Rule. Ingrain in the Membrane. 🧠

President Trump announced a deal that imposes a 15% tariff on imports from South Korea, a move that averts a steeper 25% levy originally threatened by Washington. The U.S.-EU trade deal calls for the European Union buying $250 billion worth of American energy EVERY YEAR over the next three years.

American energy is a winner in the U.S.-EU trade deal, which sets tariffs on most EU goods at 15%, half compared to the initially proposed 30%.

However, U.S. LNG exports would need to dramatically increase – from already high levels – if Europe is to buy a total of $750 billion of American energy in three years.

Is Tripling the EU Purchases of U.S. Energy Feasible?

The acceleration of U.S. exports will undoubtedly increase America’s geopolitical leverage at a time when Europe seeks to ditch Russian gas by 2027.

Yet, analysts question the feasibility of the U.S. LNG export boom as the EU will need to at least triple its 2024 imports of U.S. oil, gas, and coal to meet the terms of the trade deal.

Trump Says 'We’ll Just Step It Up'

Trump’s bet—that American producers can fill the gap.

And GREAT NEWS, Canada!

It's going to be full throttle on both sides of the border.

🇺🇸 🤝 🇨🇦 ........ for the love of oil, coal, nuclear, and natural gas.

title: Gulf of America has now opened a new frontier
caption:
$RIG
$WTI
$TALO
$SLB
......on and on

Beacon Offshore Energy has officially started oil and gas production at its long-awaited Shenandoah field in the U.S. Gulf of Mexico, the company confirmed Friday—marking a major milestone not just for Beacon, but for the ultra-deepwater Lower Tertiary Wilcox play.

Though delayed by a month, the startup is a significant moment in the arc of U.S. deepwater development. Shenandoah was once a poster child of high-spec frontier drilling. First identified as a prize in the Lower Wilcox, Shenandoah sat dormant for years—until now.

What makes this so important? The Lower Wilcox Tertiary trend is widely regarded as the last great untapped oil province in the Gulf of Mexico. 

With legacy deepwater fields declining and exploration squeezed by ESG mandates, Shenandoah isn’t just another startup. It’s a bet that the Gulf still matters—and that the Lower Wilcox isn’t done making headlines.

https://oilprice.com/Latest-Energy-News/World-News/Beacon-Offshore-Kicks-Off-Shenandoah-Production.html

title: The Robots are Coming! I mean...they're here!
caption:
😂 🤣

Why Canadian O&G should attract your investment.

FOCUS-How Canada's oil sands transformed into one of North America's lowest-cost plays

6:00am ET, 07/16/2025 - Reuters

Canadian oil sands benefit from geology, technology, and cost-cutting measures

Canadian oil sands have lower break-even prices than U.S. shale producers

Technological advances improve efficiency and reduce costs in oil sands operations

By Amanda Stephenson

CALGARY, July 16 (Reuters) - Giant shovels, driverless trucks and a dog-like robot have all helped Canada's oil sands companies including Imperial Oil and Suncor become some of North America's lowest-cost oil producers, driving down overheads even as the worst inflation in a generation pushed U.S. shale costs up. As the global oil industry enters a downturn due to economic uncertainty related to U.S. tariffs policy and OPEC+ pumping more barrels, Canada's oil sands industry finds itself in a position of strength. In the years following the oil price crash of 2014-15, international oil majors including BP, Chevron and Total sold their interests in Canadian oil sands. At the time, they classified the Canadian operations as among their more expensive, and therefore less profitable, projects worldwide. They directed their capital to cheaper oil production, and favored U.S. shale for its quicker drilling time and returns.

Since then, new technology and cost-cutting efforts have driven meaningful improvement in the industry's competitiveness that make oil sands among the cheapest producers, according to a dozen industry insiders and a Reuters analysis of the latest U.S. and Canadian company earnings. While U.S. shale companies are responding to this year's oil price downturn by dropping rigs, slashing capital spending and laying off workers, the oil sands' position of strength means Canadian companies have made virtually no changes to their previously announced production or spending plans. Some Canadian politicians are now calling for a new crude pipeline from Alberta to the Pacific coast, as part of a broader effort to strengthen the country's economy in the face of U.S. tariff threats.

The lower crude prices this year have little impact on the Canadian oil sector, Cenovus CEO Jon McKenzie said in an interview earlier this year. "This is an industry that has become much more resilient through time," he said. In one example, two four-legged robots— each nicknamed Spot because of their dog-like appearance — prowl Imperial's vast 45-year-old Cold Lake operation in Alberta, conducting routine equipment inspections and maintenance such as heat exchanger optimizations, and oil/water tank interface monitoring. The Spots free up human workers for other work and save Imperial CDN$30 million ($22 million) a year, the company said. Exxon-owned Imperial and its competitor Suncor have also switched to autonomous mining vehicles, eliminating the need to hire drivers to transport oil sands ore. The switch has improved oil output productivity at Imperial's Kearl oil sands mine by 20% since 2023, the company said. Suncor operates a 900-tonne truck at its Fort Hills operation north of Fort McMurray, Alberta, which the company says is the world's largest hydraulic mining shovel. Suncor CEO Rich Kruger said the shovel's larger bucket and more powerful digging force deliver faster ore loading and less spillage.

Oil sands producers have also made improvements in equipment reliability and performance. At Kearl, for example, Imperial has reduced expenses related to turnarounds — an industry term for the costly periods of required maintenance that often involve temporarily shutting down production — by CDN$100 million annually since 2021. The company cut the time between turnarounds from 12 to 24 months in 2024, and aims to extend that interval to 48 months in future. Suncor credits efforts including standardizing maintenance practices across mines and improving management of site water to get more production out of existing assets for contributing to the company's US$7 per barrel reduction in its West Texas Intermediate (WTI) break-even price in 2024 to $42.90. This long-term focus on cost-cutting means Canada's five biggest oil sands companies can break even — and still maintain their dividends — at WTI prices between $43.10 and $40.85, according to a Bank of Montreal analysis for Reuters.

That means oil sands producers have lowered their overall costs by approximately $10 a barrel in about seven years. Oil sands had an average break-even price of $51.80/bbl between 2017 and 2019, according to BMO.

In contrast, a recent Dallas Federal Reserve survey of over 100 oil and gas companies in Texas, New Mexico and Louisiana found that shale oil producers need a WTI oil price of $65 per barrel on average to profitably drill. Back in 2017-2019, U.S. shale producers had a break-even price of between $50 and $52 per barrel.

HIGH STARTUP COSTS, BUT LONG LIFESPANS

Part of the reason that the oil sands industry has become so cost competitive is the nature of the extraction process. Producing the thick, sticky oil that is found in the sands of Alberta is in some locations more akin to mining than oil drilling.

Where the oil is very close to the surface, companies operate massive mines, scraping up huge volumes of sand and clay and then filtering out the oil. When the oil is deeper, companies inject steam underground to loosen the deposits and then use a drilling process. An oil sands mine has big initial start-up costs but once it is operational, it can run for decades with very low production decline rates. Canadian Natural Resources, for example, at the end of 2024 had proved and probable reserves amounting to 20.1 billion barrels of oil equivalent in its portfolio, giving its oil sands mining and upgrading assets a remaining reserve lifespan of 43 years. The company's Horizon oil sands mine has been producing since 2009.

Shale oil wells, by contrast, have low start up costs. Oil output from the wells, however, begins to decline within months. Prices have begun to climb because after years of heavy drilling in the top shale fields, the most productive areas have been exhausted. Drillers are moving onto secondary areas, so they have to drill more wells to achieve the same output and that has driven up costs.

Canadian oil sands companies have also paid down debt in the past five years, allowing them to reallocate profits away from shoring up their balance sheets and towards rewarding shareholders with dividends and buybacks.

According to the Bank of Montreal, oil sands producers Canadian Natural Resources, Suncor, Cenovus, Imperial Oil and MEG Energy currently have combined net debt, excluding lease liabilities, of C$33.9 billion after paying down a combined total of almost C$22 billion in debt between 2021 and 2024.

As returns grow, Canadian oil sands producers are an increasingly attractive investment for those looking to make money from the energy industry, said Kevin Burkett, portfolio manager with Vancouver-based Burkett Asset Management.

"(Canada's oil sands) are not geopolitically risky, and they have some very appealing characteristics around productivity and costs," said Burkett, who has shares of Canadian Natural Resources and Cenovus in his portfolio.

title: Conventionalists Proclaimed Tariffs are a Tax 🇺🇸
caption:
Remember?

When the tariff talk started in Q1 2025, armchair QBs everywhere came out to proclaim the tariffs would simply translate into a tax on consumers.

Have you been keeping up with the news? These articles are everywhere.

https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-07-10/japan-s-carmakers-slash-export-prices-to-us-to-offset-tariff-hit

Japan’s automakers slashed the price of products exported to the US at a record pace, in a sign that companies are sacrificing profits to remain competitive as President Donald Trump’s tariffs hit cars.

Last month the export price index for vehicles shipped to North America plunged 19.4% from a year earlier on a contract currency basis, the biggest drop in records going back to 2016, according to the Bank of Japan’s corporate goods price report Thursday.

How wrong that Convenvetionalist proclamation has been! 😂

Did you know that for a majority of U.S. history that tariffs have been the main revenue generator? It wasn't until 1913 that Woodrow Wilson (Lib 😂) implemented an income tax on the citizens in order to reduce tariffs on other countries. So the first 137 years tariffs ON. Next 111 years tariffs OFF. So most of the TIME, tariffs were in place. The switch just got flipped again in 2025. (Aside, ever wonder why Mt. Rushmore doesn't have any Presidents that were Democrats? 😂 🤣 )

Additionally, prices have been in decline in 2025. In fact, eggs, gasoline, and most grocery store consumables have dropped, on average, substantially - gas to 2021 levels. But there's no guarantee that deflation will continue. In fact, conditions are setting up for the opposite. I wonder if this crisis will see something that's never happened to the USA before.....where the Fed sees a need to keep long-term bond yields high (that means not cutting rates this TIME) to attract foreign investment that everyone (especially Conventionalists) seems certain the world is finding an alternative. This at the same TIME as a reset in housing prices could lead to depression, not recession. 🤔

But why aren't tariffs taxing? It's not a good reason 😔...... It's because the consumer is totally tapped out. Anyone remotely connected to the corporate world knows the companies would absolutely pass on the cost to the consumers.....if they actually could. But they can't.

The consumers are tapped out. Credit data. Spending data. Savings data. They all point the same thing. 😔

So the CrossCurrents are becoming ever present. But we've seen this before....11x prior if you study the ticker tape. And we know.....now is NOT the TIME to run. Instead, it's the final stage of the meltup phase. The bubble is ever present. I think most of us can see it, right? But these bubbles can blow and blow and blow.....and blow bigger and bigger and bigger....and bigger .....before it pops! Believe it or not, this AI bubble that has started isn't even close to looking like the bubbles of the past (see future upcoming post). So the ultimate AI bubble (and this includes stocks in ALL industries) .....is just getting started if measured against all previous bubbles.

So this is a TIME to BUY 🤑 .....not BYE 🤮 ! Remember, 2025 will be the year of greatest returns in the 2020-2030 decade. 😉

It's all in the ticker tape. You just have to know how to read and listen to markets in order to TIME them successfully! 🔮 👍

So the lesson is to know you can handle the ebbs/flows, jumps/bumps within the Accuracy TIMING windows without fear for absolute amazing returns over TIME…even if you miss out on the PRECISE moment of TIME to enter the security.  Get it?  Do you really get it?  Think hard on this one next TIME you want to type a “Is it too late to buy/sell” comment to someone.  Develop the confidence yourself.  Learn how to read and listen to markets…..to successfully TIME them.  In my observation, I’ve found four types of investors. OK, let's get started. This is an important concept.....because if we aren't correctly reading the real estate/banking crisis cycle, then our results are going to suffer.

So how do we TIME markets correctly? It's a matter of precision versus accuracy. One is better than the other. So definitions please!

Precision is how close the measurements are to each other.
Accuracy is how close a given set of measurements are to their true value.

Before we geek out, "we want to talk right down to earth in a language that everybody here can easily understand." (Malcolm X). And I think that is best done with graphics. After all “Every picture tells a story, don’t it” (Rod Stewart).

Let’s think of the dart board as a measure of our success in TIMING MARKETS. Bulls-eye means we TIMED it perfectly! Our darts - are investment decision darts – each one thrown with the aim of getting close to the target’s bulls-eye. Look at the attached figures below. title: Knowledge Transfer Series #74 - It's All Relative
caption:
Bookmark it!

 Another 100 followers....another KTS post.  #74 today.....and only 64 more followers needed for the glorious KTS #75 (for those who have read the Knowledge Transfer Series in totality....in the spirit of respect for Wall Street Price Levels (KTS #21), we make sure KTS #25, #50, #75, and #100 are especially meaty!  As in smoked for 14 hours slathered with sweet and thick BBQ sauce.  I'm drooling on my shirt right now thinking about it! 🐖 😋

But today?  It's a follow up to the Class Exercise Poll from 2 days ago.  The question was what percentage of your portfolio holdings are at/near their 52-weak high?  You can view the results through this link:

https://www.blossomsocial.com/posts/POST-1750247411733-as0nW9iy_qoQV3QbaHcPIAvML

But why this poll, Beskar? 😂  Because we just taxied the runway and are cleared for takeoff! ✈️ The first half year of a new administration is in and the wolves have finally figured out what to expect........and most importantly......who is going to 🏆! We now enter the FOMO momentum stage - which lasts for many, many months at least.  Sure the crosscurrents will persist......and continue to grow (did you know that while the US unemployment rate continues to stay low ~4%........but the new college grads unemployment rate continues to climb......now at 7.8%?).  But we are in a momentum market now.....and we don't care much about that right now.......it doesn't fit what we want to hear! 😂 🤣  

Instead, we go to the playbook 📖 I. shared this upfront with you in Knowledge Transfer Series #2 - Follow the $ector! 🤔 Maybe worth a re-read.  DEFINITELY worth a re-read.....NOW!  🤑

 Why?  Because it's all relative.  This market will see indicies start making new ALL-TIME highs.  And the Conventionalists will feel smug in their ways. 😊 But for UNconventional and individual stock pickers?!?!?!  Oh shoot.....get ready!  If you are under the age of 35......welcome to something you've never experienced before!  These markets are 4-5x in a lifetime.....so enjoy it.  And it will last much longer than you think!  🤑 🪙 💲 💰

 So let's get started by reviewing the poll results.  Looks like 43% of us have AT LEAST 40% of their portfolio's holdings in stocks at/near 52-wk highs. 👍 THAT'S PRETTY AWESOME positioning compared to most retail INVESTORS (as defined by KTS #11). Also, you may have to recalculate your poll question.........AT is literally the highest CLOSING PRICE (KTS #24) over the last 365 days. I failed to stipulate that NEAR the 52-week high is within ~5% for Large Caps and ~10% for Small Caps. So maybe redo the math there. 😂

 Also, would love for anyone with an at least 40% of their portfolio holdings to type a comment below with their "All-TIME return" on their Blossom home page.  I know, I know....there's all kinds of issues with how Blossom calculates this.  But there's a relative error compared to others.  So just post what you got if you are in that cohort, ok? 😀

But let's get back to why are we looking at this? Isn't it Buy Low and Sell High, Beskar? Yes,........yes it is!  But learn right now.......that in this kind of market, you have absolutely no frickin' clue what a high price even is. 😂  If you are in the RIGHT STOCKS in this RIGHT TIME, you have zero ability to share price targets of any kind. This is a FOMO momentum market that will run past any "reasonable" valuations that you compute........AS LONG AS YOU ARE IN THE RIGHT STOCKS AT THE RIGHT TIME!

I think I hear what you're saying, Besk! So buy low, sell high is still valid.......it's just you don't think I can identify what's "high"? 😂 👍  That's exactly it - you got it!  So let the talking heads run the P/Es, Book-to-Price ratios, and other valuation metrics 🤪.  There's a TIME for that......but not now......IF YOU ARE IN THE RIGHT STOCKS AT THE RIGHT TIME. Let them try and squash expectations of the secular trends.  They are way too powerful and - at this stage of the meltup - there are way too many buyers and not enough sellers for these stocks to go nowhere or fall off a cliff!  
Ok, ok.  I know what you're saying now.  And I love watching how you pick stocks, Beskar.  Sometimes I don't know where you find these gems!?!?!?  So why not show us how to find them if you are really trying to help us, huh?

Of course!......all you have to do is ask!  

So you already know from KTS #2, that following the Sector accounts for half your gains.....and finding winning stocks within those winning sectors gives you the other half......outperforming all of your peers, indicies, and ETFs!  But what if we go one step further?  🤔 Let's now go down to the Industry level.  That's right - a place where cohorts of stocks reside in around 50 different category classifications. 👍

I made a spreadsheet for you attached in the 3 images below.

Let's look at it together. 😃 The table lists each industry, the industry's recent price strength, the number of stocks within that industry, the number of that particular industry's stocks that are AT/NEAR their 52-wk high, and what percentage of the industry is AT/NEAR it's 52-week high.

Got it?  Good. 😂  Also, I included 4 principle indicies. 

So what's the insight, Besk?  Geez......I always give you tasty meat to chew on, don't I? 😂 🤣 🐖 😋

First, I sorted the last column from high to low.  I've learned that when you have more than 20% of your industry hitting 52-wk highs, that's A LOCK for a place to be searching for the RIGHT STOCKS AT THE RIGHT TIME.  So I highlighted those in GREEN for you.  The next tier is YELLOW - reserved for industries that are in that 10%-20% of their stocks making new highs.  This is a great place to look too!  Next, the ORANGE highlights those industries in a meaningful tier of 5%-10%. RED tier is for anything above 0% but less than 5%.

WOW!  I mean......WOW!  That's right.....you get it!  It's FOLLOW THE MONEY.....WHICH IS FOLLOW THE SECTOR.....WHICH IS FOLLOW THE INDUSTRY.....WHICH IS FOLLOW THE RIGHT STOCKS AT THE RIGHT TIME!  Maybe re-read KTS #2....AGAIN! 😂 When MAG 7 was in it's prime.....how do you think these industry categories were shuffled back then?  🤔

So what other insight can we glean from this Class Exercise of 52-wk highs?  There is SO MUCH insight.......I did the work for you. 👍  And I'll list some nuggets of insight here, but why not get to work and start some analysis yourself..........so you can learn to read and listen to markets to successfully TIME them......YOU can do it! 😀 👍

Here's some of what I see:

- Tobacco, Metals & Mining, and Aerospace & Defense are places to look for stock candidates NOW!

- Industries in the YELLOW and ORANGE tiers THAT HAVE A STRONG Price Strength (2nd Column) are up and coming great places to find stock candidates as well!  If it's weak.......maybe the Industry is starting to plateau or the level of price gains is growing at a slower rate.  TIRED!

- Commodity industries are all over these top three tiers.....but we knew that!

- Banks have fallen - still robust - but not what it was.  Insurance too.  That was the strongest industry 6 months ago.

- Sin stocks starting to get money flow from whales?  Makes sense.  When this shit falls apart, drinking and smoking will be en vogue again! 🍺 🍻 🚬

- Entertainment doing well?  Sure.....last hurahh.  Concert tickets are $200!?!??!  Let's keep juicing til the money runs out! 😂

- Utilities, Electrical Equipment, and Oil & Gas on the up and up.  I guess that's what the AI boom is spending their continuous capital outlays on!

-Software?  Continues to just climb.  AI is the way.......at least for now.

-Semis?  Are they showing signs of life......or death?

-Capital markets.....that makes sense with the stock market euphoria!

-How about the indicies?  RELATIVELY, looks like the Dow Jones and the Nasdaq 100 are yellow tier while S&P500 and Russell 3000 are ORANGE tier.  Both tiers are good......but trends are weak.  So I think I'd be picking stocks with STRONG price strength and trends instead.

So you get it. This is a momentum market where buying high and selling higher later after the FOMO comes roaring in.  This is yet another beautiful KTS tool to check in with to know where you are in the economic cycle.  You can read and listen to the market to TIME it successfully.  So make like a barnacle and attach to a whale! 🐚 to 🐋

I always give you my best.

This is the Way! 🏄‍♀️ 🌊 🏄‍♂️ 🌊

title: Only 4% More Alberta!
caption:
Carney Faces Crisis as Alberta Oil Anger Boils Over

Canada’s new Prime Minister Mark Carney faces his first real challenge to Canadian unity amid a soured relationship with the long-time trade partner and ally, the United States.

A movement to secede from Canada is growing in the oil-rich province of Alberta, which contributes a large part to Canadian economy and budget and has the highest gross domestic product (GDP) per capita among Canadian provinces. 

Alberta could hold a referendum to leave Canada next year if a citizen-led petition gathers the requisite number of signatures requesting such a question to be put to a referendum.

Danielle Smith, the Premier of the Alberta province, said last month “I do not support Alberta separating from Canada. I personally still have hope that there is a path forward for a strong and sovereign Alberta within a United Canada.”

“To be clear from the outset, our government will not be putting a vote on separation from Canada on the referendum ballot; however, if there is a successful citizen-led referendum petition that is able to gather the requisite number of signatures requesting such a question to be put to a referendum, our government will respect the democratic process and include that question on the 2026 provincial referendum ballot as well,” Smith said.

For years, Alberta has been opposing federal legislation regarding emissions and resource development. Alberta has been fighting the federal government on the plan to cap emissions from oil and gas production, which the province and the industry see essentially as a cap on output.

For years, Alberta has been trying to have more pipeline options to export its huge oil and gas resources, including to markets other than the United States.

With the U.S. under President Trump, Carney’s federal government has started to realize that diversification of oil exports – 90% of which go to the U.S. – could be a smart move.

During a meeting with oil and gas executives in Canada’s oil-producing province of Alberta, Carney pledged earlier this month that the federal government would work to fast-track major projects to make Canada an energy superpower.

Carney said he discussed with Smith “what matters most: building one united Canadian economy — including getting big things built and major infrastructure projects off the ground, in Alberta and across Canada.”

Smith, for her part, said that Alberta needs a guaranteed corridor and port access to tidewater off the Pacific, Arctic, and Atlantic coasts for the international export of Alberta resources. The oil-rich province also needs an approval of an oil pipeline to the B.C. Northwest coast, repealing the Tanker Ban to enable exports from the Port of Prince Rupert, and returning the regulation of industrial emissions to the provinces, among other things, Alberta’s premier added.

Alberta is working to engage private backers for a new pipeline to ship about 1 million barrels per day (bpd) of crude from Canada’s oil-producing province to British Columbia on the West Coast, Premier Smith said last week. The pipeline would run from the oil sands in Alberta to the Port of Prince Rupert on British Columbia’s northwest coast, and to international markets afterwards, according to the plans of the province.

“There has to be a real demonstration, and very soon, that some of the priorities of Alberta are going to be addressed,” Smith told The Wall Street Journal in an interview last week.

Albertans are dissatisfied with the way the province has been treated by the federal government for years and the sentiment to secede is not just a fringe movement.

“They are quite literally our friends and neighbours who have just had enough of having their livelihoods and prosperity attacked by a hostile federal government,” Premier Smith said last month.  

In May, a poll by Ledger found that 47% of Albertans are in favor of the province becoming an independent country, while 48% are opposed.

Analysts say that the federal government addressing some or most of Alberta’s grievances, including increased access for oil and gas to international markets, could sway potential “leave” voters to want to remain part of a united Canada.  

Are you going to sign the citizen-led petition?

title: The Green Scam Unwind Continues
caption:
https://newsroom.aaa.com/2025/06/aaa-ev-survey/

ORLANDO, Fla. (June 3, 2025) – Despite the wide variety of electric vehicle (EV) models now available—over 75 options introduced in the past four years—AAA’s latest survey highlights buyers’ continued hesitation. Only 16% of U.S. adults report being “very likely” or “likely” to purchase a fully electric vehicle (EV) as their next car, the lowest percentage recorded of EV interest since 2019. The percentage of consumers indicating they would be “unlikely” or “very unlikely” to purchase an EV rose from 51% to 63%, the highest since 2022.

“Since we began tracking interest in fully electric vehicles, we’ve seen some variability,” said Greg Brannon, director of automotive engineering. “While the automotive industry is committed to long-term electrification and providing a diverse range of models, underlying consumer hesitation remains.”


Key Barriers to Go Fully Electric

High battery repair costs (62%) and purchase price (59%) are cited as key barriers to go fully electric. Other top concerns identified in this year’s survey were the perceived unsuitability of EVs for long-distance travel (57%), a lack of convenient public charging stations (56%), and fear of running out of charge while driving (55%). Thirty-one percent of those undecided or unlikely to buy an EV have safety concerns, 27 percent reported challenges installing charging stations at their residences, and 12% cited the potential reduction or elimination of tax credits and rebates.  According to AAA’s 2024 Your Driving Cost analysis, EVs had the second-highest total ownership costs due to depreciation, purchase prices, and finance charges.

Future of EVs

Despite advancements in the EV industry and the growing availability of models, public perception regarding the future of EVs remains uncertain. The percentage of U.S. drivers who believe that most cars will be electric within the next ten years has significantly declined from 40% in 2022 to 23% this year. Interest in EVs to take advantage of tax credits and rebates has decreased, dropping from 60% of those saying last year they are likely to buy an EV to 39% this year.

AAA’s Recommendations

Many consumers may find hybrid or plug-in hybrid vehicles more appealing. These vehicles combine the advantages of traditional internal combustion engines with electric power, reducing range anxiety while providing an environmentally friendly alternative. This combination meets today’s diverse needs and preferences for drivers, allowing them to enjoy electric driving without losing the familiarity of conventional gas-powered vehicles.

AAA will continue to monitor the evolving automotive landscape through consumer sentiment surveys and various research efforts to address concerns about EV ownership and provide feedback and insights for all vehicle types.

Hope is not a plan.

title: Today's News
caption:
https://floridianpress.com/2025/05/desantis-approves-law-allowing-gold-silver-as-legal-tender/

“These precious metals can start functioning like real currency again," he said. "It’ll be something that you’re going to be able to use. And it’s something that is very likely to hold its value."

Under the new law, gold and silver coins will be exempted from sales tax and check cashers like PayPal could transmit and accept these payments.

The United States used the gold standard until 1971, when President Richard Nixon ended the dollar's convertibility to gold. Recently, DeSantis said, the dollar's rising inflation rate has made it difficult for some to purchase homes and pay down debts.

The cumulative rate of inflation since Nixon's decree is nearly 700%. This means a $1 item in 1971 would be the equivalent of a $7.92 item today.



"The DeSantis-backed measure, HB 999, recognizes gold and silver coins as "legal tender" to pay down debts incurred after July 1, 2026."

That's a curious date.

title: Accumulating EMX Royalty
Accumulating Integra Resources
Who Needs Who More? 🇺🇸 vs. 🇨🇳
caption:
As it turns out, a trade war with your biggest customer isn’t the death knell some made it out to be. U.S. exports of natural gas liquids (NGLs) hit an all-time high in April, defying concerns that the escalating trade war with China would choke off demand. Ship tracking data from Kpler and Vortexa pegged NGL exports at 2.9 million barrels per day (bpd)—a record level—even as exports to China tumbled 35% month-over-month.

NGLs like ethane, propane, and butane—key inputs for everything from plastics to home heating—have long relied on China— as a top buyer. Nearly half of U.S. ethane exports typically head there, and until recently, China sourced 100% of its ethane from the U.S. But tensions boiled over this spring, and Beijing slapped a 125% tariff on U.S. ethane.

That move didn’t last long.

With no other real supply options, China quietly waived the tariff. No formal announcements, just the international equivalent of “nevermind.” Yet the doom forecasts haven’t materialized for nat gas. As China pulled back, other nations stepped up.

India more than tripled its NGL imports from the U.S. to a record 179,000 bpd. Brazil more than doubled its purchases to 113,000 bpd—the highest in five years. Japan, the second-largest buyer after China, boosted shipments by 64% to nearly 400,000 bpd, the highest since February 2023.

Producers say it’s business as usual. Enterprise Products Partners, one of the largest U.S. NGL exporters, reported no disruption to ethane, propane, or butane exports. Rival Energy Transfer echoed that sentiment, noting no trouble finding buyers. Both companies logged increases in NGL transport and export volumes in Q1.

The Energy Information Administration expects U.S. ethane production to rise 3.6% this year to 2.9 million bpd, with most of that growth headed straight to foreign markets. For now, the market has done what geopolitics couldn’t—kept U.S. energy exports booming. Because when it comes to gas liquids, nobody cares about the trade drama—they just want the product.

https://oilprice.com/Latest-Energy-News/World-News/US-Nat-Gas-Liquids-Exports-Hit-RecordEven-With-China-Playing-Hardball.html

Pain is RELATIVE. 😂

This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️ 🌊

title: The Carney 🤡 is Coming to Town
caption:
President Trump is trying to break us so that America can own us,” declared Mark Carney, Canada’s new prime minister, in his victory speech last week. “That will never, ever happen,” he pledged. Anti-Trump rhetoric may have scored Mr Carney points on the campaign trail. But it will now make his job that much harder as he visits the White House on Tuesday to meet the man he accuses of “betrayal”.

Mr Carney must persuade America—which buys three-quarters of Canadian exports—to cut his country some slack on tariffs. Canada’s economy is looking fragile. In February GDP shrank; productivity looks weak. Trade figures for March, released on Tuesday, are expected to show a widening deficit. In February, exports fell by more than 5%—including steep drops in cars, lumber and steel. Mr Carney wants to diversify to Asia and Europe, but re-routing oil and other goods is costly. Still, as America’s biggest customer, a major energy supplier and a potential source of critical minerals, Canada has leverage. Mr Carney needs every bit of it.

caption:
Alberta Premier Danielle Smith says she would hold a referendum on provincial separation next year if citizens gather the required signatures on a petition.

The current minimum standard of signatures is 10 per cent of voters in the last election – representing 177,000 names – with four months to gather the petition.

Smith says Alberta has no choice but to take steps to combat a decade of hostile federal Liberal policies and laws she says have not only taken an unfair share of Alberta’s wealth but in doing so have also undermined the oil and gas industry that drives its economy.

title: Kentucky Derby Day
caption:
Happy 151st Kentucky Derby Day, Blossomers!

@beskar_capital loves the 2nd Saturday in May and the fastest 2 minutes in sports!

I have been handicapping the race every year since 1990. In the spirit of openly sharing my picks and rationale.....I will share here 3 hours before the race! 🐎

Here's a mint julep toast to all!

Here's my thoughts on horses in contention....and then my picks in the end:

#3 Final Gambit - Deep closer that has been improving with each race. Can run in the mud too with late speed! That's what we like for the longest race any of these horses have ever run.

#5 American Promise - He better get out early. If he does, he can sprint to the end if he doesn't die in the extended length. If he doesn't, it's over early for him.

#7 Luxor Cafe - Force to be reckoned with! Japanese 🇯🇵 horse bred in Kentucky that should come in the end. It's only a matter of TIME before a Japanese horse wins the Derby. It could be today. Japanese trainers are much better than American trainers....and it will start showing soon. He's got speed and knows how to win! Only concern is he's never run here and this is a big class of talent.

#8 Journalism - No reason to think he won't win it. He stalks and closes with the best and he runs get in mud. He's been rising and getting better with each race. What else do you need?

#9 - Burnham Square - another mudder that can stalk. Has late speed with great pedigree with Unbridled Song.

#13 - Publisher - Secret pick with a great jockey and amazing pedigree. Can do it all under the radar. But always finishes 2nd....

#14 - Tiztastic - Another secret pick. Can stalk in mud....and is continously improving. Outside shot....but can definitely win it!

CALIFORNIA DREAMING
California’s 2035 gas car ban just got torched by the U.S. House, which voted 246–164 on Thursday to kill the EPA waiver that let the Golden State set stricter tailpipe rules than the federal government. The decision doesn’t just mess with California—it upends the dozen other blue states that hitched their EV ambitions to Sacramento’s regulatory wagon. Now the Senate will get a turn at the wheel, but it’s already swerving around a ruling from the Senate parliamentarian that says this repeal isn’t even allowed.

For years, California has acted like it’s the de facto EPA. Under Newsom’s zero-emission-or-bust vision, gas cars were set to become fossils by 2035.

CANADA INHERITING FAILED EU POLICY WITH ELECTION OF EU LEFTOVER LEADERSHIP
“This top-down imposition of enforced “climate policies” is justified as necessary to save the world from climate change,” Wright said.

“But I can say that climate alarmism has clearly reduced energy freedom, and, hence, prosperity and national security across Western Europe.”

Europe’s net-zero targets and rush to bet on renewable energy in the wake of the 2022 energy crisis have backfired, with energy costs for industry four to five times higher than in the United States, which can count on cheap and abundant domestic natural gas.

Deindustrialization in Europe has been taking place since the energy price spike three years ago, eroding what little competitive advantage it had over Chinese and U.S. manufacturing.


WALKING AWAY FROM US WIND FARM PROJECTS
Germany’s RWE, one of the world’s top offshore wind developers, has stopped work on its US projects for now in light of recent moves against the industry by the Trump administration, its CEO said in a text published ahead of the firm’s annual meeting.

As reported by Reuters, the comments by Markus Krebber are a “heavy blow” to the nascent US offshore wind market, which was a key pillar of former US President Joe Biden’s energy policy but which his successor Donald Trump has vowed to stop.

Krebber’s remarks come a week after Norwegian peer Equinor said it would halt offshore construction of its Empire Wind I project off the coast of New York because it received a stop-work order from US Interior Secretary Doug Burgum. At that time, the DOI said that information suggested that the project had been approved without a proper environmental analysis.

Krebber was quoted to say: “In the US ... we have stopped our offshore activities for the time being.” RWE’s annual general meeting is scheduled for April 30. “We remain cautious given the political developments,” Reuters quoted him as saying.

title: VOTE! Because Liberal Policy Could Kill Canada
caption:
Learn the future, Canada. Today, we record the country's collective IQ report card.

Think about this as you think about your children's standard of living.

California’s high gas prices stem from policy, not profiteering.

California serves as a case study in how policy can trump market fundamentals. The state has intentionally made it more expensive to produce and consume gasoline, and it’s seeing the predictable result: higher prices, shrinking supply, and reduced private investment.

A Perfect Storm of Policy, Regulation, and Infrastructure

According to the study, there are seven core drivers behind California’s persistently high gas prices. None of them are accidental, and none are easily fixed.

Environmental Regulations

California mandates a specialized gasoline blend known as CARBOB (California Reformulated Gasoline Blendstock for Oxygenate Blending), along with seasonal reformulations and a stringent Cap-and-Trade program. These collectively add $0.47 to $1.15 per gallon in compliance costs—costs not borne by drivers in other states.

High Taxes and Fees

California levies the highest gasoline taxes and regulatory fees in the nation—around $1.64 per gallon. By comparison, refiners typically make far less per gallon, often just pennies. When drivers look at the pump price, they often miss how much of it is policy-driven rather than market-driven.

Declining In-State Oil Production

Once a top oil-producing state, California now supplies only about 23% of its own crude needs. More than 60% of its crude is imported—much of it from politically unstable or distant countries. That reliance adds transportation costs and makes the state vulnerable to global price swings.

Refinery Closures

California has lost significant refining capacity over the past decade, with more to come. Phillips 66 and other operators are shuttering refineries, with California expected to lose another 8.6% of its capacity—tightening supply even further.

No Inbound Pipelines

Unlike Texas or Louisiana, California has no crude oil pipelines crossing its borders. That forces the state to rely almost entirely on marine shipments and a limited number of rail routes—both of which are more expensive and subject to disruption.

Business Climate

The state’s regulatory and political environment is viewed as unfriendly to the oil and gas industry. Since 2018, more than 360 energy-related companies have left California altogether. That includes major oil producers, pipeline operators, and support services—many of which have cited excessive red tape and regulatory hostility as key reasons for exiting.

California’s Long-Term Policy Goals

Finally, the state’s aggressive push to ban the sale of internal combustion engine vehicles by 2035 creates a chilling effect on long-term investment in fuel infrastructure. Why would companies invest billions in refining, storage, and distribution assets in a state that has openly stated its goal of making those assets obsolete?

The Ripple Effect Beyond California

This isn’t just a California problem. Neighboring states like Nevada and Arizona are highly dependent on California’s refineries—88% and 48%, respectively. So, when prices rise in California, the pain spreads across the Southwest. And if California loses more refining capacity, it’s not just residents of Los Angeles or San Francisco who will feel it.

No Evidence of Price Gouging

One of the more interesting takeaways from Mische’s report is what’s not driving up prices: corporate profiteering. Multiple investigations—including those by the FTC, the Federal Reserve, and even the state of California itself—have found no clear evidence of price manipulation by oil companies.

https://oilprice.com/Energy/Gas-Prices/Study-California-Gas-Prices-Driven-by-Policy-Not-Profiteering.html

Largely due to high construction and pipeline costs, Canada’s once-lofty ambitions to become a major player in the global LNG market have faded. As just one example, Spanish energy firm Repsol abandoned plans last March for an LNG export project on Canada’s east coast, citing the high cost of shipping gas from western Canada. Several projects are still looking for financing, including Cedar LNG and Ksi Lisims LNG and FortisBC still hopes to expand its Tilbury LNG facility outside of Vancouver. Of roughly 20 LNG projects once proposed in Canada, most have now been shelved.

A recent article by BOE Report notes that Canada and the United States began proposing LNG export facilities at roughly the same time in the early 2010’s. “In typical ‘let’s not wait around fashion’, the United States seized the moment and has become the world’s largest LNG exporter in the world since then. Canada has exported zero. Nothing.”

From 2010 to 2021, the US grew their natural gas exports by 485 percent, while Canada’s exports declined by 18 percent.

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #70 - Volume & OBV
caption:
The world of finance exists in three dimensions: price - Volume - TIME
We already know which one @beskar_capital believes is the most underappreciated and most lucrative dimension - TIME. So much so that I'm always capitalizing the word........to show my respect for it. There have been many KTS posts in the past about TIME (KTS #4, 10, 10A, 15, 22, 23, 24, 38, 39, 40, 45, 48, 66, 67, etc.)......... and there will be many posts in the future on TIME and how to harness it for profits. The wolves only acknowledge TIME in the slightest........when they HAVE to. For them, it's a game to constantly seek the holy grail of instant gratification in the moment.......constantly. Instead, it's an obsession with price movements. After all, that's how you capture profit......buy low, sell high, right?. Move on to the next carcass for more food.......as if TIME has no beneficial role. The more price fluctuates, the more meat. An absolute obsession. How do you calculate returns afterall? (Selling Price - Buying Price) / Buying Price = Percent Return. Nothing else matters.
But in this post, we are going to explore the also neglected 3rd dimension of the ticker tape: Volume. Like a typical middle child, Volume avoids conflict and serves the role of being called on when asked ....... and delivers incredible insight (in case you were wondering....Beskar is the youngest of 4 but spaced five years from the next closest brother :)).
Let's consider Volume's role in studying financial markets:
-Price can fluctuate wildly on high Volume, right?
-Price can also fluctuate wildly on low Volume, right?
-Without consideration of Volume, you can't decipher if conviction in the price movement is widespread across several investors.......or isolated to one investor, right?
- Key Beskar Buy/Sell/Hold Indicators: Accumulation/Distribution calculation (KTS #49)? Need Volume. Money Flow Indicies (KTS #25)? Need Volume. Unusual Options Activity (KTS #53)? Need Volume.
But the stealthy application of Volume in studying financial markets and the ticker tape - that most all overlook - is in finding new investment ideas when the Volume significantly changes. Especially for cyclical stocks that can see investors flood in and flood out. For those that have been following me, you are fully aware that all investment decisions by Beskar Capital are made within the context of the current phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle. And if you've read any of my posts, you are fully aware of our current heightened interest into commodities over the past year and a gradual transition away from Technology over TIME. A growth to value new world order is taking place in a choppy, but apparent (if you step back and look at the longer-term charts ). A Mag 7 .....to Lag 7 movement. Yes, it's choppy......a process taking place after 15 years of a widespread growth bull market label......to now an embracement of sectors/subsectors (old industrials) and markets (Japan) that had been left for dead (who are witnessing above average growth again). A shift from large to small & mid in an increasingly less liquid environment. A collective - yet reluctant - acknowledgement that dividends - are indeed part of the equation in a slower growing environment.
And what financial dimension gives us the most early insight on the magnitude of this paradigm? Volume.
So let's unearth (you like how I used that word here? ) how Volume can be called on at this moment to help us profit!
From past posts, we anticipated that the first 6 months of a new presidency with new policies historically results in a volatile 6 months as Wall Street begins sorting out the winners and losers. Per script, we are watching the daily EKG of the market creating steep and pointy inflection points. This new presidency and the proposed policy with immediate action is creating a volatility on steroids!!! But, we take a deep breath, we step back and study the ticker tape........to learn the future. While everyone is focused on the noise above water with the wind, the unpredictable storms, the rough waves crashing, ......... not a peaceful day at the beach, eh? TRUMP wants Powell fired. Libs keying Teslas. Tariffs are a tax! Debts are out of control. Bond market rates are sharply increasing. Government needs to shrink. Where are the rate cuts? tax cuts? Eggs!?!??!?
Instead, we put on our scuba gear and go look at the underworld.......peaceful, melodic, slower moving.........discovering beautiful coral reefs, new and unique species, and understand what's really going on below the surface. 78% of the daily trading Volume are institutions after all (KTS #11)......so it makes sense......to pay attention.....to stay below the water and watch the whales in their natural habitat. What are they eating? AND how MUCH are they eating? If we can answer these two questions .......... now..........we are going to profit tremendously over TIME! So let the sharks move from kill to kill attempting to satisfy their insatiable hunger demand.....while we safely make like a barnacle and attach to a whale!
Alright, Beskar........I got your corny analogy......can you just get on with it?!?!?!?
Right. Sorry about that......having too much fun......the Old Man and the Sea.
So how can we answer these two questions?:
(1) What are the whales eating?
(2) How much are the whales eating?
Volume.
Here's the tool. Volume & OBV. First, OBV - On Balance Volume. A simple cumulative metric that is a running balance of Volume. Joe Granville observed that OBV precedes price changes and is credited for this TIMING "discovery" in the early 1960's. TIME to dig in. Let's say a stock closes at a higher price for the day on a total volume of 100,000 shares. The OBV is recorded as a POSITIVE +100,000 shares. Now, let's say the next day, 80,000 shares are traded but the stock closes lower. Those 80,000 shares are recorded as a NEGATIVE -80,000 shares and added to the previous day's OBV, which was +100,000 shares. So the running OBV, following today's close down, is now +20,000. You get it. So if a stock is up, down, up, down, up, down with a steady average Volume....the stock isn't going anywhere......and neither is the stock.
But what if we see the OBV trending higher? So every day, let's say the stock closes higher on higher Volumes. Maybe down days have lower than average Volumes. The net effect is an OBV that is TRENDING HIGHER. And this is really the gem in this indicator........DIRECTION, NOT MAGNITUDE. Read the previous sentence again and brand it into your forehead.....like Jelly Roll!
In conjunction with OBV, stepping back and looking at total Volume charts over select TIME periods that coincide with key historical events........can get you the early glimpse to your answers about the whales!
So why now, Beskar? Again, when major market reallocations take place (like now), you will see periods of elevated volatility? Why? Well because 78% of the daily trading Volume is run by the institutions.....which means......when sector/subsector favorability shifts....they need to take massive Volumes of shares OUT from the old......and IN to the new investments. And they can't be seen as doing it or the market shocks will be so abrupt and disruptive......it could cause widespread panic and collapse. So......these whales have to slowly.......on a daily basis.......to keep selling prices HIGH for their shares in old sectors........in order to maximize profits there........and redeploy........slowly.......to go undetected.......as a way to keep entry prices low.........in a systematic, methodical, stealthy way........to keep their cost basis as low as possible in their NEW INVESTMENT......by slowing scaling in over TIME. Get it?
So guess what exposes all of this whale feeding activity? Volume & OBV.
So let me take you through a stock screener exercise that I did this past Friday after the close. Most ALL trading platforms can run this screen/filter:
(1) Filter ALL stocks in the UNIVERSE that have an OBV result "Trending Higher". (Remember: With OBV, TREND is everything, MAGNITUDE is not critical).
(2) Then, from that set of stocks, filter all stocks that have seen a positive return greater than 5% since American Liberation Day, April 2nd, 2025. (As you know, the markets - in general - have had a rough go since all the noise kicked into high gear on 4/2/2025. But a good question to pursue......what stocks have the whales been eating instead......that are actually UP significantly instead of going down with the indicies? This is why we like individual stocks and not diversified ETFs. The RIGHT focused ETFs in a market like this where the wolves are figuring winners and losers are fine......but individual stock selection can really juice the returns).
Ok, I can do that, Beskar. So what were the results?
So, out of the 12,188 stocks in the entire universe.......only 626 stocks passed these two simple criteria. Talk about a unique opportunity to see the whales feeding in their natural habitat!
So let's break it down further by sector to get more insight from this OBV trend insight!:
626 stocks are being increasingly accumulated with EARLY significant returns since American Liberation Day (~last month).
42 in Consumer Services (6.7%)
48 in Consumer Discretionary (7.7%)
55 in Consumer Staples (8.8%)
14 in Energy (2.2%)
51 in Financials (8.1%)
79 in Healthcare (12.6%)
72 in Industrials ( 11.6%)
50 in Information Technology (8.0%)
176 in Materials (28.1%)
8 in Real Estate (1.3%)
31 in Utilities (5.0%)
Does anything stand out here in the list above? Of course, Materials.....at 28.1%. Breaking it down further into SUBsector, we learn that 165 of the 176 stocks in the Materials sector that met the OBV/early return criteria since ALD (American Liberation Day).......are Metals & Mining stocks. Continuing on the early INSIDER (as defined by KTS #11) buying that started in February 2024.....in a more profound way. This is supportive data to help you identify the phase within the real estate/banking crisis cycle. Keep in mind that some sectors have far less stocks. For example, Technology, Financials, and Healthcare.........have WWWWWAAAAAYYYYYY more stocks in the universe than the others. So when you see in absolute terms.....a paltry ~50 in Tech and Financials.....compared to 165 metals and mining stocks out of a relatively "dinky" Materials sector!?!??!?!? Learn the way.
So what are those stocks? I've been sharing those posts leading up to this. All kinds. As Dr. Seuss would point out. Big dogs. Little dogs. Green dogs. Blue dogs. Tall dogs. Small dogs. Spotted dogs. Striped dogs.
Do it yourself and find out!
I will leave you will some examples in the Metals & Mining subsector to get you started......and a list of others that are similar to dig in on to confirm you are looking into it correctly.
See the charts below. Equinox for example.......since it became a publicly traded stock in 2013......it's NEVER seen the Volume that it has since American Liberation Day. And all that GREEN volume below the price chart shows that the OBV is STRONGLY TRENDING HIGHER....almost daily. Last, until this past month......the stock hasn't spent significant TIME above the long-term 200-day trend since 2000!?!?!?!? Do you think you can read this crystal ball yet???

title: Kinder Morgan Reports
caption:
From KTS #28.

"We estimate that growth to be somewhere around 16 Bcf a day with the great bulk of that coming from facilities already under construction or that have been FID. To FID a project means it is supported by long-term contracts with creditworthy entities. Otherwise, these facilities simply could not be financed. I might add parenthetically that our contracts with LNG export facilities are also supported by long-term contracts. But notwithstanding those facts, the naysayers argue that a trade war with China will lead to a diminution in need for U.S. LNG. In response, let me mention 2 countervailing factors. First, China has not imported any U.S. LNG since February and yet feedgas demand is setting records, averaging 15.5 Bcf per day in the first quarter and approaching 17 Bcf a day on several recent days. Secondly, our view is that any loss of the Chinese market will be more than offset by the efforts of governments in the EU and Asia to increase the imports of U.S. LNG to reduce trade imbalances and put themselves in a better negotiating position with regard to U.S. tariffs. South Korea and Indonesia, for example, are already in specific discussions on that strategy. And regarding the EU, we believe Europe's apparent and off-stated reluctance to ever again allow Russia to be the dominant supplier of natural gas to Europe will obviously increase its use of U.S. LNG. Based on all these factors, we remain very bullish on growth in U.S. LNG exports. Now in addition to growth in LNG feedgas demand, we see nice upticks in exports to Mexico power demand driven in part by the surge in AI and data centers and residential commercial use. Anecdotally, the new projects we have announced at Kinder Morgan over the last couple of quarters are supported in large part by long-term contracts with utilities in the Southeastern U.S., an indication of the need for more gas to feed electric generation. The point of all this detail is to put into perspective the drivers of demand for the product we transport so that any intelligent investor can make a reasoned assessment of the natural gas market for the rest of this decade and not be whipsawed by the perceived ups and downs of just 1 or 2 facets of the growth story. In truth, that growth is supported, we believe, by an array of factors that reflect the strength of natural gas as an important source of energy for years to come. And with that, I'll turn it over to Kim."

Fresh off the press!

You either listen and learn......or project your views. Darwin's Natural Selection is alive and well! 😂 😃 😄 😁 🤣

This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️ 🌊

title: TRUMP is Single-Handedly Changing Global Trade
caption:
These are moments in TIME when secular trends develop that can make you a fortune. Like automobiles in the 1920's. Internet stocks in the 1990's. On and on.

Two Steps.

(1) You just have to see it......and then,

(2) You have to act on it.

TRUMP is giving you a true gift. He tells you exactly where the puck is going.....and takes fast action on it. A politician like no other. And an investors dream. It doesn't get easier than this. While you overly emotional liberals reel from these fast-moving actions on idealogy, the rest of us will simply follow this 2-step process above and profit off it all. 😂 🤣

Let's get an update:

Thailand is the latest Asian economy looking to import higher volumes of American energy as a way to convince the U.S. Administration not to slap high tariffs on Thai goods sold in the United States.

Thailand plans to raise its U.S. LNG imports over the next five years, Finance Minister Pichai Chunhavajira said on Wednesday.

Thailand will also seek another LNG deal over the next five years, worth $600 million, as well as importing ethane from the U.S., worth about $100 million over the next four years, according to the energy minister.

Earlier this week, Indonesia, threatened with a 32% tariff, said it would offer to buy an additional $10 billion worth of American oil and liquefied petroleum gas (LPG).

Indonesia is just one of the countries looking to buy their way out of steep tariffs with deals to purchase American energy products.

South Asian nation Pakistan is actively considering the idea of importing U.S. crude oil for the first time to seek a reduction of its trade surplus with America.

South Korea is reportedly looking at more LNG imports to get Washington to drop the tariffs, while India is weighing the option to scrap its import tax on American liquefied natural gas to increase U.S. LNG imports and reduce its trade surplus with the United States.

title: They are starting to figure it out....
caption:
Do you know who controls the energy transition?

Goldman Sachs finally figured it out.

https://oilprice.com/Energy/Energy-General/Goldman-Sachs-Excluding-Big-Oil-from-ESG-Portfolios-is-a-Mistake.html

According to Michele Della Vigna, head of EMEA natural resources research at Goldman Sachs, excluding Big Oil stocks from ESG portfolios is based on a “major mistake” in evaluating the energy transition from the perspective of European investors. Della Vigna says that unlike utilities, which are primarily infrastructure builders, oil and gas companies are  “risk-takers” and “market makers”. Della Vigna says the energy transition will likely be much longer than expected, “So, we need their capabilities, the balance sheet and the risk-taking. They are some of the largest investors in low carbon and whether we like it or not, we also need their core businesses of oil and gas,” he told CNBC.

U.S. Big Oil firms appear to concur with Della Vigna’s sentiment. According to Exxon Mobil (NYSE:$XOM XOM) Darren Woods, Europe should borrow a leaf from the U.S.’ approach to climate policy, adding that the continent risks driving companies away by over-regulating. Woods told Bloomberg that various carbon capture technologies under development in the U.S. will play a key role in the global decarbonization drive.

Back in April 2023, Woods touted Exxon’s burgeoning Low Carbon business, saying it has the potential to generate hundreds of billions in revenues and even outperform its legacy oil and gas business in the coming decades. According to Woods, the business could grow to generate tens of billions of dollars in revenue after the initial 10-year ramp-up. 

"This business is going to look quite a bit different from the base business of Exxon Mobil. It is going to have a much more stable, or less cyclical, profile," Dan Ammann,  president of Exxon's two-year-old Low Carbon Business Solutions unit, vowed.

In the same year, Exxon Mobil signed a long-term contract with industrial gas company Linde Plc. (NYSE:$LIN LIN) involving offtake of carbon dioxide associated with Linde’s planned clean hydrogen project in Beaumont, Texas. Through the contract, Exxon will transport and permanently store as much as 2.2M metric tons/year of CO2 from Linde’s plant.

Exxon is hardly alone in ambitious CCS plans. 

Last February, oil field services giant Schlumberger Ltd (NYSE:$SLB SLB) discussed its newly carved SLB New Energy unit which will focus on niches such as carbon solutions, hydrogen, energy storage, geothermal/ geoenergy and critical minerals each with a minimum addressable market of $10 billion, as reported by Bloomberg NEF.

This isn't new information. But it is for the wolves.

This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️ 🌊

title: TRUMP Changing Energy DEMAND & Market Share 🇺🇸
caption:
On his first day in office, Trump removed a pause on new LNG export terminal construction, which was implemented by the Biden administration, to increase gas production and export.

U.S. LNG exports to Europe averaged 25 million tonnes a year before 2022, while they totaled 55 million tonnes in both 2022 and 2023. Trump's moves support a production growth of nearly 100 million metric tonnes a year of additional LNG by 2031.

Restrictions on the import of energy from other countries could also put pressure on governments to increase the share of energy imports coming from the U.S. 

The CEO of Australia’s Woodside Energy, Meg O’Neill said that countries with a trade imbalance with the U.S. are “all asking themselves, ‘What can we do to try to level the playing field?’” O’Neill added they are making deals now mainly “so their government can say, ‘We’re taking action. We hear you, Mr. President.’” This view was echoed by other industry leaders. 

This month, companies in Japan, Taiwan, and South Korea revived a $44 billion gas project in Alaska that was long thought impossible. The project would include the development of a massive gas terminal in Alaska to export LNG to Asia. Earlier this month, Alaska Governor Mike Dunleavy said that Alaska LNG could begin exports by 2030.

At least six Asian countries have shown interest in buying more LNG from the U.S. to boost their trade ties and avoid tariffs, including India, Bangladesh, and Vietnam. 

South Africa has also stated its intention to expand the drilling rights of U.S. companies in its waters in response to Trump’s decision to freeze aid to the country. Ukraine also signaled plans to purchase more American gas. 

Europe has already increased its import of U.S.-produced LNG since 2022, as it moved away from a dependence on Russian gas to alternative supplies. 

https://oilprice.com/Energy/Natural-Gas/Trumps-Trade-Tactics-Are-Driving-New-LNG-Deals.html
