title: Knowledge Transfer Series #63 - Triple the S&P500
caption:
Might as well just scroll down and bookmark this one now! Especially if you are a young INVESTOR (as defined by KTS #11) and don't know where to start! 😃👍

I told you this back half of the Knowledge Transfer Series was 200-level coursework. But strap in and focus.....YOU CAN DO IT! Even if you are new to @beskar_capital .

Today, I make peace with my Conventionalist friends (but only for a day)!😅🤣😂 It is one of the few places of overlap that we share.

It's the DIVIDEND.

Anyone that tracks my portfolio knows how important dividends are to Beskar Capital's portfolio. And a big secret right now......while everyone is chasing securities in a speculative run in the 2nd stage of the meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle (including myself!)......I am noticing that dividend yield is rising.......so an incredible SALE!

What does that mean, Beskar? Well, in general, just by purchasing shares and holding them, a shareholder can realize greater than 10% annual return in income alone!!! A simple stock screen at this moment returns 224 companies that are currently paying more than 10% in annual dividend yield. And the great news is that many of these companies are at low stock prices set to climb. Several royalty companies in the commodity space are absolute gems right now that you can absolutely bank on going forward. NOTE: STRONGLY RECOMMEND TO AVOID ALL BANK (with commercial real estate on their loan books) & REIT STOCKS. While these stocks will likely draw your fellow INVESTORS (as defined by KTS #11) with hefty dividend yields, resulting in share price gains as well in the near to mid term, most all of these dividends are not stable and will be pulled out from under you when the real estate/banking crisis cycle completes. Stick with the unloved commodities that are in secular trend and kicking out increasing free cash flow! 💸 💰

So first, let's get a couple definitions down. There's two ways for a security to give you profits. One is INCOME. The other is CAPITAL APPRECIATION. I can't stand to see Blossomers talk about how they are in it for the INCOME. Or I just invest/trade in companies for CAPITAL APPRECIATION. Instead, let's call on both arms for PROFIT! Don't be shy!

Definitions:

(1) INCOME: This is what a security pays out to you in CASH DIVIDENDS (usually quarterly but can be monthly, annually, or special).

(2) CAPITAL APPRECIATION: This is the profit you eventually realize after the security's price rises over TIME.

Both are beautiful. Learn to love BOTH. Especially, right now in select sectors. The royalty firms are bottoming with RSI, paying double-digit yields, and are set to increase significantly in share price! So while you are waiting for the stock price to rise, why not get paid for it? If interested, I can share those tickers.....as always.....just ask!

But for today's post, I want to share a method that relies on both INCOME & CAPITAL APPRECIATION to TRIPLE the returns of the S&P500 with a simple BUY & HOLD strategy. It's why @beskar_capital has some stocks that he will NEVER EVER sell....as in NEVER. My children and grandchildren can pass them down.......forever!

First, let's remember the post why it's important to outperform the S&P500:

https://share.blossomsocial.com/44JRN5KzY61wi8nu5

So you see....just by outperforming the S&P500 by 1%.....it brings Financial Independence (FI) to you so much sooner. Imagine 2%......5%.....10%......or even 22%! How much sooner to FI?

Let's review some facts about the S&P500 historical returns:

Important takeaways:

-There are a lot of different numbers cited for the average annual return of the index. And while the average return is easy to compute, it’s important to define what you’re computing.

-The average annual return from 1928 through mid-2024 has been 7.9%.

-But that overstates the compound annual growth rate of the index, which has been just 6.2%.

-Compound annual growth is a more accurate indication of how much an investment will grow over time. Furthermore, those numbers ignore the impact of dividends and the potential to reinvest dividends, otherwise known as total return.

-The total return of the S&P 500 produced a compound annual growth rate of 10.1% over the past 96.5 years.

-Unfortunately, inflation eats into those returns, which means the real compound annual growth rate of the index with dividends reinvested is 6.8%.

Source: https://www.fool.com/investing/stock-market/indexes/sp-500/annual-returns/

OK, Beskar! I'm IN! How can we do it with a buy & hold strategy realizing both income & capital appreciation? Are you listening newbies?

Just buy the stock $XOM .

That's it. Set it and forget it for the next decade to TRIPLE THE S&P500. 🤑🤑🤑

What!?!? That's it. Yep. That easy.....and it can be done with a single investment all at once.....now.

I don't believe you, Beskar. How does this possibly work? 🤑

First, some background on ExxonMobil. ExxonMobil has been part of the Top10 market capitalization stocks in the U.S. for every decade going back to it's evolution from Standard Oil. 🛢

Second, ExxonMobil is cranking out free cash flow from $30 Billion in FY2024 growing to $37 Billion in FY2026. And the stock price has been coiling for 2.5 years. WTF?!?!?! 💸 💸

Third, exciting TIMES are here for ExxonMobil - as they will be the ones to pick up the pieces from this failed "instant" transition. No one should be surprised to learn that ExxonMobil becomes the greenest energy company of all. Attracting the most talented engineers to solve all problems, they are on the cusp of major innovation in direct lithium extraction (did you know they invented the lithium battery ?), carbon capture, recycled plastics, etc. They are currenty making major inroads into transitioning how we use energy molecules most efficiently with the least impact on the environment. So quit hating......and empower the best! Society will transition a lot quicker if you do! 🍃 🌿

Fourth, it belongs to an elite group of 65 stocks in the S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrats Index. This is a list of public companies that have increased their dividend payments consecutively for at least 25 years. To be included in the S&P Dividend Aristocrats Index, a stock must be a member of the S&P 500 and meet certain size and liquidity requirements. 🤴 👑

Fifth, the company has a very high percentage of retail investors that rely on that dividend. They know how important it is to continue to deliver increasing dividends now and into the future.

Last, see the attached table and 3 charts.

Observations?:

(1) From the dividend rate history chart, ExxonMobil average's an annual increase has been plotted since 2001. Through the previous real estate/banking crisis cycle peak in 2007 and the March 2020 -$34 per barrel crude price through today.....this dividend grows annually at a steady average of 6.8%. Do you see that R-squared value? That is a corrlation coefficient (0.9776 is practically 1.00!) that shows that the dividend rate is nearly perfectly correlated with TIME! So steady as can be and very predictable going forward. This is the INCOME! 💰

(2) From the stock price history chart, ExxonMobil's average annual increase in stock price is 11.79%. This is the CAPITAL APPRECIATION! 📈

(3) From the table, you can see, the cumulative profit - both INCOME & CAPITAL APPRECIATION - over TIME if you made a $5,000 investment in $XOM today in 2025. Come 2035, this will predictably record $11,121 in profit ($16,121 in value). And this doesn't even include reinvesting the dividends (which is a @beskar_capital NO-NO!). Plus, this is likely extremely conservative, because this stock has been coiling for 2.5 years! The breakout that will take place over the coming ~24 months will be AMAZING in terms of stock price!

So......conservatively $XOM is predicted to return 22.24% per year while the S&P500 returns 6-8% per year. 😅🤣😂

If you don't believe me, check out the final chart with the grey background to see how. $XOM absolutely annihilates the S&P500. And now it's sitting on the verge of breakout. 📈

It's way easier than what those "expert" wolves want you to think it is. 🤑🤑🤑

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #62- Learning the Future
caption:
🔮

They hate this. 😂 You know who.....those Conventionalists. You were supposed to just invest in the indicies, set it and forget it, ride the coaster through the peaks and valleys, get your single digit percent of average annual return, retire at 70, and shuffle along through the end of your life.

TIMING markets is their Kryptonite!

With the recent expected market volatility - as the wolves try to figure out what government disruption and swamp cleaning looks like....and more important for their existence...... how it impacts stocks - the Conventionalists have woken from their winter hibernation to troll around these AMAZING 10-year returns in just months! They ABSOLUTELY HATE this! 😅🤣😂

So let's give them something to caffeniate them after a long winter's nap! 😅

A post on Learning the Future.

So prior to this post, I posted an exercise to see how well we are interpreting charts. I wanted 100+ responses....but only got 25. 😐 It's ok.....after we hit ~25, the data begins to follow a normal distribution. 😅👍

Here's the post to catch up:

https://share.blossomsocial.com/zWAzn8NZHp5Zo5WV6

And here's the voting results:
The top 4 BEST LOOKING CHARTS:
B - 23 votes
N - 21 votes
K - 16 votes
E - 16 votes

The bottom 4 WORST LOOKING CHARTS:
D - 25 votes
I - 23 votes
P - 21 votes

So as a collective KTS group, you got it right!! 🥳 🎉 🎊


Best: B, K, N
Worst: D, I, P

Special shout out to:
@rnusta
@goldenfit
@ms_shine
@irbaz

for getting a perfect individual score. Not surprised as that group has been around since the early days in KTS. CONGRATULATIONS! 😅🤣😂

Quit with the glaze, Beskar.....let's get on to the crystal ball part!

Naturally. ☺️. After all, 🎶 🎵 How can you have your pudding if you don't eat your meat? 🎵 🎶

Ok, let's eat the meat!

First, what the hell are these charts anyway? It's the Advance/Decline line for popular stock market indicies. It's kinda similar to our other A/D line (Accumulation/Distribution line) that we covered in KTS #49. It is a cumulative indicator that helps us understand market breadth. So how much market investment is spreading out to the full "width" of the index ..... and all of the stocks within it.

OK, I get that, but how is it calculated? Each day, the difference between the number of stocks within that index that CLOSED positive and the number of stocks that CLOSED negative are added to the previous day's value.

Looking at this chart alongside the actual index price chart can confirm direction of the price chart.

So if we see a price chart climbing....we would like to see the Advance/Decline line climbing as well to confirm a broadening of investment through the index, confirming the momentum in the direction. Conversely, if the index is dropping and it's Advance/Decline line is declining as well? Expect further declines in the index going forward. 🔮

It is very much like A/D Accumulation/Distribution indicating the future price direction. Maybe reread KTS #49 if not completely clear.

Understand? Keep your ears plugged, Conventionalists! Not talking to you! 😅

Ok, I'm dying here, Beskar. Can you pull the curtain back on these charts you made us grade!?!?!?!

Sure. See the attached chart. I marked up the "answers" .... and a couple that were close. See if your analysis coincides with the markup!

Observations:
- B: The DOW is hitting higher highs and higher lows with its broadening indicator...and just tested a previous "ceiling" as a new floor! Great sign for Industrials in this index! 😄🤑👍

- K: The FTSE 100 is convincingly breaking out! This is the top 100 United Kingdom stocks. Remember when I bought Rolls Royce the other day? $RYCEY . I will plan to accumulate more. Probably a good sign for those UK Pharmaceuticals too! 😉🇬🇧 The FTSE 250 and FTSE ALL are improving in breadth...but for now, it's probably best to 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 the best companies there. No one is talking about UK stocks are they? 😅🤣😂 Well, the EARLY INSIDERS (as defined by KTS #11) are. 😅🤣😂

-N: The German DAX. Well that country has been a real shitshow for the past couple of years! But since Christmas when it broke KTS #23? Not sure there's anything that's been better ! 😅🤣😂 Again, confirming investment in Europe is finally waking up while the world focuses on Trump! 😅🤣😂

-D: The NASDAQ. Remember when I posted about a Tech Transition (KTS #18) in the summer of last year? Yes, as we said in that post, investing in Tech is a GREAT PLACE TO BE.....as long as the company you hold BEATS EARNINGS, RAISES GUIDANCE, AND THEN BEATS THOSE FORWARD ESTIMATES......quarter after quarter. When that music stops, you better race to find a chair. I think most of you can see that happening in select tech companies. This chart suggests we can expect that to continue......but don't confuse....after all the E chart, the NASDAQ 100, is still in consolidation.......holding on to ........ hope! 😅🤣😂

-I: The Russell Microcap continues an awful trend. These are the smallest stocks. So dogshit micropennies by nature. Remember, this part of the real estate/banking crisis cycle loves SELECT tickers in this group. So stock picker territory indeed. Like all gold, you have to pan for it through the muck! 😅🤣😂 Breadth in microcap indicies is rare......but that's why we go for the winners by using our KTS toolset! 😄👍🤑

-P: This is the Straits Times index (Singapore). A clearly defined stairway to hell! 😈 A series of Lower High, Lower Lows in a descending channel to the center of the Earth. 💀 ☠️. BUT, we watch this and Q (Malaysia) carefully over TIME......because when these break KTS #23, we know that 🎶 🎵 Santa Claus is coming to town. Santa Claus is com-ing to TOWN!!! 🎶 🎵 🎅 🎁

So there you have it. The very beginning of building your 🔮. Learn the Future.

So we can successfully TIME markets! 🤑🤑🤑 Anyone that says you can't do it either never looked into it because some trusted Conventionalist or Ultrarich source told them so.......or they tried....and THEY can't figure out how! 😅🤣😂

Take that 💥, Conventionalists! 😅🤣😂 (God bless them. They really are good people and mean well despite their brainwashed regurgitations of trite sayings from an Investing for Dummies 101 book).

I continue to give you my absolute best. 😁👍

title: New Position in Verde Clean Fuels
caption:
${{VGAS-STOCK-US-USD}}

If Diamondback is in.....I'm in.

The low-carbon car of the future is the one you're already driving.

Verde’s renewable gasoline in a traditional internal combustion engine has a lower life cycle carbon intensity than an EV. 

Did you read that above? Uh-oh. 😅🤣😂

KTS #19: Closing of $50 Million Equity Investment by Cottonmouth Ventures, LLC, a Wholly-Owned Subsidiary of Diamondback Energy, Inc.
Cottonmouth purchases 12.5 million shares of Verde Class A common stock at a price of $4.00 per share.

Verde produces renewable, low-carbon gasoline from biomass, such as agricultural byproducts.

This is a new, cleaner way to produce the same gasoline that’s currently used in internal combustion engine (ICE) vehicles and leverages existing refueling infrastructure.

When gasoline is produced from gasified biomass, Verde puts more carbon in the ground than carbon is released when the gasoline is consumed, resulting in carbon-negative gasoline.

We have developed two different pathways to gasoline production, each with the goal of reducing carbon emissions as the world moves towards net zero.

Natural gas-to-gasoline

30% less carbon-intensive gasoline from natural gas

By using natural gas as the feedstock, Verde’s technology mitigates harmful flaring in oil-producing regions. Instead, it enables producers to use their natural gas to create valuable commodity-grade RBOB gasoline, i.e, the same gasoline we use in our cars but 30% less carbon-intensive. 

Our technology generates in-basin demand for natural gas, alleviating pipeline and takeaway constraints, while also reducing flaring and its harmful greenhouse gas emissions.

This enables oil and gas companies to transform this underutilized energy stream into a marketable product, providing a higher-value pathway for monetization of associated natural gas and ultimately reducing carbon emissions.

Verde has a joint development agreement with Cottonmouth Ventures, a subsidiary of Diamondback Energy (NASDAQ: FANG), to build a natural gas-to-gasoline facility in Martin County, Texas.

This facility in the Permian, expected to be operational in 2027, will produce approximately 3,000 barrels per day of fully refined gasoline and mitigate flaring by consuming 34 million cubic feet of natural gas per day.

Verde's modular plant will set a new benchmark for natural gas-to-gasoline production, serving as a scalable model for future facilities across the Permian Basin and beyond.

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #61 - 15% Exit Selling
caption:
3,100+ followers and - as promised - another Knowledge Transfer Series post!

This post marks the final how/when/why to SELL a security. From KTS #56, we realized that there are more than 50 Whys to Sell a Stock. You can check in on that post here:

From this, we agreed to go in depth on 5 of them before we get back to the content we need to ...... in order to stay on TIME with the real estate/banking crisis cycle! After all......we are in the middle of a massive bull market........so in balance.....we are BUYING, not SELLING! 😅🤑👍

The first 4 of 5 SELLING tools we covered were:

1) KTS #57: Sector Rotation Selling
2) KTS #58: Smart Money Selling
3) KTS #59: RSI Selling
4) KTS #60: TAM Selling

And now to wrap up this SELLING mini-series within the Knowledge Transfer Series, we dig in on 15% Exit Selling. Though this one didn't receive a single vote.....one of my favorite bros, @adventure69 , typed in a comment that Beskar should just pick the ones he thinks are best for this market. So here we go!

15% Exit Selling. Seems pretty straightforward. And it is. This is an ABSOLUTELY PERFECT tool to implement right now for a whole host of reasons. Here's why:

(1) This technique will help you DEVELOP THE RIGHT INVESTOR/TRADER PSYCHOLOGY that will be absolutely necessary as the real estate/banking crisis cycle starts peaking - so not a bad one to practice with now. You must never forget why you are here and reading this post right now. Most everyone that joins Blossom to improve their returns over TIME......first and foremost. Key point: No matter what a Conventionalist tells you.......it is NOT TRUE that when you buy shares of stock.......that you are "Investing" in a company you believe in. You're not! What's really going on is we are just trading paper amongst ourselves.......with the objective to maximize profits among these exchanges. Kind of a sick and depressing perspective I suppose......but factual. The shares you electronically "buy" have been floating around in the open market in most cases since the company went public.

Why do I harp on this? Because....our human instinct is to want to support a company buy buying their shares. We want these companies to WIN! You like Starbucks....so you want some $SBUX . LuLu Lemon? $LULU Doesn't it feel great when you own shares in a company that you just love their products/services? You mean I can buy those great clothes......AND get rich doing it? 🤑 It's almost like they are paying for our wardrobe!!! What fun! 😁👍

You can't escape it. We ALL do it. You are human. 😅 But that mentality interferes with why you're here. Remember? You are here to maximize profits on exchanging paper in the open market. The point is......this technique is a fantastic tool to begin to dehumanize your mission. 😂 If interested, not a bad idea to test it out with a dedicated portion of a portfolio.

Selling out can be difficult because it can continue to climb as soon as you go. Your human mind will be playing greedy tricks on you by convincing yourself it's too early to leave.....or why not let it run up to 20% instead of 15% exit because it seems like the momentum is there? With this technique....don't do it......stay disciplined ......... because they can't take away your profits once you lock them in. 🎶 Don't look back! 🎶 🎵

Last, you MUST convince yourself that there is ALWAYS a profit-making opportunity at the exact moment you are ready to deploy capital and buy a stock. ALWAYS. Once you fully realize this within your market psychology......you will be able to part with your favorite companies and your hope for them to do well. Remember......hope is not a successful investment strategy despite being the most popular one! 😅

2) This market. The lame duck period (period of TIME that starts following the day of an election resulting in a change in leadership........and runs 6 months past inauguration day) is a volatile period. Stocks fly up and down as the wolves try and position themselves early into the future beneficiaries of the new world order. In the USA, election happens every 4 years....but the change in leadership is sometimes 8 year periods (e.g. - two consecutive party terms). So this is a relatively unique TIME in our lifetimes. And this tool is perfect! Keep in mind that the institutional money (which controls 78% of the daily trading volume) is sloshing around right now like a liquid in a truck going 80 mph down the highway with stop and gos! The wolves really don't know who's going to win. Everyday, they learn a little more about all of these executive orders.......which companies SHOULD benefit, which will get clipped. Wind energy......sorry! EVs.....subsidies pulled right out from under them! But crypto? energy services? These major policy shifts will ripple through the balance sheets of the impacted companies.........and the wolves - right now as we speak - are transferring massive money flows both in/out of who they think the winning/losing horses will be. This creates an extremely volatile market. One that can make you sick.....up 38% one day.....down 22% the next......only to recover up 20% soon thereafter. We talked about Beta a few posts ago.......the Beta on individual stocks can climb in periods like this. So 🎶 🎵 Go on, take the money and run 🎶 🎵 is a great strategy for this market.

3) This technique is also good now to learn how to realize that there are markets where trading and stock picking are more profitable than investing. In the long run, investing entitles you to compounding rights.......which is something traders can't hold a candle to. That is a future KTS. But for now.......stocks - on net - can go nowhere for the first half of 2025......as the bets are made on who will win/lose. But while most others "invested" right now look at a roller coaster ride of ups and downs to a net nothing.........you on the other hand, can extract large profits out of this market with this tool.

OK, Beskar. I can see what you're saying. But what's the process to do this?

Great question. So......simple really. And again for those curious......not a bad idea to dedicate a portion of your portfolio to this to baby step in......or if you feel better not to risk real money......just do it with a watchlist.......so you can be prepared for the next TIME a similar market like this rolls around.

STEP ONE: PIck a stock that has clearly broke out of the Consolidation phase and IS EARLY into the Accumulation phase. Great if it passes KTS tests #23/#25/#50 as well. But refer to KTS#29 for help on breakout confirmations. This is NOT DIFFICULT. Feel free to throw out a ticker to the class for feedback on potential candidates!

STEP TWO: Use your KTS#9 (trading tool) for a good entry point. Remember, we are in a trader's market......need to use the right tool for the right job. Note: If you are a stubborn investor that must always adhere to your one-way approach......I would strongly recommend that you don't even look at markets for the next 5-6 months and go vacation or something! ⛱️

STEP THREE: Buy the stock when RSI is Oversold.

STEP FOUR: Record the cost basis stock price.

STEP FIVE: Multiply the cost basis stock price by 1.15 and immediately enter a LIMIT ORDER to SELL the stock at that +15% gain.

STEP SIX: Redeploy into the next ticker candidate that satisfies STEP ONE above!

When you pick 3-4 stocks to do this with......every day you'll see your cash balance jump when these orders trigger and sell as they realize the 15% Exit stage left moment. But it's kinda fun to constantly go shopping for the next one! 😁

A final point, in this market, we do not use stop losses for this technique in THIS market. The stocks are so volatile, you simply cannot compete with the wolves and algorithims that are set to flush you out of positions. Rapid instant drops and gains are the norm in this environment. You can see it everywhere in the ticker tape......especially in the final 5 minutes of the wolves trading day before closing TIME (KTS #24). Stop losses are a beautiful tool........in most markets......but not this one. 😉

Learn to read and listen to what markets are telling you. Don't be so arrogant to think you are going to move markets - you're not a market maker! Knowing the market you are in is key.......and knowing which are the RIGHT TOOLS for the RIGHT JOB! 🧰

BUY/SELL/RECYCLE

For this market, This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️ 🌊

title: Tariff Talk Heats Up, 🇨🇦! FRIDAY....or else!

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #60 - TAM Selling
caption:
Over 3,000 followers.....and here we go.....another KTS post!

So 2 left in our mini-series on the how/when/why to SELL a security. In KTS#56, we've posted that there's more than 50 Whys to Sell a Stock - and in this mini-series on SELLING - we've decided to cover 5 of them. Catch up in Knowledge Transfer Series posts #57: Sector Rotation Selling, #58: Smart Money Selling, #59: RSI Selling.......and now here today, #60: TAM Selling!

So let's get right after it!

What is TAM Selling? First, a few definitions:

TAM: Total Addressable Market. Also known as total available market. So this is a measure of the total demand for a product of service. As an example, think about the U.S. soft drink market. The TAM would be the total demand by ALL CONSUMERS for soft drinks. A quick internet search for the soft drink market TAM: "The U.S. soft drinks market size was valued at USD 285.93 billion in 2023 and is projected to grow at a CAGR 7.5% from 2024 to 2030." So roughly $286 BILLION.

Our next term is market share.

Market Share: Market share is the percentage of TAM that a single company owns. Obviously, companies compete to occupy as much of the TAM as possible. The biggest share owners of TAM make the most money!!!!! So back to our example of the U.S. soft drink market, the market share any one company has is the percentage of the Total Addressable (Available) Market for U.S. soft drinks. Make sense? 😄👍

Some of the major companies fighting for share are listed below (some public, some private):

Key U.S. Soft Drinks Companies:

AriZona Beverages USA
BlueTriton Brands, Inc.
CAROLINA BEVERAGE GROUP, LLC
DS Services of America, Inc.
Keurig Dr Pepper
Monster Energy Company
National Beverage Corp.
PepsiCo
The Coca‑Cola Company
The Kraft Heinz Company

So this method of selling is a long-term investing/divesting positioning. It's how INSIDERS (as defined by KTS #11) look at securities to find winners. And yes.......you guessed it..........It's how INVESTORS (as defined by KTS #11) become bagholders of losing companies.

So the crux of this method is to identify companies that are GAINING market share of the TAM pie. All companies fight for as much market share as possible in capitalistic competition. Market share is in total flux moving up and down......as another example, did you hear recently that Nike $NKE is losing market share to Adidas $ADDYY ? I bet fairly recently you learned that $NKE hasn't been doing so well. And.......like most INVESTORS, you probably only figured it out at some point within the past year. Am I right? Well, had you been paying attention to TAM/Market Share, you would have been able to clearly see the money flows of the INSIDERS (remember, they are the institutional money that commands 78% of the daily trading volume).....and that Nike started LOSING at the expense of Adidas WINNING.....2 years ago!?!?!? You could have been switching out of Nike......and into Adidas......right alongside the EARLY INVESTORS over 2 years ago!?!??! All the while, your Conventionalist Investor friends tried to get you to dollar cost average (DCA) into a market share loser!!!! Do you see why you shouldn't be blindly DCA/DRIP? 🤮 Conventionalist garbage for the masses! For those that need the visual, check out the attached 2-year comparison chart attached!

WOW Beskar! Why haven't you told me about this already?

Well, we have limitless content to bring.....and we'll continue building our toolset over TIME.....but for this post, we'll start moving into PEER COMPARISONS.

Your trading platform should have a PEER COMPARISONS section for research. Those that follow me, know I strongly recommend using Schwab as an investment platform, which comes with full access to the GOLD STANDARD trading platform, Think or Swim. If you are in the U.S., it's a no-brainer. If you are Canadian......find out why you can't get it......after all ....... it's the old Canadian firm TD Ameritrade that brought all of the original brainpower! Anyway, all platforms should have a peer comparison performance chart.

And don't let the beauty of this technique go unnoticed. While your Conventionalist friends are busy grinding through balance sheets, computing price targets, and wasting countless hours of dissection into any one company..........you get your Wolf Biologist degree and join the UNConventionalist Army......and instead follow the INSIDER money (which translates into stock price performance) to understand who's WINNING......and who's LOSING!🤑🤑🤑

Another example please, Beskar!

OK, attached are 2 other charts for companies in the Oil & Gas industry - a Beskar favorite at this point in the real estate/banking crisis cycle! Both $NOV and $FTI compete for TAM in the energy services space - heavily in the offshore market. First chart is a 10-year chart that has FTI, NOV, and the S&P500 in comparison. Notice in this one, that since the last Oil and Gas BUST......in 2015.....both of these subsector stocks started in decline.....while the S&P500 continued to climb. Definitely NOT a TIME to be invested in the Oil and Gas space, eh? But come 2022? Beskar started buying into Oil & Gas Service companies in the Fall of 2022. Specifically, FTI over historically dominant player, NOV. How did I know how to do this? Peer Comparisons. After an initial stock position in FTI, I moved into CALL LEAPs. For those that follow me, they know FTI is a core holding and a favorite stock in my portfolio.......and continues to be as the cycle plays out. See second comparison chart of these two over the last 3 years. Note the divergence between FTI and NOV......and how all along......you would have know who's GAINING share and who's LOSING it. Also note - for our Conventional ETF Indexer Friends - that FTI delivered 10x+ returns over the S&P500! And this is why we invest in stocks, not indicies! Learn to read and listen to what markets are telling you........to successfully TIME markets. Conventionalists will blab on and on about "Time in the market is better than timing the market".......or constantly buying beaten down stocks, regardless of the current market we're in. Don't listen to them......that's an approach of hope. 🤢 Instead, focus on who actually is WINNING and monitor the money flows. Because in reality, it's the money flow into a stock in accumulation that causes the stock price to rise - nothing else. It doesn't matter that the historical P/E is so far below its historical average........if the institutional money isn't buying. Oil and gas stocks have been a disaster since 2015. And you would have been a fool to own them then (if seeking capital appreciation. Dividends make them always worthy. But now, you get both!). But today, you can see the rebound, can't you? And more importantly, you can see which ones are gaining share! 😄👍

If you want to outperform the S&P500, start following the money.......as it transfers into stock performance. And give yourself an extra advantage by finding the WINNINGEST stocks in those sectors. And if they aren't winning, they are LOSING.....and the stock charts will tell you so!

TAM Selling gets you out of NOV and into FTI .......... when the TIME is right!

One final point and exercise here. For new industries and themes....think Cybersecurity, Carbon Sequestration, Nuclear, Drone Technology, Fat Drugs, etc. and on and on............start trying to pull up the peer comparison (and in relation to S&P500) to just start killing it - your stock-picking performance will improve greatly and you will trounce most all of your fellow Blossomers 😆........ that don't take the TIME to read the Knowledge Transfer Series carefully. You know I want YOU to WIN! 😉🤑

This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️ 🌊

P.S. - Make sure you catch up reading all Knowledge Transfer Series posts carefully. These are foundational to the 200-level courses that le ahead. So if you've ever said to yourself, I really need to read all of those KTS posts......I'd recommend that you find the TIME now. We'll need those as we interpret the peaks of the cycle in the coming years.

I always give you amazing, juicy meat, don't I? 😅🤣😂

title: What Is Going On: Housing in Calgary & Edmonton ??
caption:
For those that follow me, you know that Beskar Capital's investment decisions are always made taking into account the phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle.

So that requires constantly monitoring housing and credit trends. 😉🤑

Today, a review of the 2024 and 2025 outlook in Canada. 🇨🇦 Since a lot of you are constantly talking about housing (and for good reason), I am sharing this link, if interested:

https://www.cmhc-schl.gc.ca/professionals/housing-markets-data-and-research/market-reports/housing-market/housing-market-outlook

Here's a summary of the results:

MONTREAL:
- Slight upturn in housing starts ✅️
- Moderate recovery in property price ✅️
- Decrease in vacancy rate and increase in prices for rental market. ✅️

OTTAWA:
- Anticipated increase in housing starts ✅️
- Modest house price growth ✅️
- Marginal increase in vacancy rate 😔

TORONTO:
- Decline in housing starts 😔
- Increase in housing prices ✅️
- Slight increase in vacancy rate 😔

VANCOUVER:
- Decline in new home construction 😔
- Prices rebounding slowly and projected new highs in 2026 (😉) ✅️
- Rental vacancy rates are tight and prices are high 💪

EDMONTON:
- Strong new housing starts 💪
- Growing resale market 💪
- Rental demand remains tight, surpassing supply!?!?! 💪

CALGARY:
- Robust demand in housing starts with increasing population and income levels 💪
- Increased development of multifamily homes 💪
- Upward trend with price gains 💪
- Further tightening of vacancy rates 💪

Why on Earth is the Housing Market in Calgary & Edmonton behaving so much differently than the rest of Canada!?!?!?

Something big is going on in the regional economies of Calgary and Edmonton that are resulting in robust growth in housing starts, housing prices, multifamily homes, and average income levels with a corresponding undersupply in RENTAL properties as well!?!?!

Oh geez....Canadians, can you help me understand this phenomenon? 😉

Can we profit off of this insight somehow?🤑

This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️ 🌊

P.S. - I included a couple of TIME trend charts for Calgary for further review.

Adding 100 new followers in just a few days! That's great......because I have so much content to bring in this market that we need to move faster!

So calling all BOTS and NOOBS......if you want to realize absolutely amazing double-digit annualized returns and consistently outperform the S&P500 now and for years to come, start with my first post from early March 2024, "They are telling us so what are you waiting for?". Here it is:

https://share.blossomsocial.com/hSZ2kv7DBpEGpCjR8

This is unabashedly the greatest post in Blossom history. Ok, ok......maybe a little too self-promoting! 😊 But maybe give it a like and bookmark if you feel it deserves it! I'll share why later! 😉🤑

After reading that, then you are ready to join the journey in the Knowledge Transfer Series. It currently has 58 posts (including today's) and each are titled "Knowledge Transfer Series #__" from 1 to 58.......and beyond! :) In this series, we expose the UNConventional tools and methods of the Wall Street Wolves to successfully TIME markets - a very different approach to Conventional brainwashing in the world of 7% annualized returns through index ETFs, DCA, and DRIP where you can only realize FI (Financial Independence) when you are too old to do anything else but sip on soup and fall asleep at 6 pm! 🤪

So we are in the middle of a how to SELL miniseries of 5 posts where we highlight in detail just 5 of the more than 50+ how/when/why SELLING approaches. We select the ones that have been voted on and are the most helpful in this particular market!🤑

Today's is RSI Selling! I can't possibly do a miniseries on how/when/why to SELLING without covering the most popular one - RSI Sellling. As a common Technical Trader Technique, it is a great tool for this market. So let's get started.

RSI is the Relative Strength Index. For those unfamiliar with it, you should read Knowledge Transfer Series #9 as a prerequisite prior to moving on in this post. Here it is:

https://share.blossomsocial.com/QqPuDunCNtp5JauL7

The method is rigid. As soon as your stock trends into OVERSOLD territory (RSI<=30), you BUY the stock. Conversely, as soon as your stock trends into OVERBOUGHT territory (RSI>=70), you SELL the stock. It's that straightforward! You didn't really think these Wall Streeters are much smarter than that, did you? 😅🤣😂

The RSI - for any stock - oscillates back and forth between OVERSOLD to OVERBOUGHT to OVERSOLD to OVERBOUGHT to OVERSOLD to OVERBOUGHT......on and on. Get it? So the technique is just to jump in when OVERSOLD (RSI<30) and get out when OVERBOUGHT (RSI>70). All trading platforms have this indicator available and usually defaults on a 14-day RSI.

Now, notice that this method has absolutely nothing to do with fundamentals - you know, how the business and their valuation metrics are doing. It's pure trader. Consequently, @beskar_capital doesn't use this that much. I do use it to optimize entry points in a stock I'm looking to enter.....but as I accumulate positions for long-term compounding, I overlook the intermediate OVERBOUGHT indicators. Besides, stocks can sit in these OVERBOUGHT/OVERSOLD ranges for a long period of TIME. Still, if I'm a new investor, I think dedicating a portion of a portfolio to this method to "feel" how it works in growing your capital in an outperforming way makes a lot of sense!

So @beskar_capital tweeks this method - just a touch - for available stocks to select. Recognize that this method works well with ANY STOCK in ANY MARKET. However, in this particular market, I like to screen stocks for high Beta (Beta >=1.0) and within 5% of their 52-week high. 😉 Beta!?!?!? What's that, Beskar !?!?! Beta is a greek letter that is used in the world of finance as a measure for volatility of a security relative to the S&P500. So, the Beta for the S&P500 is 1.0 . And securities with a Beta >1.0 are more volatile. We like that in this market because stocks that move more than the S&P500 can deliver above average returns when using the RSI-oscillator method.

Great news! ALL trading platforms can do this. So first run a scan on stocks that have a high Beta (>1.0) and are within 5% of their 52-week high. Then, simply find when the stock enters and exits the OVERBOUGHT (>=70) or OVERSOLD (<=30) range to take action! 🫠👍🤑

Examples please, Beskar?

Of course, I ran the Beta/52-wk High stock screen above and picked some at random. See the 4 charts below. The charts are self-explanatory....but feel free to ask any questions!

This method allows you to continually rotate into a new security that's flashing OVERSOLD and holding until OVERBOUGHT. It is a systematic approach - that if you stay disciplined - will lock force you into outperformance! 😉 And that's our goal, isn't it? 😅🤣😂

This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️ 🌊

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #58- Smart Money Selling
caption:
Suuuuuuueeeeeeeeyyyyyyyy! Calling the pigs for dinner! 🐖 🐷 🐽 🤑🤑🤑

Just crossed the 2,800 follower threshold which means TIME for another Knowledge Transfer Series post!

We are in the middle of our mini series of posts on the how/when/why of SELLING. If interested, catch up by starting with an overview of the miniseries in KTS #56 50 Ways to Leave Your Lover. Then, KTS #57 (Sector Rotation Sell) is 1 of 5 SELLING techniques that were voted ........ and are solid approaches to SELLING in this market. So this post here is #2 of 5 came in 2nd in our poll with votes from @figuringitout , @gabbyluo , @thewinner , and @snieuwold - Smart Money Selling. A good one for this phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle.

Smart Money Selling: A security moves from the Accumulation phase into a Consolidation/Distribution phase.
The preface for this SELLING technique requires the re-read of KTS #49: The Reversal Indicator where we cover the Accumulation/Distribution line indicator. And I recommend a refresher in the most often referenced KTS #11: Insiders vs. Investors. Here's the link to both:

https://share.blossomsocial.com/qSotJcxSUZyeEYsL9

https://share.blossomsocial.com/gKHC1p5jZAMWyD6Y6

So give them a careful read and then come back here! 😄👍

Let's dig in!

So we already know that any security is constantly in one of three phases: (1) Accumulation, (2) Consolidation, and (3) Distribution.

Accumulation is when both INSIDERS and INVESTORS are fighting to purchase a stock and there are very few holders of the stock willing to sell it. This heavy DEMAND for shares with an UNDERSUPPLY of available shares to purchase results in the price of the stock to climb. The slope of the climb depends on the fevered pitch of the buyers. Obviously, the more FOMO the steeper the slope of the rise in the stock's price chart. That's easy to understand, right?

Consolidation is when INSIDERS are SELLING but INVESTORS are BUYING. Here the DEMAND for acquiring shares in the stock is in BALANCED SUPPLY with shares available from willing sellers. This condition results in a stock's price moving up and down within a relatively tight range (relative to the prior period of accumulation or distribution). Got it?

Distribution is the only other possible phase that a stock can experience. Here the DEMAND for shares is far less than the number of available shares provided by willing sellers - a condition of OVERSUPPLY. As a result, the stock price drops. As with the Accumulation phase, the steepness of the slope is tied to the furor in which INSIDERS are dumping the stock. Naturally, if INSIDERS are running for the exits, the stock price drops quickly. Ok, you got it.

Start using these words to describe stock price charts. Accumulation, Consolidation, and Distribution. Why? Because the greatest investor/trader of our TIME tells us so. We will unveil inKTS #100 or #101. Raise your financial game and........ Just Do It! 🤪

Quick inclass exercise: Make 3 columns (Accumulation, Consolidation, Distribution) , look at the chart for each security you own and record it's current (AS IN RIGHT NOW) which phase it's in. Why? Because it will set in just how much of your portfolio the INSIDERS (you know, the whales that control 78% of the daily trading volume) actually like. So quit asking @beskar_capital if it's still a buy.......and just look at the damn chart to see if INSIDERS are ACCUMULATING it! 😅

Let's get in to the selling technique now.

Ask yourself, what if ALL of my stocks were in Accumulation phase? Do you think your portfolio is going to go up in value, down in value, or stay about the same? 😂 How do you think a portfolio with 100% of it's securities in the Accumulation phase will perform against a portfolio in 100% Consolidation, or one in 100% Distribution? 😅🤣😂 Funny......and elementary isn't it? I can hear the Conventionalists loading up their rifles now ready to shoot you between the eyes about investing being a process of evaluating company fundamentals and their silly trite sayings like "Time in the market is better than timing the market". Ugh! I really can't stand those rigid 101 rules they live by, can you tell? 🤮🤮🤮

Anyway, let's get back to it - we don't need those distractors right now. It really isn't harder than that. You just need to get better at identifying which phase your security is in. So start practicing. It's so easy after you've looked at 10,000+ charts. Sorry, no easy button for experience Z's. 😂

So what if we just hold securities in the Accumulation phase then? 😂🤣😅

And that's what the Smart Money Selling approach is. I don't call it chasing performance......but I do acknowledge that it's riding with the whales. So what! 🐚 🐋

So here's how this particular selling technique works. (1) USING A 1-YEAR chart. Find a security price chart that started climbing. (2) As long as the Accumulation/Distribution line makes a NEW HIGH in after the CLOSE - since the accumulation phase began - within the next 10 trading days of the previous Accumulation/Distribution high, you continue to hold and reset the clock for another 10 days. Does Blossom have BOLD yet? If it did, I'd bold the sentence above. Again, if a NEW HIGH (since the accumulation phase began) in the A/D line AFTER THE CLOSE continues to be established, you continue to surf. Get it? So if 10 trading days pass......and the A/D line fails to establsh a NEW HIGH, then you exit the position regardless of current trading price. It's a hard set rule. You understand, right?

See smart money can't hide from a few indicators. I prefer Money Flow .....however, the Accumulation/Distribution line indicator is an adequate proxy for money flow and seems to be available in everyone's platform. It works the same. Notice that we aren't using the price chart outside of identifying the phase (accumulation/consolidation/distribution). Instead, we focus on the A/D line. Why? Because smart money can start scaling out of a stock while the price continues to climb. We ride the whales so we're comfortable leaving when they do.......even if that means giving up the tippy top of the price chart. Make sense?

Ok, how about some examples? I picked two. One is a red hot stock for years to come, $GEV (😅🤑) that's a free tip for the ages!) and the other is a stock in the 10 most talked about stocks on Blossom, $RGTI .

SEE CHARTS BELOW.

First, with GE Vernova, phase identification of price chart - yellow arrows are periods of consolidation, the green is a period of accumulation. So, once we saw the phase change around August 2024 from Consolidation to Accumulation, we can start the 10 day trading clock on the A/D line, which is shown below. This A/D line is where we solely focus our attention after this initial step. So those little green dots since the "clock" started are new highs in the A/D AT THE CLOSE since the accumulation phase began. Notice new highs are sometimes set 3 days later, some 8 days later....but all within 10 days of the previous "reset of the 10-day trading day clock", get it? Now, come mid November, AT THE CLOSE, we went 11 trading days without establishing a new high since the accumulation phase began, the Smart Money Sell trigger is in. Goodbye GE Vernova......for now. 😉 You can see in the price chart above the A/D line that the stock transitioned into Consolidation from Accumulation. You can also see that once we entered 2025, we transitioned back into Accumulation, which it is currently in......so we can "start the clock" again! 😄👍

Our second example, Rigetti Computing? See if you can figure this one yourself. I added self-explanatory text to chart so you can see how to handicap it. You can see how using this Money Flow Selling technique, you would have exited, Rigetti right after Halloween........and you would stay out of it - even through today. Why? Because we focus on the A/D line - the "smart" money. Yeah, we missed some price gain........but we also missed any future elevator drop. The INSIDERS just aren't buying it..........and neither should you! 😂🤣😅

This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️ 🌊

title: Selling Half of MDU Resources
Knowledge Transfer Series #57-Sector Rotation Sell
caption:
Added another 100 followers so here's the next Knowledge Transfer Series!

Been getting a lot of questions about the how/when/why to sell a security. So posted KTS #56 with the 50 Ways to Leave Your Lover (aka 50 Whys to Sell Your Stock! 😅). If you missed that post, you can catch up here:

https://share.blossomsocial.com/RCQYvFYCpiQuUhxM9

Also, following that post, I opened a POLL on which of the first 25 reasons to SELL you had a preference to hear about in detail for the next 5 Knowledge Transfer Series posts. The POLL is open for another 5 days, so not too late to get your votes in, if interested. Here's a link to that POLL:

https://share.blossomsocial.com/tMPX6k7LFZvX5SeQ6

OK, let's kick off the first of the SELLING posts. The first winner of the most votes is....The Sector Rotation Sell . So #6 gives special thanks to those that cast a vote for #6: @tackaberry17 , @figuringitout , @thedarkknight , @ninjaturtle , and @snieuwold . 😂👍

Before we dig in on this......I respectfully request that you READ and/or RE-READ KTS #2: Follow the $ector, which you can find here:

https://share.blossomsocial.com/wSRr7hzh4nj2TU8f9

Whew! That's a lot of internal links, Beskar!?!?! Correct. Now make sure to read them and quit skimming them so we can really dig in on this juicy meat!!!! 😅

Alright.....we're ready. Let's Go!

So remember that research shows that ~50% of a stock's performance is tied to the favorability of the industry's sector/subsector. The remaining ~50% of a stock's performance is tied to the business of the stock. Got it? Got it!

So that means that crappy companies can see their stock rise if the sector/subsector that they belong to is in favor with the wolves. A rising tide lifts all boats situation. However, if you are a well-performing company (KTS#17).....AND your sector/subsector is in favor, then the stock is going UP, UP, UP! 🤑🤑🤑

So most of those wolves subscribe to the thinking that in order to be successful in outperforming the S&P500 consistently, you must have a systematic method to buying/selling stocks and not to deviate from it........or else your outperformance will stop. My training as an engineer doesn't permit me to subscribe to this thinking (Sorry Wolves!), but for those that are hell bent in believing so.......#6 Sector Rotation Selling would be the buying/selling method I'd recommend.

Trend Strength and Industry Strength - that's it!

So the way this works, if both the Stock's Trend is STRONG and the Industry Strength is STRONG, you hold it (or accumulate more). If either one turns WEAK, you SELL. Pretty easy, huh?

Ok, ok.......I really like this idea. But where do you find whether the Trend and the Industry is Strong? Let's take the Industry first. You need to learn the tickers (punch them in some watchlist to track) of the industry sectors and subsectors. There's 11 sectors and 20 subsectors. You can find them here:

https://www.sectorspdrs.com/sectortracker

https://www.ssga.com/us/en/intermediary/capabilities/equities/sector-investing/gics-sector-and-industry-map

LOOK AT THE ONE-YEAR Chart. Read previous sentence again and don't forget it! :) As long as the sector ticker's CLOSING price (KTS #24) is ABOVE it's RISING 200-day Exponential Moving Average, the sector is STRONG. So check that box!

Now, we need to assess the TREND of the stock to make sure it's also ABOVE it's RISING 200-day EMA. If so, the TREND is also STRONG......and regardless of what the stock price is doing, we HOLD or ACCUMULATE the stock.

Conversely, if EITHER the INDUSTRY or the TREND is WEAK, we SELL and move into another stock that is STRONG in both......instead.

Excited yet?

Ok, let's give some examples. SEE ATTACHED CHARTS.

First up, a random stock pick from the vault that I know nothing about. $INTA-STOCK-US-USD-STOCK-US-USD Intapp, Inc. Some AI/CRM/Cloud Services company. So see if YOU can handicap it. INDUSTRY first. That's a software company.....so look at $XSW . The yellow line is the 200-day EMA. The mountain chart is the ETFs price chart. Notice for the past year, this subsector is STRONG. The few times it closed below the 200 DEMA (red circles), the DEMA was still RISING.......so that is NOT a trigger to SELL. So check that box! Next, let's look at the same for stock in question, $INTA . Here, when the stock's price closes below a FLAT to FALLING 200 DEMA (purple circles), the stock's TREND is WEAK, and you SELL. When it closes above a RISING 200 DEMA (green circle), it's a good candidate to own (hold or accumulate) - as long as the INDUSTRY is STRONG too. Make sense?

Now, look at the pink price movement in the stock in late September thru early October. The stock's price dropped from $51.19 down to $43.85 - that's a drop of 14.3% !?!?!? Yet......both the INDUSTRY AND the TREND are STRONG. So we would know........NOT to SELL! These are dip buying moments. Is this making sense?

Let's try another one.

Here, we'll check out Johnson & Johnson, $JNJ-STOCK-US-USD-STOCK-US-USD-STOCK-US-USD , a well-known Pharmaceuticals company. First step? That's right, check for INDUSTRY strength on the ONE-YEAR CHART. Looks STRONG from January to April 2024......and then again from 4th of July to Christmas. Otherwise, the Pharmaceutical Subsector is WEAK.....and we wouldn't consider $JNJ . Now, let's look at the stock chart itself. We could have bought it around the 4th of July as it CLOSED ABOVE a RISING 200 DEMA and held it all along while the INDUSTRY was STRONG too. But, we would have SOLD the stock in early November when $JNJ 's TREND went WEAK.....even though the INDUSTRY remained STRONG through Christmas. Get it?

Study these charts carefully and ask any questions.

This is a fantastic guide to help investors learning their way to step into the ART of SELLING stock.

🎶 🎵"You've got to know when to hold 'em
Know when to fold 'em
Know when to walk away
And know when to run

You never count your money
When you're sittin' at the table
There'll be time enough for countin'
When the dealin's done" 🎶 🎵

You want meat? You got it. Don't forget to vote on the next How/When/Why to SELL stocks!

This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️ 🌊 😅🤣😂

P.S. - We are presenting these Selling Techniques even though we are in FULL BUY mode for 2025! Learn now.....because we'll need to ready when the TIME comes.

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #56-50 Ways Leave Lover
caption:
Humor Me. 😅

Break out the vinyl and play the Simon & Garfunkel tune – all the way through.  Oops…..I mean search up Spotify. 😊

50 Ways to Leave Your Lover:

🎶🎵 "The problem is all inside your head", she said to me, The answer is easy if you take it logically,
I'd like to help you in your struggle to be free, There must be fifty ways to leave your lover.

She said it's really not my habit to intrude,
Furthermore, I hope my meaning won't be lost or misconstrued,
But I'll repeat myself at the risk of being crude,
There must be fifty ways to leave your lover”🎶 🎵

As we kickoff a mini series of posts within the Knowledge Transfer Series on the how, when, and why of SELLING securities, we have to know that there’s truly …… 50 Ways (Whys) to Leave (Sell) Your Lover (Stock)!  As this meltup phase starts to show signs of maturing ……we need to develop our approach to selling. (don’t worry…..we  still have a llllloooonnnnngggg way to the top if you want to rock ‘n roll). ⚡️ 🤑 🤘

And that approach to selling is multifaceted. We know markets aren’t driven by pure logic – otherwise we would use the formula for that. So while the algorithms try their best to incorporate as much math as possible into the solutioning, the bots have no answer to the human elements – things like emotion, interpretation, and greed..……which are the biggest driver of price changes.

Selling – like Buying - is as much of an Art as it is a Science.

No matter the field of study. Regardless of industry.  The world is not black and white.  It’s grey. 

That’s why we need lawyers after all. Ask a doctor.  ALL of that math and science education.  The MCAT, NRMP, and USMLEs. Countless numbers of specimens that have been worked on…..and fixed.  Yet?  They will ALL tell you………the profession is just as much Art……as Science. Next TIME you have a discussion with a doctor, ask him/her to confirm this.

OK Beskar, I know you love your music and metaphors. I follow what you’re saying.  But when are you going to give us that meat you always talk about?  We’re waiting. 

Hold on fool, don’t be a jit. ( https://www.urbandictionary.com/define.php?term=jit We can thank my kids for this!).😅🤣😂

I need to stress that there’s 50 Whys to Sell Your Stock. I can’t transfer 35+ years of investment knowledge about SELLING in a single post. So before we dig in on the mechanics of some of these 50 whys in the next few posts…….I’m using this post to list out 25 of the 50+ (that’s all I can fit in the post 😊) to start thinking about the many reasons why:

1) RSI Selling: Short-term Classic Oscillator Overbought/Oversold Selling

2) TAM Selling:  TAM - Total Addressable Market Limitation Selling that occurs when growth becomes value

3) FOMO Selling:  FOMO (Fear of Missing Out) Selling securities that fail to demonstrate healthy pullbacks on rapid rises

4) Tranche Selling: Selling out of positions in tranches

5) Pattern Selling: Selling out when a security confirms a bearish technical pattern or fails to confirm a bullish one.

6) Sector Rotation Selling: When a bullish sector/subsector turns bearish with INSIDERS (as defined by KTS #11)

7) Smart Money Selling: A security moves from the Accumulation phase into a Consolidation/Distribution phase.

8) Opportunity Cost Selling: Selling a security to allocate that capital into a different security that has a better chance to outperform

9) Risk Management Selling:  Selling to uphold self-imposed portfolio limits (e.g. – no more than 5% in crypto, 30% in precious metals, 20% in options, etc.).

10) Fraud Induced Selling: Cooked books, abrupt, unexplained leave of key executives

11) Single Secular Selling: A method of capturing upswings and exiting downdrafts in a single stock in the middle of a long-term climb

12) 15% Exit Selling: A method of exiting a security as soon as it results in a 15% return.

13) Stop Loss Selling:  A method of exiting a stock at a level set by the investor before the losses become unbearable and accelerate.

14) Rapid Gain Selling:  A method of selling that locks in >25% profits in any period less than one week.

15) Moat Selling: Selling a security when there is a significant decrease in the company’s competitive advantages.

16) Cycle Selling: Selling a security because it’s historical performance in past monthly, seasonal, economic, etc. cycles is underperforming.

17) Panic Selling:  Selling a security because everyone else is in a relative short-period of TIME.

18) Tax Harvest Selling: Selling securities that have dropped in value to offset capital gains in other securities sold to avoid paying taxes.

19) Liquidity Selling: Selling securities for the sole purpose of freeing up cash for expenses, regardless of outlook.

20) Dead Money Selling:  Selling a security because it has been in an extended period of consolidation and shows no signs of recovery or INSIDER interest.

21) Downward Earnings Estimate Revision Selling: When the collective analysts forward earnings estimates are lowered as a result in reduced business activity.

22) Money Flow Selling:  KTS #25 flashes and the security has moved from a position of significant money inflows to a money outflows.

23) Relative Performance vs. SPY Selling: KTS #50 flashes as a stock that had been outperforming the SPY starts consistently underperforming the SPY.

24) Short Selling: Selling a security before you actually own it…….as you expect the price to decline…….for the sole purpose of buying it back at a lower price to profit off the difference.

25) House Money Selling: Selling a security as soon as it hits 100% Return and then selling out your original capital investment amount…..letting the profits run and compound into more profits.

So you wanted to know the 50 Ways to Leave Your Lover, eh?  😊

Well here’s my Top 25.  Read. Digest. Read again.  And then we’ll dig in on some of these.  

If interested, track my trades to see the when/why I sell.  I always give a rationale.

Is that the meat you wanted, jit? 😂

This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️ 🌊

P.S. - Blossomers, I am getting 100 new follower increments so rapidly (up to 2,600) that I’m having to crank out these Knowledge Transfer Series like candy just to stay true to my promise!  All caught up now…….but stay tuned as the bots are on to me!

title: Well, Well, Well.....What Do You Know, Hawkeye ?
caption:
I just did it for you this year. 😂

Next year, we'll do it together. 👍

After that, you're on your own. 🎣

From Knowledge Transfer Series #52, Do U Hear What I Hear suggested exercise, I went ahead and included the summary table for the New Year Rotation....which goes from the weeks BEFORE Christmas to AFTER through the first trading week in January.

Key Observations in the "CHANGE in SHARE" of 52-Week Highs:

* The Energy Sector captured the highest rotation (dark green) from 1% to 9% (+8%) of the 52-week high pool. 😉🤑🤑🤑

* Other significant POSITIVE sector rotations (green) were Healthcare (+7%), Materials (+7%), and Information Technology (+5%).

* Most sectors stayed held their respective allocation (yellow to orange): Consumer Staples, Communication Services, Industrials, Utilities, Consumer Discretionary, and Real Estate.

* The Financial Sector had the biggest drop (-30%).

Key Observations in the "ABSOLUTE SHARE" of 52-Week Highs:

* Despite the significant drop in share, Financials still hold 23% of all stocks making 52-Week Highs.

* Utilities and Real Estate each comprise a mere 2% of stocks hitting 52-Week Highs.

* All other sectors are virtually the same in their share (between 6% and 11%).

Also noteworthy is that the number of stocks hitting 52-week highs jumped from 388 stocks to 1,227 stocks - a jump of 216% !!! So while the talking heads blab about the lack of a Santa Claus rally..........we know better........the miracle is real.......and Santa works in mysterious ways. 🎅

So while your fellow INVESTORS (as defined by KTS #11) talk about stockpiling cash, talk about valuations, and fear an imminent recession amongst the unknowns of the next administration.............HAVE NO FEAR and rotate with the EARLY INSIDERS (as defined by KTS #11).

We got this. 🤑👍

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #55 - The Mental Edge
caption:
Since 2018.......I've been able to laugh about it. 😅🤣😂

But it took awhile........many years........to develop the ULTIMATE Mental Edge.

Like a recovery addict, the first step is acknowledgement. Then talk about it. So I share this with you so you can learn from it - to develop your toolset - and most importantly....... not make the same mistake. It's why - in most markets - I primarily INVEST......and not trade. If it does happen to you, I hope this gives you the psychological power needed to recover and press on to victory....versus wallow in self pity.....and quit.

At this stage of the meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle, we've mentioned before......the need to take profits to reduce debts.......and improve the health of our bodies. 2025 will be most amazing and it'll be easy to forget this sage advice.......but the true winners, with the iron guts, and the strong mentality to deal with market blows......will have their personal finances and personal health in top shape. You have about one year from now to get in that place of holistic health.

OK, let's get back to this event you learned from, @beskar_capital ! What are you trying to tell us exactly?

Well.....It's shown me the light on how to SELL......which is just as much of an art & science as how to BUY. I know we've been doing A LOT of BUYING recently (and will continue to do so through 2025). But I hear a lot of questions about how/when to SELL. Those that follow me regularly, know a lot of my strategy there......but I will dedicate the next few KTS on it......since we just posted Knowledge Transfer Series #54 - Buy & Sold.......a TIMELY tool that bleeds into Trader World......for about 6 months or so. Maybe give it a re-read?

But the ultimate lesson learned from what I am about to share...........is how to not dwell on the goal just given up. Like a losing hockey goalie or a struck out baseball batter.....you can learn from how it happened to avoid it the next TIME......but the psychology MUST be to constantly look forward to the next opportunity. There is - and will ALWAYS be - an amazing investment opportunity right in front of you.

OK.....like a New Year's resolution.......I share this real life story. Let's jump in:

Mighty Beskar is a mere human (I think I have to tell this in 3rd person! 😅). And when it comes to markets, he is a mere INVESTOR (as defined by KTS #11). In October 2007, Beskar's study of the real estate/banking crisis cycle benefitted him tremendously in preparing for the market peak and the subsequent downturn. Beskar had his family finances in a sound place.....and his personal health was solid. The overall market (not individual stocks 😉) started to tank. Interest rates dropped like a rock. While neighbors used the low rate environment to reduce their 30-yr mortgage to 15-yr with the same monthly payment......Beskar UNConventionally refinanced into a NEW 30-year fixed to lower the monthly payment even more..........and use the difference to invest in the upcoming bottom of the barrel stock price environment. No regrets. It was masterful in my opinion.

Come 2009, Beskar started buying all kinds of monster stocks at less than $10 a share that just skyrocketed.......a fish in a barrel situation......an environment that will most likely return around the dark days of the rock bottom ~October 2028. But Beskar's TIMING - while not precise overall....was accurate......and he benefitted tremendously.

But one incredible mistake was made. I swear this is a true story. (Take a deep breath, Beskar. 😅 ).

On March 9th, 2009, I purchased 1,000 shares of $NVDA for around $9.00 per share after applying KTS #20, following a favorite stock investment fund that has an amazing record of early entries into amazing performing stocks. Later in September of that year, the stock doubled.......and I SOLD OUT of ALL 1,000 shares. 100% return in 6 months? Can't argue with that right?

Had I not sold out of that position, I would be sitting on a 60,940% return over 15 years.....or an annualized return of 4,063%. It would be worth nearly $5.5 million today. (Shit....I can't believe I just typed this.😅🤮 ).

But I can laugh about it today. Now I just sing Kelly Clarkson's 🎶🎵"What doesn't Kill You Makes You Stronger" 🎶 🎵 whenever I think about it. 😂🤣

Naturally, this "trade" has had a profound impact on me. I've developed a drive like no other. I have an iron gut to withstand any indigestion. But most importantly, I've developed the Mental Edge.

Before you feel pity for me..(ok....too much pity for me 🤣)......know that I've had several absolutely ridiculous wins since then (but not to that level :
😂). And I'm here to share my experiences with you..........so you can avoid making the same mistakes, learn the how and when of buying/selling, and of course, how to TIME markets successfully to consistently realize absolutely amazing double-digit returns, handily outperforming the S&P500, year after year.

For those new to the Knowledge Transfer Series, I post a new one every 100 new followers. If interested in learning from my 34+ years of investing experience, I strongly suggest you start with my first post - self-proclaimed greatest post in Blossom history 😅😊 - on Blossom in March 2024 titled "They are telling us so what are you waiting for" post (type in Blossom search window) ......... and then move into the Knowledge Transfer Series posts #1 thru #55 here today.......and beyond.

We will start talking about how @beskar_capital SELLS positions in some of these upcoming posts. It's a product of research knowledge gleaned from the truly greatest investors/traders/economists of ALL-TIME.......and it's not the oft-regurgitated names that circulate.

I promise - as always - to give you my absolute best.

Welcome to the New Year. 2025 will be the most profitable year of the 2020-2030 decade. But we need to start expanding our toolset to develop THE MENTAL EDGE.

This is the Way!

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #54 - Buy & Sold
caption:
Forget what you know to be right......

........for the next 6 months.

Those that follow me know I am primarily a fundamental investor that seeks to understand valuation......but has an appreciation for the other legs of the stool: technicals, order/money flows, and TIME.

@beskar_capital analyzes companies AFTER a deep analysis of candidates using the tools in the Knowledge Transfer Series. We know over TIME.....that ALL STOCKS.....will move with their earnings (KTS #17).

Those that follow me also know that we are constantly identifying where we are in the real estate/ banking crisis cycle and make moves to TIME markets to successfully, and consistently achieve double-digit annualized returns to outperform the S&P500.

And it's this consideration of the cycle......that far outweighs any consideration of valuation.......for the next 6 months.

Read that previous paragraph again.

I am an INVESTOR (KTS #11) - NOT a trader. But......I am also a constant observer of the TYPE OF MARKET we are in. And this one....in the 2nd stage of the meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle (next 6 months)......is one to rotate gains. Use your Knowledge Transfer Series toolbox to identify candidates and move in......then move on.

Buy & Hold.....becomes Buy & Sold.....for the next 6 months to start 2025.

Why is this so and how do YOU know, Beskar?

Great question. Because the real estate/banking crisis behavior is repeatable and on a playbook calendar.

Here's why. First, Baby Boomer cash investments (known as M2 money measure by the Fed, which is CDs, T-bills, money market funds, etc.) are pouring back into the market. Bank deposits have dropped from $20.5 trillion to $18 trillion today. But those 5% yields will continue to drop to 3% - 3.5%......and EVEN LOWER. So the BULL will be sustained......per real estate/banking crisis cycle predicted behavior. 😉

Additionally, with inflation remaining an issue and poised for a resurgence, this new come to market money is in full FOMO mode. The toy-generation Baby Boomers are jealous of these returns (imagine that! 😂🤣😅). You know the ones.....hundreds of percent within months that we've been demonstrating? 🤑🤑🤑

So what happens now? Check out the Advance/Decline line for any of the indicies. Since April 2023, the line in Russell3000 has been on long-term climb of consistent slope. This "broadening" of the market will ramp even more in the coming year......which means that ..... more and more....and more companies will start getting bids......as Investors (KTS#11) coming into the party seek out those returns you've been logging.......in tickers that haven't moved yet. This phenomenon will occur regardless of fundamentals. But......many of these turnarounds.....will have improving earnings estimates so possible to buffer these candidates.

Indeed it is TIME to SMILE (Reread KTS #45).

So what tickers to target? Low-priced stocks.

Typically, low-priced stocks (called "penny" stocks for anything trading less than $5.00. This is also a level that is a barrier to many institutions that have set rules that they can't buy penny stocks. But guess what happens now at this stage? 🤑🤑🤑😅

That's right, penny stocks come out of penny range.....and the institutions come flying in to buy these stocks! So start watching stocks in the $4.00 range......and how they get gobbled up once they CLOSE above the $5.00 range. Guess what happens to the stock price then? 🚀 😂

This brings me to another recommended Knowledge Transfer source - the Motley Fool, founded by brothers David & Tom Gardner. While their bread and butter is a focus on the forward-looking VALUATION metric PEG (Price to Earnings Growth ratio)....that matters in most markets.....it's their breakdown of low-priced stocks that we apply here now.

They consider $10-$20 stocks safe from a risk standpoint but a loonnngggg runway of growth ahead of it. A $1.00 price increase on a $10.00 stock is 10% return in no TIME! A lot different than $1.00 price change on a $30 stock (a modest 3.3% return).

But in this market? One where low-priced warrants are flying.....options trading hitting all-time highs? We want to see "how low can you go" 😅🤣😂 for the next 6 months!!! Remember, the reason we like options......is because they JUICE the returns over the underlying stock movements. Why? Because they carry a LOWER PRICE! It's a way to bet on a company trading at $20, $30, $50, $100, or more........for pennies! And movements in these pennies....are HIGH PERCENTAGES. Don't forget how we get returns......it's PERCENTAGES! 😅🤣😂🤑🤑🤑

So look to low-priced stocks that are starting to SMILE. Use KTS# 9, #23, #25, #45, #47, and #50 - especially.

These amazing returns can come in 1- 2 months. Lock in the profits as this hysteria sets in....and redeploy the capital into others. This is a TIME when you can realize a decade of returns within 2 years!!! Not kidding! 🤑🤑🤑

Opportunities will be everywhere. Anything at a 52-week high......MUST be considered. If also under $10.00 also.....lookout! 🤑🤑🤑 Any Conventionalist that tries to get in your head about valuation and/or avoiding a momentum market.......FOR THE NEXT 6 MONTHS......should be avoided at all costs! 😅🤣😂

It is going to get super crazy. My screens (KTS#??) are showing all kinds of Chinese small cap stocks waking up after a long hibernation. Crypto is going to reaccelerate in a way most couldn't possibly imagine. Options Activity will SKYROCKET. Penny stocks will start to FLY.....these Baby Boomers will chase anything.....and most everything.

Knowing what kind of market you're in is key. Use the right tool for the right job. This next 6 months is tough for INVESTORS (as defined by KTS #11) who prefer fundamentals .... but know......this is not the market we are in. So WRONG TOOL!!!😅🤣😂

Welcome to 2025.....it's a momentum, stock pickers market among the collection of low-priced stocks. It lasts for ~6 months. So don't scold it. Embrace it. And enjoy it! 😅🤣😂🤑🤑🤑

This is the Way! 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️

P.S. - I owe 2 Knowledge Transfer Series posts as I write one for every 100 new followers. Very grateful for the acknowledgement by @maxstocks and the list of most TIME spent....so I am getting these out! An honor to be included with that group in the TOP 10. Thank you all. Not even a year in Blossom and have so many followers. My mission is to help young investors WIN now........and position to continue winning ahead of the collapse that comes in 4-6 quarters from now following the peak in the real estate/banking crisis cycle. Let's do it!!!
