title: Knowledge Transfer Series #34 - Buying Put Options
caption:
This is a follow on to KTS posts #30, #31, #32, #33, and #33A. Those MUST be read first prior to reading this as it builds off those posts.

OK, so we talked about BUYING CALL Options in KTS #33/#33A as a way to shop for coupons (contracts) as a way to speculate on STOCKS GOING UP. But what if we want to speculate on STOCKS GOING DOWN?

The option type to do this is called BUYING A PUT OPTION. How does it work? Here, you pay a cost (premium) for the right - or option - to SELL a stock at a set price (strike) before the expiration date (expiration). So just the opposite of Buying a Call option.

WHEN TO BUY A PUT OPTION:
(1) When you want to speculate on the downside of stocks. A great alternative to shorting stock.
(2) A way to buy CHEAP INSURANCE against a stock you currently have in your portfolio that has made a great run! Reread this one again for those of you that are more conservative and like to hedge and manage your risks.
(3) Breaking support of consolidation into a descending channel pattern. Reminer to reference KTS #29 and memorize these patterns!!!
(4) One sledge hammer (pennant, reverse flag patterns) ALMOST ALWAYS gives a second hammer (dead cat bounce). If these terms are new to you.....I said reference KTS #29! 😅
(5) At the apex of a rising Wedge pattern - especially in the late stages. Also in a busted momentum trend. Think tired Tech Transition (KTS #18).
(6) When VIX < 20

WORST CASE: You pay a one-time premium and the Put option expires worthless because the stock never drops enough.

So essentially you are looking to acquire (buy by paying a premium) coupons (contracts) that permit you to SELL a product, like beer, (stock) at a fixed price (strike) before the expiration date (expiration). So imagine if you had a coupon (contract) giving you the right to SELL a case of beer for $30 and the current market price for a case was $24, then you could make $6 on the difference.

Now you are asking....wait a second, why the hell would someone be willing to take my coupon (put option contract) for $30 case of beer when the current market price is $24 !?!?! The answer is.....because they HAVE TO. With options, they call them contracts for a reason. You must remember - for any option (call or put, buy or sell), it is a transaction between two parties. One will win. One will lose. When you BUY a PUT option contract, it is because someone "on the other side of the table" sold it to you. You are speculating the underlying stock is going down while they are speculating the stock goes up. Get it? So when you choose to use your coupon (exercise your option contract), you get to sell 100 shares of the underlying stock..and they are obligated to BUY it at the strike price in the contract. Of course they aren't happy if the current market price of the stock is way below the strike in the contract....but they entered in the contract so they have to. 😂 Imagine getting a note that you have to buy 100 shares of Nvidia for $150 a share while the current market price is $115 per share!

Again, this isn't complicated. Buying a PUT is just the opposite of buying a CALL - you are acquiring a contract that allows you to SELL a stock at a fixed strike price instead of BUY a stock at a fixed strike price. So you are speculating that the stock will go down and you profit off the difference between the strike price and the current market price.

Again, there will be a Knowledge Transfer Series #34A to review potential candidate BUYING a PUT option for stocks I think are headed down by the expiration date.

Here is one company I am considering:

Their negative, massive FCF is projected to decline even further in coming year...and this is if all goes "well". Maybe @bradleytalksmoney can give us a balance sheet review?

I am going to look into other companies but this is tougher in a runaway, irrational bull market. BUT, you can imagine that as we near the completion of the real estate/banking crisis cycle, this is absolutely one of the most profitable (easiest?) ways to juice your returns just before the elevator drop.

Remember, rising markets take the escalator. Falling markets take the elevator. 😉😅👍

Puts are a great way to make loads of profit just ahead of a bear market...5-7 quarters from now. And those that don't know how to read, listen to, and successfully TIME markets keep buying with euphoric complacency.. while we carefully position by BIYING PUT options at ultra cheap prices! 😅🤣😂😉👍

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #33- Buying Call Options
caption:

Rapid-fire OPTIONS post week within KTS. Let's roll.

Continuing our grocery store coupon analogy, BUYING a CALL Option contract(s) is simply buying a grocery store coupon (contract) for a cost (premium). It gives us the right, or option - but not obligation - to buy (using the coupon, or exercising an option) a product like beer (unerlying stock) at a fixed price (strike) that may be lower than current market prices (called "in the money", ITM) or higher than the current market price (called "out of the money", OTM) before the coupon (contract) expiration date (expiration).

Contract, Premium, Underlying Stock, Strike, and Expiartion.

Ha ha, you finance gurus that want to make the "complex derivative security market" so complicated - I just coherently explained your jargon-laden "expertise" in layman's terms in one simple sentence! Take that! 😅🤣😂

Different phases of the real estate/banking crisis cycle are optimal for different types of options. We cover BUYING A CALL first because it is a perfect type for this stage of the meltup phase.

SO WHEN to BUY a CALL OPTION?
1) When you want to speculate on the upside of stocks while minimizing risk and adding a pop to your portfolio.
2) Breaking out of consolidation into an ascending channel pattern...or long-term rising channel.
3) One Bull Flag pole ALMOST ALWAYS gives a second Bull Flag pole. 😉
4) The apex of a Falling Wedge pattern.
5) When the VIX < 20

WORST CASE: You pay a one-time premium and the CALL expires worthless because the stock doesn't rise enough.

Reread KTS#29 to get your paaterns ingrained in the membrane.

Whoa... shoot! This is like an answer key!?!?
I told you the Knowledge Transfer Series is where you get juicy meat falling off the bone! 😂

HOW BUYING A CALL OPTION WORKS:
You pay a cost (premium) for a coupon (contract) that gives you the right to purchase a product (stock) at a set price (strike) before the expiration date (expiration).

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #32-Anatomy of an Option
caption:
This is a follow on to KTS #30 & #31. Both must be read prior to reading this post.

Let's get right into it! In the stock market, we buy/sell "shares" of a particular company. In the Options market, we buy/sell "contracts" in a particular company. EVERY single options contract, regardless of the company, is equivalent to 100 shares of the particular company. So anyone holding one contract has the right to purchase 100 shares of that company at a set price. To recap,

1 contract = 100 shares
2 contracts = 200 shares
5 contracts = 500 shares
100 contracts = 10,000 shares

You get it.

Now, think of an options contract like a grocery store coupon. A grocery store coupon entitles you to buy, say a case of beer, say for $12..before the coupon expiration date. Since a case of beer currently costs $24 without a coupon, a coupon to buy it for $12 - hey, that's a pretty good deal!

Another component to an options contract is the expiration date. This is the day the options contract becomes worthless. Again, thinking of an options contract like a grocery store coupon, there's an expiration date there too! So if my $12 for a case of beer coupon expires in two weeks at the end of July, I better use it before then to save some 💰..otherwise I have to go back to buying beer at the same price as everyone else that doesn't have a coupon at $24/case. 🫤

The key takeaway here is that the coupon itself has VALUE - what someone is willing to pay for it! So think of the options market as a Coupon Trader Marketplace! 😂 If I had 10 coupons for $12 per case beer, I could SELL these coupons (before they expired) to other shoppers..and save the same amount of money, or more...without ever even having to buy beer at all! The value of each coupon is $11.99 (any buyer would save a penny with a coupon)..until it expires..on the expiration date.. worthless. Here, the buyer is willing to pay $11 for the coupon and then the $12 for the case, for a total of $23 - saving money! In the options market, this $11 above is called the "premium" and the fixed price ($12 for a case) is called the "strike price". That's not too hard to understand, right?

Now imagine I had 5 different grocery coupons (contracts) for beer:

#1: $12 /case with an expiration date of August 1, 2024
#2: $6 /case expiring September 1, 2024
#3: $20 /case expiring September 1, 2024
#4: $24 /case expiring September 1, 2027
#5: $25 /case expiring September 1, 2030

By owning these coupons (contracts), I have the right, or option, to buy cases of beer at a fixed price anytime before each coupon expires. Easy enough. But let's say - instead - I wanted to SELL all 5 of my coupons above? Without looking below yet, how would you rank the coupons (contracts) in terms of value to a willing buyer?

#2, #1, #5, #3, #4

How?

I bet I could sell coupon #1 for $11 (premium). Why? Considering the current price without a coupon is $24, a $12 (strike price) coupon (contract) expiring soon in just 2 weeks on August 1, 2024, someone will buy my coupon (contact) for $11 to save $1 over everyone else buying a case.

I could probably fetch $17 (premium) for coupon #2. Similarly to coupon #1, the buyer could save $1 if he had this coupon. The buyer's total cost for the case is the cost of the coupon, $17 (premium) + the cost of the case granted by the coupon, $6 (strike price), or $23 for a case compared to everyone else without coupons paying $24. Get it? Go thru the example again if not clear enough.

I could find someone to buy coupon #3 off me for $3. This coupon (contract) is worth less because at $20 (strike) for a case, is pretty close to the price without any coupon.

I could probably get $2 for coupon #4. While this coupon (contract) locks in the exact price for what a case of beer currently goes for without a coupon, it doesn't expire until 3 years from now!?! So I can lock in today's price per case for beer I buy in the summer of 2027!! With speculation of inflation looks like, I can understand why some buyer's would be willing to pay $2 (premium) for a coupon (contract) that is for the same price as today. Get it?

I could probably get $6 (premium) for coupon #5. Wait, how? Even though the $25 (strike) coupon (contract) is for $1 more than the current price of a case ($24), it doesn't expire for 6 years from now! Surely a case of beer will cost ALOT more than $25, 6 years from now, if it's currently $24 ?

So this is how the options market works: a coupon marketplace. Those that hold the coupons (contracts) have the right to either use the coupon (called "exercising the option" before it expires) or they can trade the paper coupon (contract) for a cost (premium). The value of these coupons (contracts) fluctuates wildly as buyers/sellers speculate about the future price of beer (stock). This can really juice your returns compared to stock investing without putting up much capital (minimize risk).

Next, we dig in on Call Options.

This is the Way! 🍻

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #31- Option Ground Rules
caption:
The next 5 posts after this one is an attempt to demystify options. Please read KTS #30 before reading on.

This is a great TIME within the cycle (meltup phase) to understand how to use options are derivatives of stock securities that were designed to MINIMIZE risk and JUICE RETURNS.

Before we start, here are the ground rules:

(1) UNDER NO CIRCUMSTANCE - EVER - AS IN NEVER - WILL ANYONE INFER THAT @beskar_capital is suggesting, recommending, or inferring for anyone to EVER SELL an Uncovered Call Option. I NEVER will. Selling Uncovered Call Options is extremely risky (especially in the meltup phase of a runaway bull market). You can lose your shirt, your house, and many have even taken their life. 😪 Again, I will NEVER suggest in any way selling an Uncovered Call Option.

(2) As you read these next 5 posts, it may be tempting to run out and take action right away. When trading options, it is recommended that you baby step in with 2 contracts maximum until you watch at least 20 option transactions play out from cradle to grave.

(3) Volatility: When you check on the daily return on an options contract you own, be prepared to see -63% last week, +19% this week, or over monthly basis, -89% or +440%. These are real types of option returns..and thus we baby step in with just 1 or 2 contracts until we fortify the gut! 😆

(4) Locking in ~15-20% gains in less than one month (or even within the day you bought it). It is my experience that my option contracts, will present themselves with an UNREALIZED return more often than not of > +15% at some point while you are holding it (for the type of options we cover). For those that can develop the ability to ROUTINELY lock in those types of gains and sell/buy the contract(s) once it flashes those kind of returns, the potential exists for incredible repeatable performance. The reality is that it will be too tempting to hold it hoping for +15% to quickly turn into +120% return in a few days. This does happen - frequently enough that you hold - and then it swings to -34%..and never recovers. So, a good habit is (sports analogy) to learn how to routinely hit singles and doubles vs. swinging for the fences. We are human...and we make mistakes with emotion...so this WILL trip you up. Once you hit a grand slam (e.g. - make a $2,000 profit in less than a month on a $200 investment) you will likely want to keep swinging for the parking lot...when you should "go with the pitch" and take the easier singles and doubles that routinely present themselves. After all, investing is all about discipline, eh?

(5) That said in (4) above, there are times when we hold unrealized gains on an options contract even if the unrealized gain is +634%!?!!? If the thesis is still intact, I like to lock in my original investment amount by selling/buying enough contracts to cover it, and let the rest run (house's money).

(6) The options market is a much smaller market with much, much fewer participants. Typically the shrewdest, most cunning, smartest wolves are running in it. Think about a dark forest with lots of howling around you. 😂 But at this phase in the real estate/banking crisis cycle, boat loads of hares and deer come into these woods. We come in too..armed and ready atop a tree in a deer stand. Reread KTS #30 as to why we would consider coming into this woodlands.

(7) We will not be intimidated by the terminology or vocabulary around options. We refuse to be deterred in upstanding how they work and promise to be persistent in our learning.

Whenever you come across words like strike, expiration, TIME decay, contract, put, call, derivatives, etc...just substitute a common, comfortable word that means the same thing. The mechanics of how they work is not complicated. But when they throw those new vocabulary words around while we are trying to learn the mechanics of it, it can fluster us. So we won't let that happen.

(8) Ask questions. That's how learning best takes place.

(9) Even if you decide not to engage in the derivative market, it is worth your TIME to learn how the options market works. Because - in typical times - when there's no newbies running around in it trying to get rich - the options market is an incredible forecasting tool that you can read to get better estimates of where stock prices are really going to go in the coming days, weeks, months, and even years. Read the previous sentence again. Strongly recommend for your stock picking..that you learn how to read and time the market for stocks you own. Wall Street knows this trick..and routinely reference the options market before making investment decisions in stocks - e en if they will never participate in the options market. 😉

(10) We will cover the mechanics of how basic options work only. I believe the KTS will add incredible value by outlining the WHEN & BEST type of option for select patterns.

I think that's it. This will be fun...if you are willing to engage.

This is the Way. 😎 🏄‍♂️

caption:
We made it!!! We are finally here.

Up to this point we have assembled an amazing set of Knowledge Transfer Series tools to UNconventionally identify investment opportunities (KTS #2,3,6,7,8,9,13,15,16,26,27, and 28), handicap fundamentals using quick screeners focusing on increasing earnings and free cash flow (KTS #17 and 28), lower risk by layering KTS tool after tool following INSIDERS using money flow analysis (KTS #12,14,19,20, 24, and 25), finding trend inflection points for entry/exit points using technical analysis (KTS #9,10/10A,18,21,22,23, and 29) - all to achieve absolutely amazing double-digit annualized returns as we constantly navigate and TIME THE MARKETS within the meltup phase of this runaway bull market stage of the real estate/banking crisis cycle.

Now we are at KTS#30 - Options. This will be presented over the next 5 KTS posts in an attempt to demystify the complexity that Wall Street wants you to think it is.

Why now @beskar_capital? Because this is the TIME in the cycle. How so? What will start happening here is the realization and acknowledgement - by every investor - that we are in a BULL market. Did you see yesterday's news of 19-year JPM veteran equity strategist who called for a Bull market in 2022 and Bear market since - doubling, tripling, and quadruplets down on his bets - just threw up his hands and quit (or canned 😅)? This is like clockwork in the meltup of the RE/BC cycle. Next up, as the 2nd half of the year progresses, expect to see all the Baby Boomer cash and money market funds to start FOMOing in to equities. And it's for this reason that we bring Options into our KTS toolset now. Get ready for ultracrazy valuations, ridiculous speculations, and just plain stupid stock price movements that make you question whether fundamental analysis is even part of the investment thesis equation anymore. Still, as a means to participate in these stock run ups....and eventual collapses, we will keep fundamental analysis close ..and eventually even closer as we get nearer and nearer the cycle completion 5-7 quarters from now ... so the rip currents don't take us out to sea. These tasty waves have been building under the water's surface and we need to prepare for the maximum thrill experience - and options can be a Kowabunga! 🏄‍♀️ 🌊 🏄‍♂️

Of course Conventionalists will tell you Options are risky.....when in fact the opposite is true. Options are derivatives (alternative investment vehicles of the same underlying stock) that were created to actually LESSEN RISK. It's true. With options, you are actually investing a smaller amount of capital that has the ability to "juice" your returns in a short period of TIME. As we go through the subsequent KTS tools, there is a time decay in all options and higher volatility than in stocks themselves (for shorter-dated options) that need to be accounted for. We will cover these - but for now, let's lay the groundwork for why considering options - especially at this point in the cycle - is worthwhile.

First, let's look at the who. In the stock market, we've come to accept that it is a shrewd arena where one's gains come at the expense of another's losses. INSIDERS (as defined by KTS#11) dominate the daily trading volume (78%) and their aggregated actions "control" price movements. They accumulate positions in after hours, protect their investments through analyst upgrades, and flash crash stocks as a way to accumulate even more at lower prices...only to dump them all at a later point for juicy profits in a short period of TIME.

In the Options market - it's shrewd on steroids - it is a best of breed - most predatory wolves that don't carry even an ounce of delayed gratification traits. These ravenous beasts make make day traders look like investors. Wait Beskar, why on Earth would I want to enter the options world at this time in what sounds like a Roman Colesseum without being on par with these adversaries!?!?! Two reasons: (1) Because a flood of newbies come into this arena in this part of the cycle who aren't wolves and do so without a proper toolset, and (2) Because the predatory wolves - with their closely spaced, beady eyes - can't see .... and don't care to look at any potential meat to eat farther out than a couple of months. They completely ignore a special type of option derivative investment vehicle - LEAPS. LEAPS are Long-term Equity AnticiPation Securities. Don't let these pea-brains deter you with their jargon - they are just long-dated options that INVESTORS can use to anticipate longer term price chart trends (ever hear of Beskar giving weight to 1-yr, 3-yr charts in investment decisions? 😉).

The wolves don't care about LEAPS.....until it comes within their feeding TIMEframe, which is typically a quarter, or <3 months. So golden opportunity to get in ahead of the wolves interest...and when they come? 😅🤣😂 Talk about juicy returns while we manage risk.

Keep an open mind.

This is the Way!

title: TRUMP 😡 vs. BIDEN 💀
caption:
Ok, ok. We've done a pretty good job of suppressing discussion of politics on Blossom.

And I'm not going to fuel it either. But those that follow me know to constantly apply the macroeconomic tools (KTS posts) we developed, step back, and observe how they play out in stocks. We've come to know that the market provides insight 3 to 9 months in advance - and if we just drop our arrogant approach of thinking we can make markets by investing in our own hopes - but listen to the market - we can make profit in amazing ways.

I'm certain by now everyone has heard some sort of recap of the U.S. Presidential debate.....if you didn't watch it live. The market did.....and placed their bets. Supposed "Trump" stocks turned in a gain of ~3+% and "Biden" stocks were trounced in Friday's session following the debate. ( https://www.msn.com/en-us/money/markets/last-call-panel-discusses-market-reaction-to-presidential-debate/vi-BB1p5Kwf)

The market is placing its bets.

An arrogant investor will blow this off as inconsequential. An intelligent investor will tune in and follow the money (KTS #25).

What is likely to happen from now until the end of the calendar year is positioning by Wall Street into unloved stocks that will reverse course if there's a change in leadership. The level of these gains can forecast the outcome. So pay attention!!!!

But more importantly, these are some of the biggest, tastiest waves you will ever see in the super speculative meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle as it completes. The conditions are peeeerrrrffffeeeeeccccttttt! 😎 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️

For those that follow me, it is important to note that the real estate/banking crisis cycle completes on TIME, every TIME - regardless of who becomes President. But running up to the election and beyond.....if there's a change in leadership, then there will be a change in policy. And a change in policy will have a profound impact on the stocks that have been unloved for 4 years. So why not accumulate alongside the smart money instead of waiting until the ballot count? This is a rare moment in our investing lives.......a meltup phase coinciding with a Presidential election. This is where the great stock pickers make the most. So if you want to really profit in this rare event, expand your tickers, Blossomers and start looking at the stocks waking up! THEY ARE EVERYWHERE!!!!!

Last, if someone asked me if I could only pick a single stock as an indicator for forecasting the Presidential election in this meltup phase? It would be $NAK . There's no fundamental analysis to be done here. This is PURELY speculative and it's performance going forward is essentially a referendum on reducing the Permitting Policies. The higher it rises above 30 cents per share, the more likely Trump will win. The lower it goes below 30 cents per share, the more likely Biden (or his likely replacement 😅) will win.

You can do your own due diligence. I'm never going to tell you to buy or sell a stock. But I will share my buys and sells for anyone interested to see how I position and TIME markets. I saw the movement in $NAK on Friday. It's likely just the beginning - and I will be buying right next to the wolves. 😂

Watch this one ticker indicator and skip all those biased, conflicting polling results.

$NAK will tell you who is really going to win the Presidential election.

The sooner you learn that the INSIDERS (as defined by KTS #11) know way more than we do and act on it far in advance of it becoming public knowledge, the greater your portfolio returns will be.

The market is constantly speaking to us. Learn to read, listen, and successfully TIME the markets.

title: The Real Estate/Banking Crisis is Fully Intact
caption:
The meltup phase of this runaway bull market will continue, on TIME, everytime, as always. Enjoy it!!!! We are now at 5-7 quarters left before the market starts to reel. That leaves plenty of TIME to enjoy the broadening of the market into small and mid cap, 2nd tier boring AI-related, cryptos, forex, and especially options. Oh my, this is going to be fun!!!! 😅

As we progress through this final period of nonsensical euphoria (which you cannot afford to sit and watch in amazement while you cope with inflated prices of everything), what lurks and lingers in the background is continuing to gain momentum - CRE, or Commercial Real Estate loans.

Yesterday, you may have heard the great news (😂) that all 31 banks passed the Federal Reserve's stress test - a "CRE Shock Scenario". This is a drill where the Fed models a severe recession. Specifically, it tests select big banks balance sheets' ability to survive:

1) a 36% decline in U.S. home prices.
2) a 55% drop in equity prices.
3) a rise in unemployment to 10%.

@beskar_capital wants you to remember these input numbers. Why? Because the Fed uses these past averages from prior real estate/banking crisis cycles to model the next one. And they will repeat this "stress test" monthly ........ until the cycle completes. Ingrain those numbers in your head (36%, 55%, and 10%) and marry that with 5-7 quarters from now so YOU can successfully TIME the markets.

The U.S. Case-Schiller Home Price index is melting up alongside the markets too. See attached. This unsustainability has long passed the point of any possibility of a soft landing. It has NEVER happened in the real estate/banking crisis cycle. As the equity markets rise, the spending of this new found wealth will likewise accompany it....snowballing even bigger and bigger....until it ends. Truly a runaway bull market that ALWAYS comes in the final phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle.

Sifting through the article and pulling the key takeaways:

-The 31 banks tested have sufficient capital to absorb $685 billion in CRE losses. 👍

-Vacancy rates in office buildings have past historic peaks of 20% 👎

-$929 billion of $4.7 TRILLION CRE mortgages come due in 2024 👎

- The rest comes due in subsequent years after that. 👎👎👎

- % of CRE on the loan books? Goldman, RBC USA, Capital One, and Northern Trust were projected at 16%, 16%, 15%, and 13%. In the last real estate/banking crisis cycle it was anyone greater than 10% was in trouble. It won't be different here. And these percentages will rise as they unearth higher rated loans as lower rated loans in the revaluation.

- The last takeaway is the doozy. "One criticism of the Fed's stress test by analysts was that it did not include the regional banks.....that hold the MAJORITY OF CRE LOANS. Regional lenders are also less regulated than their larger peers."

Can we say......oh shit?

So for those of you looking to take out a mortgage near the top of this cycle with your new found downpayment from what this market is about to deliver in the RIGHT STOCKS at the RIGHT TIME, I recommend you give this post a 2nd, 3rd, and 4th re-read.

Here's yesterday's news story:

https://www.reuters.com/markets/us/big-us-banks-withstand-feds-commercial-real-estate-shock-scenario-2024-06-26/

For those that want to see how to position a portfolio for absolutely amazing double-digit annualized returns through the meltup of this runaway bull market.....and then through the cycles natural, timely completion, read my post from 4 months ago titled "They are telling us so what are you waiting for " and then move into the Knowledge Transfer Series.

I am really trying to help all. However, the choice is yours.

This is the Way.

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #29 - Power of Patterns
caption:
The Knowledge Transfer Series is a compilation of posts piecing together a comprehensive toolset to navigate the constant, known and predictable cycles from nearly 35 years of personal investment - experience guided by hundreds of years experience from the greatest investors and economists - by successfully reading, listening, and TIMING markets to realize absolutely amazing double-digit annualized returns.

Whew! Now, that was a run-on sentence if I've ever seen one! But it's a byproduct of my excitement to get to the content of today's post while responsibly giving background to new readers! 😂

For the record, those that have been following the series know that I am primarily driven by fundamental analysis (read prior KTS posts).

Those that have been following also know we have come to appreciate the insight provided by fundamental analysis, technical analysis, and order flow analysis. While diehard specialists of either one of these approaches disregard the other two, we've come to understand the value in each. And when all 3 flash in the same direction, they usually create lower risk highly-profitable opportunities.

Today - a really important one to lay our foundation for developing a skill set to be able to step back and see the long-term profitable opportunities over TIME (the most neglected parameter in the world of finance KTS #10/ #10A) in stock investing.......using a typically short-term trading tool - PATTERN IDENTIFICATION.

This will help us as we forge into understanding options - in an easily digestible way! This will be of critical importance as we advance in the meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle before it completes in 5-7 quarters from now.

Study the attached chart. Learn it. Know it like a server knows the beer list. Google trading patterns and you will find a hundred or more of these cheatsheets. But they are all a little different. My recommendation - USE THIS ONE. Why? This one clearly identifies the point to BUY with green dots on the yellow BULLISH patterns and clearly shows blue/red dots at the point to SELL on the red BEARISH patterns. The chart is simple, uncluttered and truly conveys one-thousand plus words.

Where @beskar_capital really enjoys this is identifying patterns on LONGER-DATED CHARTS - at least 1-year, but preferred 3-year and 5-year charts (but even 10-year and 20-year charts). Why? Because of TIME. The period (length) of TIME that it takes to develop one of these patterns typically correlates with the period of TIME of the breakout or breakdown. What!?!?! Really??? This amazing phenomenon was unearthed over 100 years ago (KTS#100) but we are going to put this tool on a carabiner clip for at-the-ready access.

I bet the attached pattern chart is used 95% of the TIME in a less than 1-year chart analysis. We can use it there too - and I will sometimes for entry/exit points. But I promise if you give it a try by applying to 1-year charts and greater - as an INVESTOR (as defined by KTS #11) - you will be able to "see" highly profitable investment opportunities! 😂

So let's get busy, study these patterns, and identify buying and selling points from the ticker tape of our current holdings. Backtest these patterns with FUNDAMENTALLY-strong stocks to see how you would have entered or exited a stock had you had this tool before. FIND THE TIME to identify one of these patterns in at least one stock you own and share it below. Let's help everyone learn.

As a gift for sticking with this post even though you never really gave trading patterns much attention, one particular bullish pattern is a gold mine in the meltup phase.

Guess which one?

The Bullish Triangle. So go out there and find the Bullish Triangles in charts of fundamentally strong companies and share with the group. Use AT LEAST a 3-month chart, but see if you can find for 1-year chart or more. Remember this attached pattern key. The stock price must break the pattern, close above it, then come back and close outside of the pattern and reverse again.

Why does this tool work? Why wouldn't it - if you don't think the algorithms are tuned into identifying these patterns in stock charts, you are way too naive. Give a search on trading pattern charts and you will get an eternal list of returns to sift through. We've learned to listen and follow the INSIDERS (KTS #11). After looking at hundreds of charts in combination with KTS #19 & #20, you will learn that the EARLY INSIDERS (KTS #11) use this tool frequently! 😅🤣😂

Last, different phases of the cycle have a preferred pattern to identify for amazing profit setups. We need to start developing this skill as we progress in the next 5-7 quarters so we can TIME the market successfully to continue realizing absolutely amazing double-digit annualized returns.

This is the way! 😅 😎 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️

title: This is the Way Series #2 - KTS #28
caption:
Yesterday, I shared in the Knowledge Transfer Series #28, an efficient way to keep tabs on your companies (stocks) using a powerful App that professionals use on a daily basis. Re-read KTS #28 to see all of the features and functionality.....it's FREE and it will save your TIME and improve your portfolio performance!

But it is feature listed in that post (7) that I want to demonstrate here in our application of tools series I call This is the Way Series (TITWS). This is the Financials tab under each company. Here you can find several metrics that may be useful to you.

For @beskar_capital , I use 2 key metrics as a first level screener when considering stock candidates to potentially invest in: Forward P/E & Free Cash Flow.

(1) So specifically, I like to click on the current year and see what the current P/E and the current year's Free Cash Flow.....just as a base level.

(2) Next, I like to see the Forward P/E in the out years to be declining AND the Free Cash Flow (FCF) to be climbing.

(3) One neat trick is that you can get a rough idea of where the price should be in each of the coming years by dividing the current year Forward P/E by the out year Forward P/E and then multiply that number by it's current price! 😜🤣🤪😝😂😅. Seriously try this.

Ok Beskar, that looks crazy. Example please? Ok, ok. I get it. But it's a little trick that can help you know where the stock is going😉.

For taking the TIME to read this far, I'm going to give you a very near future starter (KTS #26) for @beskar_capital before I actually make it official in my portfolio. So you can see when I do.

The stock $EQT. This is the premier, world class natural gas producer in the Appalachian basin. This basin is the cheapest cost to produce in the world. It's like the Permian basin to oil. Anyway, you know about the sector and the importance of gas to everything from AI to the developing world to EVs (Yes, it's true, EVs run on natural gas! 😉).

So let's get to it. I open the App, search on $EQT and then click on the Financials tab and record them:

2024E P/E: 35.3 FCF: $558 Million
2025E P/E: 8.5 FCF: $2.6 Billion
2026E P/E: 6.7. FCF: $3.2 Billion

Yes!!!!! P/E trend is DECLINING and FCF trend is INCREASING! This one passes the test! And is that really a projected free cash flow of $3.2 Billion !?!?!? Oh my......that's bigger than some countries' GDP??? 😅

OK....let's get back to it and get an idea of where this stock's price should go if these collective analyst metrics play out (as they often do! 😉😅).

For FY2026, take 2024 Forward P/E ÷ 2026 Forward P/E = 35.3 ÷ 6.7 = 5.27x

So, current price is $40. Multiplied by 5.27 = ~ $200 per share in 2026.

What!?!??!?! Wouldn't that be nice! Yes, it would be. If the analysts, collectively, are right then it will be (KTS #17)......because any stock's price eventually follows it's earnings.

So let's enjoy watching $EQT stock price by the end of FY2026.

This is a GREAT first screener on stocks you are considering investing in .......ESPECIALLY at this point in the real estate/banking crisis cycle.

DECLINING Forward P/Es and INCREASING Free Cash Flows.

So why not give your portfolio an audit and see how many of your holdings satisfy the test above? Because, we know, that a stock's price will eventually always follow it's earnings. A simple observation that can help you consistently be profitable in your investments. This is how you can realize sustainable, double-digit annualized returns.

This is the Way!

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #28 - The Ultimate Clues
caption:
Those that have been following my posts and approach know that at the core of it is the realization that 78% of the daily trading volume is conducted by institutional money flows and that us INVESTORS (as defined by KTS #11) are left to read, listen, and observe the activity of the INSIDERS (as defined by KTS #11). By doing this, we can beat all but the EARLY INSIDERS to realize consistent double-digit annualized returns.

There are many ways to observe them which we covered in KTS #2, KTS#19, KTS #20, KTS#23, and KTS #25. And we will uncover all of them in the KTS. But today, we add a killer tool that allows us to listen directly to the most important INSIDERS, the company officers. So straight from the horse's mouth! 😅 The earnings call or conference call.

Maybe consider a re-read of KTS #17 to underscore the importance of fundamental analysis of the companies you have stock in...or, more importantly, stock candidates you are considering.

The earnings call is a quarterly event that provides more insight into the current and future state of the company than is reported...anywhere. You will learn when earnings will ramp up, any planned share issues, new capital expenditure changes, operational cost cutting, buybacks, potential acquisition interests, and so much more! So much of this information gives clear guidance on when the business turns , accelerates, or slows....which we learned from KTS #17...the stock price will eventually always follow the earnings.

BUT....there's a caution here. You don't need a degree in human psychology....but you do need to grow your ability to detect B.S. Many (most?) CEOs are glorified cheerleaders. But as you listen to the calls, you will figure these ones out quickly....as they are constantly presenting an overly optimistic outlook. And they don't have as firm of a grip on the core business ..as a CEO that is ALL IN.

But when you come across the CEOs that are ALL IN, show humility when business slows, and offers genuine insight on plans to turn around and grow...you've got a gold mine! These CEOs create a culture and you can tell by how all of the other officers (CFO, COO, etc.) speak during the call. WHEN BUSINESS IS ON THE VERGE OF BREAKOUT GROWTH, THE ENTIRE TEAM WILL SOUND EXCITED ABOUT THE FUTURE AND EACH WILL DISCLOSE ALL KINDS OF POSITIVE DETAILED INFORMATION. These are awesome attributes of a highly functioning TEAM that embrace continously improvement. And most importantly for us as INVESTORS, this is what we really invest in - the people. A bad executive team can kill a company's performance. A great executive team can propel it to new heights!

I like what you're saying, but that sounds like A LOT of work, Beskar? I have a family, and a life outside of Blossom, and I can't sit on conference calls in all of my free time.

I agree with you! But here's how you can streamline it! Over decades, I have done everything from logging on to the investor link on company websites (yes, I had AOL when the internet started...thanks Al Gore! 😅🤣😂), TOS platform, and others. But what I've been using for the past 2-3 years is the BEST yet!

Many of you might have it already, but for those that don't, I STRONGLY RECOMMEND the FREE app, Quartr. Paid version is incredible.

QUARTR is an amazing app that allows you to quickly hear it's INSIDER Officers speak. Key differentiating features that will save you TIME are:

1) Allows you to search the entire database of ALL company transcripts for key words. Try it with "direct lithium extraction" 😉or "ChatGPT" or "Taseko" or "carbon capture".

2) Allows you to speed the conference call 1x, 1.1x, 1.25x, 1.5x, or 2x. So you can make a 40 minute call, 20 min!

3) Reduce your time to listen and learn on these calls even further with the 15 second advance/rewind buttons. So you can cut out another 3 minutes on risks and disclosure statements.

4) Follow your companies by adding to a list that sends you notifications whenever an earnings call, annual meeting, or conference presentation is made......in REAL TIME! No sign ins....nothing!

5) A printed transcript, earnings release, and the presentation slide deck....all available at your finger tips. Talk about efficient!

6) Also, it splits the earnings call into opening remarks by the officers....and the juiciest part...when the analysts that cover the company grill management for clues to formulate and compute their price targets. You can learn SO MUCH from how management responds to these questions. I am confident after you listen to 20 of these, your B.S. detector will be properly trained! 😂

7) It also has the past year's key financials..BUT MOST IMPORTANTLY, it has the Forward metrics..and specifically for our approach, it has forward P/E and FreeCash Flows (the collective analysts' estimates).

Tomorrow I plan to publish This Is The Way Series #2 to demo.

Next time you workout, try Quartr. Your portfolio performance will improve!

This is the way. 😅

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #27 - David vs. Goliath
caption:
This is when the fun begins. 😂

The size of our opponent doesn't matter. The INSIDERS (as defined by KTS #11) may have scale, early information, and advantaged security investment vehicles....but they should never underestimate their opponent. As mere INVESTORS (as defined by KTS #11), the size of our opponent does not matter and these challenges are nothing to fear. We know we can beat all but the EARLY INSIDERS and most all of these INVESTORS. These lessons from David vs. Goliath will serve us well as we continue to build .... and trust....our KTS toolkit!

So Canada and Europe just came out with (hawkish) rate cuts!?!! Just barely cutting while mainly talking about inflation concerns! 😂 Increasing liquidity is the last thing a growing economy with low unemployment needs....but it is par for the course in the meltup phase of the cycle. A coordinated effort among Central banks (to try and do something to help Joe Biden? 😅).

Regardless of the reason, cutting rates helps smaller businesses that require leverage to grow......lower rates means less cash to banks and more cash to growing profits. So we should now look to adding SELECT small cap companies to our portfolio. Small Cap outperformance is what made Warren and Charlie great....as they frequently miss the days of 50% annualized returns now that they made the transition from David....to Goliath.

So in typical KTS fashion, we are always reading, listening, and observing those INSIDERS and what they are doing ....or are going to do. 😉

First, let's get some indicies defined. The Russell 3000 is the top 3,000 US stocks. The Russell 1000 is the top 1,000 stocks and the Russell 2000 is the NEXT 2,000 top US stocks. Combined, it's the Russell 3000. Make sense? The Russell 1000 median market cap (size) is ~$30 billion. The Russell 2000 median market cap is ~$1 billion. So, as we head into the final speculative frenzy of the meltup phase as the world begins cutting rates that benefits small cap stocks particularly.....we want to look at the Russell 2000, small cap index. Check out the attached chart of Russell 2000 vs. The S&P500. The 2-yr return of the Russell 2000 (mountainchart) (16.9%) is HALF of the S&P500 (green line) (31.8%). Since over long periods of TIME, these have a fairly strong correlation, this current environment is ripe for picking the RIGHT small caps for tremendously outsized returns. I do not recommend an ETF for small caps because the index is always filled with some winners and more losers...so the collective returns are muted in an ETF, even when small caps are in favor.

But what if we go inside the index and see which ones those Goliath INSIDERS have to buy.....and buy them before they do??? 😅🤣😂

It's called the Russell Reconstitution. Every year in June, the LSEG ADDS new, growing small caps and DELETES fallen, declining small caps. And the great news is that they approve the new additions 2-3 weeks BEFORE they officially make the changes. After the official reconstituition date, then ALL THOSE Goliath INSIDERS HAVE TO BUY THESE NEW COMPANIES TO KEEP THEIR ETF in line with the index! So you can literally buy the stocks ahead of the massive money pools that are FORCED to buy and hold these new companies that have all kinds of upside and prospective growth!!?!?! That's frickin' crazy @beskar_capital ! Yes...it is. But it's an annual event and it's all right there in plain view for you to see.

Example? Last year at this time you could have seen $FTAI being added to the Russell2000. At the time, it was trading at $29.63. Today, 1- year later, it's $82.67. That's an annualized return of 180% . So many more.

Shoot, Beskar...when does it happen this year? This Monday June 10th, LSEG finalizes the additions. June 28th, those INSIDER sucker's have to buy them!! 😅🤣😂 . Sorry for the weekend homework again, but you won't want to blame the dog for this one. Get busy here by clicking on the Russell3000 additions link: https://www.lseg.com/en/ftse-russell/russell-reconstitution

So how does Beskar find the RIGHT stocks among these additions? So I downloaded the tickers from the PDF at the above link and then apply KTS #19. Next, I apply KTS #17. Then, I will apply KTS #2, #7, #20, #23, and #25.

The resulting list is purely priceless. 😂

Nobody ever told you this, did they? I told you this is when the fun begins.

This is the way! 😎 🏄‍♂️ 🌊 🏄‍♀️

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #26-MLBaseball Portfolio
caption:
Sports analogies. They're everywhere. Too much so. Whether in a business meeting, a life lesson discussion with family, or casual talk with a neighbor - we LOVE communicating using Sports analogies. Even if you don't like Sports that much, you have to stay tuned in a little just to understand how people think. No wonder the mega data collectors Apple, Amazon, and Google are investing heavily into Sports! 😅🤣😂

Well, I'm human....and I am just as guilty as the others as I frequently revert to Sports analogies. So I understand if you aren't a "fan" and I apologize upfront for that! 😅

Today, I want to share my observations on HOLDINGS ALLOCATION and how you can do as most of the pros do (not as they say) and give yourself a distinct advantage compared to the Conventionalists and security pickers!

To be most direct with the takeaway from today: If you want to outperform the markets and other security pickers, Run you portfolio like a Major League baseball team!

You may be familiar with the 26-man active roster?: https://www.mlb.com/glossary/transactions/26-man-roster

....and the 40-man roster? https://www.mlb.com/glossary/transactions/40-man-roster

Let's look at my portfolio to see what I mean. The first 9 "players" on my portfolio "roster" are my starters - stocks that comprise ~>5% each by themselves of my portfolio. Of those 9, 4 are my "star" players, each with>10% of my portfolio roster. These 9 starters carry 75% of the return of my portfolio!

The next 17 "players" fill out my active substitutes of my 26-man active roster, each carrying the weight of more than 1% of my portfolio, but less than 5%. So my active 26-man roster carries the weight of ~95% of my portfolio performance!

That leaves my next 34 "players"!?!?! A total of 60 stocks!?!! These are designated to my larger 40-man roster and minor leagues to potentially get "called up" to the big leagues.

I see Blossom posts all the time about the right number of stocks to hold. Most arrive at 20-30. But this suggestion is outdated. With today's tools and the ability to get to real-time attributes virtually in an instant, once a thorough fundamental analysis has been conducted, it will soon make a lot of sense to you why YOU SHOULD take on more! Stocks are like kernels of popcorn in that they don't all pop at the same moment. By building a farm system for the future, you will always be able to pull up today's stars....and tomorrow's heroes as well.....giving your portfolio a competitive advantage to consistently yielding double-digit, annualized returns!

Ole' Warren and Charlie will tell you that you only need 4-5 good stocks in your portfolio. But maybe consider what they DO instead of what they SAY. Pulling up the latest Berkshire 13F, I see 43 players - not 4 or 5. 😂🤣😅

And guess what?....they have stars, starters, active substitutes, and minor leagues in roughly the same percentages as my portfolio. Try an exercise....go to holdingschannel.com, (a lot easier than Edgar SEC site - you're welcome!) scroll down on the left side and see links to all the top "greats" and look at their full portfolio holding PERCENTAGE allocations.......there's a repeatable pattern here everyone. 😅🤣😂

So maybe observing the greats and what they actually do can help us be great too by building a major league portfolio? 😉

Maybe re-read the KTS "Expand Your Tickers" to get a leg up on the markets and build your farm system.

Observe, read, to INSIDERS (as defined by KTS #11) to successfully TIME the markets.

PLAY BALL!

This is the way! 😎 ⚾️ 🧢 🏏

Knowledge Transfer Series #25-It's the Money Flow!
caption:
Well here we are. The 25th KTS post. In keeping with Wall Street's infatuation with key price levels (KTS #21) and the Memorial Day weekend cookout, I am offering the juiciest steaks on Volumes 25, 50, 75, 100, etc. For those that have been following all along, you get it! 😄

MONEY FLOW. This is the most important metric for entering or exiting a security to realize sustainable double-digit annualized returns. This is a tool that allows you to TIME the markets successfully and heavily weights the probability of success of a trade in your favor.

Hopefully by now, you realize that when the Conventionalists say "everybody knows you can't TIME markets",.......you know that really translates into "I can't TIME markets, I never looked into it to prove to myself that it can't be done, and I blindly accept that it can't be done because some of the ultrarich told me so." 😂

If you still aren't here yet, you're falling behind - go back and carefully review my trades, rationales, posts, and portfolio to see it in action with detailed descriptions.

OK. So we all agree that the INSIDERS (as defined by KTS #11) control ~80% of the daily trading volume. And we've come to accept that as INVESTORS (as defined by KTS #11), that we cannot beat ALL INSIDERS, but we can beat MOST INSIDERS, and nearly all INVESTORS.

So what's the best way to track the NET activity of these WHALE INSIDERS (Officers, KTS #19; Institutions KTS#20)? MONEY FLOW.

Definition please? Money Flow is a metric that solely focuses on price and volume calculations with respect to their daily CLOSING PRICE over TIME that gives us insight to the sentiment towards a security by institutions (who control the supermajority of the volume, and eventually price direction) - without having to monitor institutional order flows. The metric oscillates between periods of NET accumulation or NET distribution over periods of TIME.

Whoa Beskar! You gotta be frickin' kidding me. Really? Yes, MONEY FLOW is a forecast tool that allows us to successfully TIME MARKETS by watching what the INSIDERS, collectively, are doing with the stock/ETF. So no more of this Blackstone is selling XYZ...or Buffet is buying ABC. Instead, we can monitor the NET SENTIMENT of ALL INSIDERS to evaluate whether the security is coming under net buying/selling pressure - and act accordingly. Take that, you lazy Conventionalists! 💥 🤣

And the power comes when you study the trend of the daily-computed money flow PERSISTENCIES. Imagine a money flow reading that has indicated buying pressure (net accumulation by INSIDERS) for 1 month and the stock price remains in a tight range, going nowhere. Guess what direction it's headed once this balanced buying/selling TIME period completes? Makes it kinda easy, huh? Now imagine 3 months or even 6 months of money flow persistence with a stock price that hasn't moved? Massive gains to be had.

Conversely, if the security's price hasn't moved and the money flow indicates selling pressure, we know what is eventually coming, don't we? 🤢

I need an example please! OK, before I do this, I need to remind you that I am a FUNDAMENTALLY-BASED, ACTIVE INVESTOR. RE-READ KTS #9 and #
17 to confirm, in case you forgot. Bute recognize that there are things we can learn from technical analysts!

So $COST, the stock developed a clear symmetrical triangle pattern (we will get to this later in KTS) from April 2022 to April 2023. Over that same time period, the money flow indicators showed INSIDERS heavily accumulating the stock as it fell from $600 to $485. Once it broke out above and converted the triangle's ceiling to a floor, we knew that we had a year long run to the upside because of the accumulation in Money Flow. I entered at $500 in April 2023 at sold out in February 2024 at $750. That was 50% in less than a year. That's the kind of double-digit annualized returns we consistently seek out. Thank you, Money Flow! 😘

Now, some may say, oh the tax implication. Why not hold a year for LT gains vs. Short term because you were just a month away. Sorry, when you are realizing returns like this, you move to where the opportunity costs are minimized. I needed to start plowing those gross proceeds from $COSTT into $TGB, which was trading at $1.50 - with Money Flow accumulation readings - persistently for a month with the price going nowhere! Still holding that one today at $3.00. So that's 100% return in 2.5 months. Can you see why I didn't wait on $COST for "intelligent tax considerations"?

Opportunity Cost. We will have a whole KTS on this someday. Hey LeCorb @lecorb! - can you give us all a post on Opportunity Cost in your role as a senior executive in business to help with definition?

There are different Money Flow metrics and we will get to this later but start probing on your own to test.

Enjoy this juicy steak!

Happy Memorial Day....This is the Way!

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #24- 🎶 Closing Time 🎶
caption:
🎶 🎵 "Closing Time, every new beginning
Comes from some other beginning's end." 🎶 🎵

As we approach 4:00 pm EST - the daily Closing Time puts an end to just another market day.

That couldn't be further from the truth!

In the world of Wall St, the price at the official CLOSE has always become a part of the historical record going back to 1790 when it began in Philadelphia. It has come up recently when investors questioned the SEC with "Hey! Why do crypto investors get to trade 24/7 and we equity traders/investors can only access liquid markets for 6.5 hours every non-holiday." The exchanges are advocating for this evolution (more volume = more money) and the old wolves are certainly pushing against it citing that traders have lives too and the day needs to "end".

It will be interesting to see if this becomes a reality. But for now, we should/need to understand the significance of the time just before....and just after the close. While it's a hot topic now, I suspect that this "debate" will be squashed by those that control the market. So KTS followers know that means this is a battle, INSIDERS vs. The Exchanges.

So today's tool is temporarily on the extinction list. Buffaloes and American Eagles were on it once, too. But once you've learned how important the Closing bell is to Wall Street, you'll understand why they will fight to the death to keep it! 😅🤣😂

As we've touched on in KTS #12, #14 and #19, there is a lot of activity that occurs in the 30 minutes before and after the Close. Here's just a small sample of the kind of actions you'll routinely see:

- Just before or just after the close, huge price spikes or drops by INSIDERS (as defined by KTS #11) to either continue an accumulation/distribution plan into/out of a security - as an attempt to hide their "bets" from the market. We saw this with $HXL officers 15 seconds after the close just a week ago. Had you bought it when we pointed it out in KTS #19...at a CHEAPER PRICE than officers!, you would be up 15+% in ~ 1 week with so much more upside ahead. But you could sell it now and realize double-digit annualized returns just by sitting out the rest of the year. 😂

- Algorithms, set by institutions for a predefined Closing price, automatically trade the necessary shares to end the day that they have a majority control of the flow. THIS WILL BECOME A CRITICAL COMPONENT of understanding stock entry and exit point signals as we develop our toolbox. We'll proceed into this concept slowly through a number of KTS posts....so we don't lose anybody.

- Immediately following the close, you will routinely see MAJOR PRESS RELEASES. And not talking about earnings releases.

This last reason is the one that I've developed a useful tool for - and the focus of this KTS #24. Ask yourself, if this is the case, what are the odds that a press release after the CLOSE is good news? You thinking 10%? My experience is that is way too high!

What kind of news are you talking about?

Here's the common flashing SELL signals on company news stories:

- Closing key operations for "routine maintenance", fires, fatalities, etc. $MERC
had a fire at a key production pulp mill in July 2022. The stock's price fell from $16 to $8 over the course of the next 365 days. This is a common reaction to news that most INVESTORS don't ever see since they are obsessed with near-term price movements.

- The firing/"retiring" of a company officer for which there is no previous, publicly-stated succession plan. Especially the CFO. If a CFO is fired and you get wind of it in an after-hours press release, this is a MANDATE to SELL IMMEDIATELY. This is fairly (relatively) common by the way. Make sure to throw your HOPE out the window ...and why not come back to this stock you love when KTS #23 emerges. $ORGN announced a significant change in operations in early Aug 2023, then a change in CFO later in Oct 2023, after the CLOSE. title: Knowledge Transfer Series #23- Switchback Mountain
caption:
For those new to the KTS series, we use this series to pass along a toolset that will help you consistently outperform the S&P500, realizing sustainable, double-digit annualized returns by avoiding the conventional wisdom for the poor distributed to all, studying the cycles, and by learning to read, listen, and TIME the markets successfully. We understand that we cannot beat all INSIDERS (as defined in KTS #11), but we will beat MOST INSIDERS....and nearly ALL INVESTORS (as defined by KTS #11). The overall objective of the series is to teach how to fish versus giving fish.

We have created a follow on series, This Is The Way (TITW) to demonstrate how these tools are applied. This series will have far fewer posts until the toolkit is built up sufficiently and followers have had a chance by testing, proving, and failing for themselves to learn how it really works properly.

Today, we dig another layer on TIMING the markets. The Conventionalists will be out in full force to stomp out this fire, threatening their existence. 😅🤣😂

Time to eat.....Suuueeeey! Ok, as most of you know by now, in this part of the cycle (meltup phase), I spend time looking at longer-dated price charts OF STOCKS THAT I HAVE A FAVORABLE FUNDAMENTAL ANALYSIS ON. That's an important point. In the meltup phase, this tool works particularly well - so don't fall asleep! To keep you engaged, we are going to have a hands-on session today.

I am going to give you two groups of stocks and ask you to pull up their 5-year charts and come to a conclusion on the difference between the two groups.

Group 1: $MMM, $AOS, $TGB, $ASPN, $BALL, $BCO, $COF, and $CMI

Now,

Group 2: $BMY, $PTLO, $JACK, $WBA, $$TSLA, $NIO, $TDOC, and $U

Take the time to click on the 5-year price chart for each one. What makes the charts in each group similar to others within their group.....but different from those In the other group?

Waiting......

TIME's up! ⏰️

Group 1 is a collection of stocks whose 5-year charts saw a period of decline, where the stock's price made a consistent series of LOWER highs and LOWER lows during the period of decline BEFORE reversing course to enter a new period of TIME, where the stock's 5-yr price chart is currently setting HIGHER highs and HIGHER lows. The 2nd attribute of Group 1 stocks - THE current stock price of each is within 5% of their 52-week (1-year) high. Read the last sentence again. This switchback in the 5-year price chart trend signals it's TIME to slap on the backpack for a climb like the Yodler game in the Price is Right! In the meltup phase, especially, this is a major BUY signal.

Group 2? As you likely figured out...they are still hiking down the mountain. In the meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle - when so many stocks are climbing - this is NOT a good sign. Major SELL signal. In most cases, you will do yourself a huge favor by parting with these now vs. regretting you didn't sell earlier...later - realizing that your capital could have been elsewhere while you wait for your HOPE stocks....switchback . Lil Uzi $VRT vs. $TSLA ring a bell? Does this tool one up Charlie buying and sitting on his hands? Yep, it does. I had a comment in my KTS #10/#10A, that asked how many people TIMED the $MSFT re-entry in 2012 after laying off it for a decade. Don't really care, but I know KTS #23 allows you to TIME the markets to be one of them (re-read KTS #10/#10A).

To close this KTS #23 tool, I grabbed 4 Canadian stocks that are frequently debated for buying/selling on Blossom. Get out your KTS #23 and settle it once and for all. Let me know which 2 stocks are buys right now.....and which tow are sells. They are: $ENB, $TD, $TU, $BN.

It's not as hard as you might think. TIMING markets is possible. @stocksonthego2.0 heard I could come in and buy $NIO after it was done declining all the while he was DCA ing down cutting hands on that falling knife - securing more shares than he has. He asked me to tell him when I do. Now we all know when. 😉

title: Tetra Technologies Core Holding Completed
Knowledge Transfer Series#22-Charts Map the Future
caption:
If you haven't learned by now through the Knowledge Transfer Series, we'll just come out and say it directly:

Charts are market timing puzzle pieces.

If you've been following my moves since we started in the first week of March this year, you have already seen - firsthand - double-digit returns in just two months. And this is during a sharp drop in the market indicies. But we remind ourselves, we don't care about those so much - because we are INVESTORS that INVEST in companies, not indicies. We leave the subpar performance to the Conventionalists and the Indexers.

Most fundamentalists hate charts (Full Disclosure: I most appreciate a fundamental approach to stock selection). They ridicule them as magical crystal balls, tea leaves,etc. and scoff that you can read a human psychology in the print. They proclaim markets cannot be TIMED, despite their acknowledgement that they have personally NEVER LOOKED INTO IT! Arrogance at it's finest! They just DRIP and DCA until it finally works out and proclaim they avoided some kind of risk, which it's evident they have no clue of assessing by the mere fact they overweight in a heavily-weighted S&P500 vehicle. Well, it's their loss. As they cap their returns over TIME to that that rivals current money market/CD rates, we will just let them watch us rake it in. Find some sick kind of enjoyment in that they are quietly rooting against us while considering what we say and do. I pray for them to join and learn to TIME the markets.

I ask that you reread KTS #15. This is more important than you know as we get closer to cycle completion.

Now, let's get started. Imagine a 10,000 piece puzzle. If you complete it, you've figured it out. That's what WallSt wants you to think the market is. Too complicated to understand or even know where to begin. But, it's not as hard as you think. Break it down by identifying the key pieces first. Start with border. Use the box as a guide. Focus on sections of the puzzle (sectors) that are easier than others.

We know that INSIDERS (KTS#11) make markets by acting on information well in advance of public release. And it is even accepted by Conventionalists that the CHARTS do tell about future economic conditions (they use a cliché verbage, "priced in"). And remember that hate charts and proclaim VooDoo. How ironic their acceptance?😅🤣😂

So why not step back and look at, say 20-year charts, of very specific companies that can help us understand where we are in the market cycle? Can CHARTS actually tell us where we are in the cycle and how to position on a long-term basis? You bet!

Let's think about some recent stock price movements on 20-year charts as examples:
- Does $GLD-STOCK-US-USD, $WPM-STOCK-US-USD breaking out to all-time highs tell us about where we are in the real estate/banking crisis cycle?
- Does the rapid climb in crime stocks $BCO-STOCK-US-USD (money safes, trucks), $CXW-STOCK-US-USD, $GEO-STOCK-US-USD (building jails, staff) repeat at certain points in the cycle?
- Does a run up in aerospace & defense stocks (e.g. $RTX-STOCK-US-USD, $LDOS-STOCK-US-USD) tell us anything? Do wars occur at certain parts of the market cycle!?!?!??
- What part of the cycle do insurance premiums, and company profits, skyrocket and people quit carrying it? $KIE ?
- Has the regional banks already peaked in the cycle? $KRE

All of the above reflect growing FEAR in our society that correlates with select border puzzle pieces. As we continue to build the framework of the market puzzle - with the objective to continue to TIME it - we can learn from history that this is repeated and rhymes - over and over and over and over.....

So we can expect during the continued fevered frenzy of speculation that will continue to propel THE RIGHT STOCKS AT THE RIGHT TIME, we also know that there will be - per script - many other popular stocks that will get clobbered. So those popular stocks of recent past, many are on the verge of reversal.

What will trigger the next adjustment during this meltup phase of the current real estate/banking crisis cycle that is set to end in the next 5-7 quarters? CRE.

Watch the fear grow.....along with select stocks. May and October are peak months for bank failures. There were 5 last year and one already this year. We will have more this year than last, as typical in the cycle progression. In fact, in the 567 bank failures since the turn of the century. ~4/5ths occurred during the last meltup phase and subsequent recession. So wouldn't be surprised one bit if we start seeing multiple bank runs later this month. Why? Because it's TIME for it and the charts are telling us so.

Did @beskar_capital just call a bank run this year....and likely later this month!?!? 🤔

This is what happens in this part of the cycle. Learn to read, listen, and TIME the markets.....by studying the charts. How else can we make the pieces fit?

Still a raging bull market.

In the past couple of KTS posts, we developed specific tools to track the activity of INSIDERS (as defined by KTS #11) - a tool for corporate officers & directors (KTS #19) and one for the big institutional insiders (KTS #20). Can't wait for May 10th, right? 😉 (KTS #20).

As INVESTORS that read, listen, and TIME the markets - to sustainably realize double-digit annualized returns - we have come to willingly accept that we can't beat ALL INSIDERS, but we can beat MOST INSIDERS and nearly ALL INVESTORS (KTS #11).

Today, we will take a look at an elementary concept that INSIDERS commonly employ, which we can make observations and develop our understanding of INSIDERS activity - PRICE LEVELS.

Remember counting books in Preschool? ...Or the number line in first grade? Remember playing flashcards in 3rd grade? All fond memories, right?

Now, remember decimals? Or how about remainders on long division? Fractions? Not as fun, correct?

Same on Wall Street. They LOVE whole numbers and integers. After all, many of those dummies think like 1st graders anyway. I know that's a rough statement to make, but if you listen enough, over time you will see what I mean.

And there's a hierarchy, too. $100 is preferred over $95. $45 is preferred over $44. If we go to smaller cap stocks, $3.00 is preferred over $2.89. $2.75 is preferred over $2.71. $2.50 and $2.00 is preferred over $2.25. But $2.25 is preferred over $2.18. $1,000, $100, $10, and $1.00 are a stronger preference than $900, $70, or $30. But $900, $70, and $30 are preferred over $860, $68, and $29.

You can look at these price levels as obstacles like in Monty Python and the Holy Grail. Whether it's the Black Knight of the Reliable Bridge for whom None Shall Pass, the forest Knights Who Say Ni!, or the Troll Bridge Keeper who must know your favorite color. In much the same way, these key price levels act as points of challenge for stock prices - more so than valuation calculations. Going forward we will talk about "floors" or "ceilings" and how they act as price levels of "SUPPORT" or "RESISTANCE ", respectively. This is a key concept to observing INSIDERS (KTS #11) behavior and activity.....in order to TIME the markets.

Don't blow this insight off. Think commutative property. IF Wall St = 1st Grade Mentality AND 1st Grade Mentality = Easy Numbers, THEN Wall St = Easy Numbers.

Start keying in on these price levels whenever you hear/see them. Whether stock charts, financial talking heads, Blossom posts, or your neighbor's stock tips. You'll see these easy, round numbers time and again. It can be like a drinking game! 😂

Seriously, once you've observed and invested for a lllooonnnggg time, you'll notice these price levels will pop out - much more than some valuation metrics price target you calculated. It puts a different spin on what's truly logical. 😉

Examples please?

- that when a stock breaks through and closes above a certain percentage above next $100 level, it's going to run for awhile
- when a falling Biotech stock holds $1.00 +/- a certain percentage (we'll get to this), it has bottomed.
- when a stock falls to the 50 cents level (+/- percentage) and stays there for OVER a period of months, it is a sign of INSIDERS accumulating the stock and the bottom is in.
- or how about a recent "hands on" example. Those that follow me know I've highlighted the INSIDERS accumulating $USAS (U.S. ticker). Pull up the 1 month chart and you learn that EVERY DAY since April 15th, the stock low has been $0.26 .😂 Now that's a FLOOR!!!

So much to learn with price levels and how stocks' (and the INSIDER market makers) behave around them.

This is the way.

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #20 - INSIDER WHALES
caption:
What TIMING? Today we add an ultimate tool to our kit just in TIME for its first use. I love TIMING. Think hammer/screwdriver - because this is one to use quarterly in our double-digit annualized return mission.

For those new/fairly new to this series, I ask that you (re)read KTS #11 and KTS #19 before proceeding. We learned that 78% of the trading volume is performed by INSIDERS (as defined by KTS #11). INSIDERS are the market makers - and we as INVESTORS accept we can't beat ALL INSIDERS, but we can beat MOST INSIDERS and nearly all INVESTORS. We also learn in this series that we listen, read, and TIME markets following early INVESTORS movements. We don't buy stocks in perpetual declining patterns or sideways for-who-knows-how-long patterns (KTS #10). Hope is not a successful investment strategy despite being the most popular one. When the fundamentals, the technicals, and the INSIDERS activity align, we come in and ride that surf.

So hammer time - let's go! In KTS #19, we identified a tool to watch INSIDERS (1) & (2), officers/directors. Today, we target the WHALES of INSIDERS, (3) the Institutions, Hedge/Mutual funds, etc.

So how do you monitor their buys/sells - after all, these guys aren't just buying 50,000 shares. They accumulate positions over weeks in the hundreds of thousands to multimillion shares in a company. Quick fact: they will use ALL investment vehicles available to build a position of their targeted size to do it. That's options, preferred stock, open market stock, warrants, convertible derivatives, etc. AND THEY WILL DO THEIR BEST TO ACCUMULATE THE SHARES WITHOUT INVESTORS BEING ABLE TO DETECT THEM. Because, if INVESTORS catch on, they will not be able to build the profitable position size. The wolves fight among themselves ..and certainly any other species (INVESTORS) to rip every piece of meat off the carcass and chase them away until their finished. And being nocturnal, they attempt to go completely unnoticed while the INVESTORS are sleeping.

But, by law, these WHALES have to report their holdings (for each stock, the number of shares they own) by 6 weeks after the quarter ends. So 4x a year. So when do you think most all of these whales file the form if they don't want you to catch on? That's right - THE VERY LAST DAY THEY HAVE TO IN ORDER TO COMPLY WITH THE LAW! 😂 So once March ends (1Q), 6 weeks later puts us at the 2nd Friday of May. 2Q - June 30 + 6 weeks = 2nd Friday of August. 3Q is then Friday of the 2nd week of November. 4Q is the 2nd Friday of February. The weekend right after these come out are major homework weekends, if you really want to outperform the market and realize ridiculous profits.

This SEC form (13-HR/13F) at the SEC EDGAR site is filled with all kinds of nuggets. And I give you this hammer as an investment housing warming gift. Shown above, we are coming up on a big release by all of the funds on May 10th (some actually file on Thursday just to make sure they beat compliance 😂). So you have time to get ready.

Ok, so I know what to look for and when to look for it. But which funds should I look at? And how do you do it?

Here's some pointers:

- Find an ACTIVELY MANAGED FUND THAT HAS A HISTORICAL DOUBLE-DIGIT ANNUALIZED RETURN, and start there. I posted one great.😉

-We know mutual funds, ETFs, and hedge funds are dilutive-by-design investment vehicles. If these fund managers are realizing 15% annualized returns, then you know some stocks are the workhorses that are carrying all the weight of the inderperfirmers, such that when all securities in the fund are averaged together, it brings the collective fund performance DOWN to the 15% annualized return in our example above.

- So if we just track these successful INSIDER WHALES - and instead of buying their fund - just buy the gross outperforming stocks within their fund that carry all of their inderperforming stocks (baggage) and still beat the S&P500, then we will absolutely kill the S&P500. Get it?

- You can go find a find right now, and download it's last 8 (or even more if so inclined) FORM 13-HRs (2 years, 1 per quarter, right?). And you can see them building positions into stocks before it shows up in the market. Then, when these breakout technically, we pile in with our measly 500 shares. 😅🤣😂

Amazing, huh? Didn't I tell you this Knowledge Transfer Series gives you some amazing meat? You will learn all kinds of insight about stocks, markets, and cycles here - we are just getting started. You will learn why the market behaves like it does around these releases too. 😉

I have been successfully using this method for over 25 years with amazing results. We are humans and we have brains - so let's use them against the humans that don't use them (Conventionalist indexers).

As INVESTORS that share the vast ocean waters with these INSIDERS, we are either plankton or barnacles to these whales - choose barnacle and ride along.

This is the way. 😅
