title: Knowledge TransferSeries #19-Insider Trading Ideas
caption:
We know that INSIDERS (as defined by KNS #11) are the powerful market makers that make stocks move. We know, and accept, that we are mere INVESTORS that can read, listen, and TIME markets to find optimal entry and exit points following early INSIDERS. We also understand that we won't beat ALL of the INSIDERS, but we can beat MOST of the INSIDERS and virtually ALL INVESTORS on a sustainable path of realizing double-digit annualized returns, year after year.

So let's add another tool to the box! Breaking down the INSIDERS, we have distinct groups: (1) company executive officers (CEO, CFO, CTO, SVP, CIO, etc), (2) Board Directors, (3) > 5% Ownership Entities (e.g. institutional investors, investment banks, wealthy individuals, think Berkshire Hathaway, Goldman Sachs, etc.), (4) Some others (e.g. - Select sell-side analysts that get rewarded with insider information if they cheer the company story). All of these entities are eligible candidates for access to inside information that isn't publicly available and collectively make up....the INSIDERS.

Today, we will add a tool to monitor #1 and #2, company officers and directors - the most important signaler to a company's rising stock chart.

Peter Lynch made an observation in One Up on Wall Street, that these insiders SELL their stock for a whole multitude of reasons......but they BUY their own stock for only one reason.......because they think it is going up! I read this book in my very first year of investing (1990) and have tested and verified this observation to be amazingly predictive. It's true, officers will SELL company stock to find cash for a new home, alimony, vacations, kids accounts, etc. They also SELL when they have to by a compensation plan. They also SELL when they think the company stock price is high....and headed lower. But the point is, officers and directors SELLING stock doesn't really signal much of anything.

But BUYING stock directly on the open market says EVERYTHING. Now some buying occurs as part of a new here's compensation package (SEC Form 3). And some buying occurs as in routine time periods as part of an automated rotation of exercising of options - as part of a pay structure. I'm (and Peter Lynch) not talking about those exceptions.

Instead, we are referencing DIRECT, OPEN MARKET PURCHASES OF COMPANY STOCK BY OFFICERS AND DIRECTORS ALONGSIDE OF US GROUNDLING INVESTORS. To find this information, you have to visit SEC site EDGAR and search on Form 4 and look for the code "P", which stands for "Purchase". And of course, the higher the investment amount (and correspondingly higher number of shares) translates into a level of conviction for these officers and directors.

What!?!?! You mean I can look up when the CEO and CFO - NOBODY has more access to the company's near, mid, and long term prospects than these two - purchases shares on the open market?......and then BUY SHARES AT A CHEAPER PRICE THAN THEY DO!??!?!!?

Yes. In most cases, you can. Typically, however, as soon as this happens, the stock builds a floor that it commonly won't drop much below, if at all, the officers' purchase price. And even more common, the stock's price takes off.

So what's this tool? The SEC EDGAR site is one to look into.....but it isn't user friendly. Instead, we use openinsider.com . This is a great resource to get ideas to further investigate potential investment ideas with the fundamental and technical tools we are adding in the Knowledge Transfer Series.

So here's so

I have proven to myself that when any of a company's officers makes A SIGNIFICANT purchase (using Form 4) of their own company stock directly on the open market alongside us measlyINVESTORS, that the stock is almost guaranteed to double within 4 years....at the latest. Often, a 100% return is realized within 18 months. Commonly within 3 years. Any of these outsized returns over TIME blows away the broad-based, dilutive-by-design mediocre returns of the S&P500 investment vehicles.

So here's some tips to MAXIMIZE returns by isolating the best stocks to piggyback on the officers buys at www.openinsider.com :

(1) At top menu, click on 'Latest Insider Purchases'
(2) Just click the checkbox in front of 'P - Purchase '
(3) In the 'Traded K$' box, type 145 (this is a minimum purchase of $145,000)
(4) In the Insider Title box, click the checkbox in front of 'Officer' for the best and virtual lock leads.
(5) If interested in expanding the search, go ahead and click on the checkbooks in front of 'Director', '10% own', AND 'Other' too. This is still an extremely high probability of realizing the returns over TIME discussed in the paragraph above.

Now, when you get some ideas from this screener, why not consider the sector favorability and the company's fundamentals & technical analysis. Whoa!!! This layered approach really starts to reduce risk, eh?

We will get this later in KNS and how to build ironclad investments.😉

This is the way.

title: Here we go with the meltup promises!
caption:
If you have been following me, you know that I successfully read, listen, and TIME markets and their cycles.

I have pointed out that this phase will be marked with governments spending for the masses and planning for the biggest, brightest, and best of everything from buildings to technology. Last year, it was a Biden plan of internet for everyone. This morning, a $7 billion grant to gift solar rooftops to the low income households and a high-speed rail from Vegas to L.A........with complete disregard to government revenues or budgets.

On the surface, this may feel like a call to invest in beaten down solar stocks and companies that build high-speed rail.

Don't do it!

We are in the meltup phase of the real estate/banking crisis cycle, which has ~2 years left. These projects are desperation moves to keep the bull raging beyond this timeframe.....but it won't. The cycle will complete on time, as it always does, and leave these projects stranded or never started when the collapse comes.

The solar stock companies have some of the worst balance sheets right now and the solar market is expected to collapse as a result. Interest rates are killing any demand from anyone for solar. While these stocks may experience a short-lived pop on the news of this $7 billion "plan", the best investment is to buy puts, if anything. Remember last time around on the solar buyout when balance sheets turned to 💩?

SunRun $RUN , is destined to head lower and I can't imagine anyone buying them out with that balance sheet. Save yourself and stay away!

Understanding which news.......is newsworthy....... is part of how we read and time the cycle.

Follow my posts, track my trades, and monitor my portfolio for absolutely amazing double-digit annualized returns over the next ~2 years before the cycle completes and learn how to position a portfolio for what comes afterwards. Start with my post "They are telling us so what are you waiting for " from well over a month ago and then move into the Knowledge Transfer Series to learn how.

So the aim of this series is to build our toolkit of investment strategies to learn to listen, read, and time the markets and the cycles to successfully - both consistently and sustainably - realize double-digit annualized returns.

Early in this series, we provided a straightforward ETF only approach to handily beat the S&P500 - that I quit using. This follow the $ector (KNS #2) approach is a proven method that can be enhanced by digging in on the key stocks that move these sectors through cycle rotation.

Also through KNS, we have clearly stated the phase of the current real estate/banking crisis cycle, the TIMING of its completion, and why the current portfolio looks like it does heavy in Capital Markets, Industrials, Materials, and mainly Commodity-related securities. Last year the portfolio looked different as the Tech sector was dominant.

We have now reached the part of the cycle will Tech really begins to tire out. It is important to recognize that each Tech stock chart can behave very differently. For the TECH SECTOR, YOU NOW MUST GIVE ALMOST FULL WEIGHT INTO ANY ENTRY INTO ANY INVESTMENT DECISION BY LOOKING AT THE TREND IN EARNINGS ESTIMATE REVISIONS. At this stage in the meltup phase in TECH stocks, you should run from anything seeing a downtrend in earnings estimate revisions.

Why is this so? title: Knowledge Transfer Series #17-Fundamental Analysis
caption:
Without a doubt, the most important step to evaluating the direction and magnitude of a company's stock price over an investor TIME period is fundamental analysis. Sorry, technical analysts and order flow analysts. That's not to say those approaches don't have incredibly valuable insight.....even over the long-term and investor time periods. But, fundamental analysis requires a deep dive into the balance sheet for an understanding of leverage ratios (debt levels), growth rates in revenue, gross and net profit margins, free cash flows, share issuance, executive compensation, revenue per employee, price to sales, price to book, capital expenditure plans, on and on, etc.

Now once we have all these indicators, we need to analyze the company trend in these indicators over time, relative to competitors, relative to other industries, or not relative to anything if a company doesn't have a long enough history. NOW...we also have to keep in mind these ratios are constantly changing.....so we need to monitor these as well.

@bradleytalksmoney made an insightful observation the other day when doing a fundamental exercise on investing in Blossom and arriving at the conclusion that it's just too early to be able to fundamentally handicap the company AND THAT YOU ARE REALLY MAKING A BET ON THE LEADERSHIP TEAM TO EXECUTE. And of course, we ALL want Max and the Blossom team to knock it out of the park! Congratulations again to their amazing work! This observation is so true. For all companies and their corresponding stock prices, a longer term INVESTOR (as defined by KNS #11) is really just buying into the capabilities, the vision, and the talent of the leadership team. If they're not good, the company will suffer setbacks or even fail, and the stock price will follow. Conversely, a management team that can execute on plans to grow the company will see its stock price rise.

And @stocksonthego2.0 is exactly right in always making sure you know what you own from a fundamental perspective.

Oh geez..fundamental analysis is exhausting and sounds like A LOT OF WORK! It is. And there's no escaping this. You really need to put the time in to understand companies - especiallyonce you own them! You can't do as Old Charlie used to say, "Buy a few great companies and just sit on your ass!" without staying on top of the fundamentals. After all, Ole Charlie/Warren frequently reversed course on huge investments they've made based on an initial fundamental analysis only to not sit on their ass and quickly scale down in a big way or just plain exit when the fundamentals change (e.g. Wells Fargo, U.S. Bank, BYD, on and on).

BUT - I have a Cliff Notes version for you that gets you close and takes into account a lot of it. Or for younger generations that don't know what Cliff Notes even are, I have a Chegg or probably now, a ChatGPT easy button (pronounced today with a valley girl dialect "butt-in"). OK, OK, I couldn't resist.

RISING ANNUAL EARNINGS ESTIMATES FOR INVESTORS. There it is. Wall Street wants you to think the financial world is so complicated with endless computer screens and complex algorithms, jargon terms that make them sound like guru experts, premiums, durations, volatility, flows, accreditation, derivatives, risk tolerance, 60:40 models, allocation levels, etc.

They want you to think it is overly complicated.....because it's not. Think about that. They want you in the ocean, but without any life preserver so they can use their predatory behaviors like sharks. The reality is that there are a lot of dummies dominating that industry (not to disparage the several brilliant ones that I have learned to follow and read with the privilege to ride along - like a kid chasing an autograph 😂). But there's a lot of wealthy people tied to Wall Street that fell into dumb luck and consider themselves experts. Don't buy it. If you really believe them to be authorities, then you are just taking the bait. That's the mysterious world they want you to think it is.

So recommended exercise for over this weekend: jot down the tickers for each of your holdings (spreadsheet is nice for electronic archival). Then, look back at the TREND IN ANNUAL earnings estimates over the past 3 years - PROVE TO YOURSELF that THE TREND IN EARNINGS ESTIMATES is HIGHLY CORRELATED to stock price movements. What you will see is that companies whose earnings per share rises, their stock price rises. If the earnings estimate trend is flat, then so is the stock price. If the earnings estimates are falling, so is the stock price.

WOW! Really? Yes, really. In the end, it all is quite rational (despite periods of irrational markets or unloved/overloved stocks). You will see this correlation in virtually all your stocks and in virtually all stocks in the entire universe. So for INVESTORS, always know the direction of the change in ANNUAL earnings estimates.

More coming on the trends in earnings estimates.

This is the way.

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #16- Expand Your Tickers
caption:
Double header KNS posts for Today and Tomorrow.......because TIMING is right!😉😂

Remember studying for Biology or Geography exams?  Were you any good at it?  If so, you are going to rock it on your aim to financial independence. And I'm talking about the TIME it takes to get there.  If those subjects were a struggle for you, it's ok, you'll just have to study and reread a little bit more.

For some, it comes easy, tell me the capital of Norway and it's a permanent "ingrain in the brain".  For others, it takes time and must be reiterated over and over until it sinks in.  Well, it works that way with the market too.  

The point is - the broader your knowledge of the available opportunities (different stocks) and what these companies do, the higher your level of outperformance as you will be able to more accurately READ and TIME the markets.  Look out!  Here come the Conventionalists with their single-digit annualized returns at best with their Peter Lynch quotes about limiting your investing only to stocks in what you know - like your field of work.  Yikes!  While I don't disagree with investing in your workplace industry to identify great opportunities,  I do disagree that you should stop there.  That's outdated advice that was relevant when the tedious and time-consuming process to research a company was to take a weeknight, go to your local library,  pull the tickest book off the shelf (VALUE LINE), and turn to the page of the stock ticker of your research report, and get busy taking notes!  Yes, that's what we did when we were your age!?!?!?🤪

Instead, today's tools and technology are just sitting there for you to relatively quickly start absorbing what most all companies do.  This is not one to blow off.  There's some 4,500+ listed stocks in U.S. and, I think, some 11,000+, worldwide.  Not saying to make 11,000 flashcards and learn it overnight....but I am saying, while you are young, start expanding your universe of companies and what they do.  This will facilitate you reaching a $2 million nest egg via double-digit annualized returns by the age of 50, not 70 (reference my post by Vic Lederman - "Why it is important to beat the S&P500".

And knowing the key tickers (stocks that drive sectors and subsectors) will greatly advance your performance. Can I make a suggestion on a good place to start? Go to State Street SPDR Sectors/Subsectors and jot down all 11 Sector and 21 Subsector ETF tickers. Then, take your time a take one of these 32 ETF tickers at a time and look into the stocks that make up the sector/subsector. How about 5 a month? That will greatly improve your knowledge of the markets and when you hear tickers in the news, it will help you connect the dots. And most importantly, it will bring context to our development of reading where we are in the cycle.

Here's the facts. If you just stay tuned to your current ticker universe, you have NO CHANCE to realize sustainable, double-digit annualized returns. The stocks working great for you today and in the near to midterm future won't be working for you later. It's just a fact. Either accept it or stubbornly keep hoping. Most of the investors on this site are setting up to get absolutely annihilated in about 2 years as they will continue holding the solid performance they are currently delivering with Conventionalist wisdom to just keep holding cliff dropping stocks. You don't have to be a victim. But it takes an open mind and a willingness to learn more. But you're on Blossom, so you already qualify as interested in finance and growing your performance! 👏

So the large drop in stock prices over a short period of time (last 4 days), we'll learn later in this series is an EXTREMLY BULLISH event. In fact, in this part of the cycle, market retracements are typically not less than 6%. We're currently at 4%.

And if you know the businesses behind the tickers, you'll know where the economy is truly headed.

For example, I bet many of you are a little nervous over the past 4 days? There will be a time for that, but we are nowhere near that now.

Analysts are COLLECTIVELY upgrading and raising price targets during this market drop in a BIG way...Mohawk Industries(carpets), Lowe's (Home improvement), Martin Marietta, Summit Materials, Eagle Materials (sand and gravel for Asphalt and concrete), ExxonMobil (gasoline), Valero (diesel for trucking), Copper Miners $TGB and $IVPAF (Dr. Copper tells you where economy runs), Cleveland Cliffs ( $CLF) and Nucor ( $NUE) (Steel - industrial boom), on and on. It's easy to see - no worry!

At this point, for me, I can read where "the puck is going" not where the puck has been. Spend your precious financial learning time doing this instead of liking yet another post of someone proud of their DCA efforts .. and I promise you that you will quickly raise your performance relative to the masses.

This is the way.

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #15 - The 20-Year Chart
caption:
I hope by now you realize that these Knowledge Transfer Series posts are loaded with real meat. Think of your favorite smoked meat where you sink in with a deep bite...and your teeth never come in contact with any bone. My mouth is literally watering right now as I think about it. 😋

Anyway, the purpose of this series is to convey the decades of knowledge I have obtained from the truly greatest investors of all-time (and it's not limited to the 2nd tier "greatest" ones that get all the press and requotes) to demonstrate HOW TO READ AND TIME THE MARKETS with profitable actions to sustain annualized double-digit returns. We do this by developing our toolbox to handle different market conditions using both tools from Fundamental and Technical analysis in combination of watching the activity of the INSIDERS (KNS #11), realizing we can't beat ALL of them, but we can beat MOST of them and SQUASH our fellow INVESTORS (KNS #11).

But we are certain to exclude the 75% of Conventionalist guidance - which is distributed to the masses of retail investors. These are the ones that talk incessantly about DRIP, DCA, dilutive S&P500 investment vehicles, bonds, and returns on cash holdings, on and on. Makes me nauseous. Young investors willing to take the time to learn about how to make their savings work for them aren't a good match for this lazy investor product suite listed above!

Always remember, Conventionalist advice is delivered to the general investing public. And most all of the general investing public is relatively poor. Conversely, the wealthy do something different. So why on Earth would we follow the advice that puts us on a path of "Conventional Wisdom"? You know what they all have in common? The same All-Time returns. Check it out for yourself. Everytime you see those acronyms and hear those cute sayings in a post (DCA, DRIP, "Buy Low, Sell High", "Buy when there's blood on the streets", etc.), test it out, and click on the author's all-time returns. If it's ~7% +/- 2.5%, mark it down as "Conventionalist". It is really amazing how that regurgitated advice plays through into all of their portfolio returns. Anyway, if it isn't double-digit, annualized returns, then maybe you should look elsewhere. Just know that Wall Street wants you to be a Conventionalist (prey).

OK. Got that out. Now, let's add a new tool as we make more progress on our ability to read and time markets. As you know, I am a proponent of knowing where you are in the cycle. There are many cycles in play at any given time. Examples include: seasonal, business, product, real estate/banking crisis, super cycles, etc. Different sectors, subsectors, stocks, bonds, ETFs, etc. work best in different phases of any given cycle. There can be overlap, intersection, coincidence, and separation in cycles. Sounds like a lot to keep track of? Not really - because we follow the Wall Street playbook and their subsequent activity to position appropriately. But it's enough that we can't tackle in just one post.

If you go back and re-read my post, "They are telling us so what are you waiting for?", you know that I'm positioned for where we are in the real estate/banking crisis cycle. They date back to the early 1800's when the stock markets got their start. Like clockwork, they start and expire on TIME.

The title of today's post is The 20-Year Chart. It is perhaps the most neglected chart of them all. And if you really claim to be a long-term investor, it should be referenced more frequently, if not the most. For companies that have a 20-year history, this chart can give incredible insight in to what we can similarly expect in price movements today at this point in the cycle. Why does this generally work? It requires an understanding of cycle duration and when they begin. We will get to that in this series. But for now, why not pull up the 20-year chart for all of your stocks that have a 20-year history and start thinking about how and why this gives us some insight with the timeframes I've laid out in previous posts. We'll explain more in later posts. Try this exercise before we get into that.

This is the way.

One example, $USAS, the wolves came in in the last 10 minutes to close above the open for one of the few stocks up on the day. We love to buy these stocks because we know that Wall Street can't risk NOT accumulating a stock with high conviction. I read and time these movements for extreme profits.

Really trying to help all. Best of luck!

title: Why Outperforming the S&P 500 Index Matters
caption:
By Vic Lederman

$2,074,904.21...

That's how much money a 68-year-old would have today if they had invested $10,000 in the S&P 500 Index when they were still 18 years old.

It's an average annual return of nearly 11.3%.

But it's only possible if they had reinvested all the dividends paid throughout all those years... and not spent a single penny of their gains.

That's a lot of patience to practice over 50 years.

As we get older, we (hopefully) learn to be more patient. The only problem is most older folks don't have 50 years to invest to see these kinds of returns materialize.

It takes a special kind of individual to learn to start investing at a young age and stay the course for 50 years without wavering.

That's just not the way most folks are programmed...

We tend to be aggressive risk-takers when we're younger. After all, it's hard to wait for that distant payday.

As we get older, we tend to become more risk-averse. And we usually don't mind waiting for the guarantee of stable returns.

That's why investors constantly seek outperformance. It's the only way to see those kinds of gains within a time frame that doesn't involve already being unable to enjoy life (or worse) by the time they cash out.

For instance, if you could find an investment that had a consistent average return just 3% higher than the S&P 500, you could cut down the waiting time by 10 years.

You would have nearly $2.1 million by the age of 58 instead of 68.

To bring that waiting time down by another 10 years, the investment must return an average 19.5% a year for three decades.

Now, investing for 30 years sounds doable. Start young, and you'll be barely 50 years old when that final big paycheck arrives.

But finding an investment that generates 19.5% annually for three decades is no small feat.

If fact, it's nearly impossible to do so without a little bit of both patience and time that I mentioned above.

As the late Charlie Munger – the legendary Warren Buffett's longtime business partner and friend – famously said...

Investing is where you find a few great companies and then sit on your ass.

It's not that investing in the S&P 500 Index is wrong. It's still one of the most profitable trades out there.

But if you want to live a comfortable life with the help of the stock market, you're going to have to find great companies that have the potential to deliver those kinds of outperforming gains.

Good investing,

Vic Lederman

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #14 - INSIDERS Among Us
caption:
I really didn't want to do this topic yet.  But I really want to help this community.  

I struggled with bringing the curriculum in order, with new concepts building on previous concepts to slowly extend the learning.  But these trading days don't come often and they tell you so much - so let's just skip a chapter and flip to the back of the book and check our answers.

Let's go.  As all know by now, we track INSIDERS (as defined in KNS #11)  knowing we can't beat all of them and we'll enjoy beating many of them and virtuallyall INVESTORS (as defined in KNS #11).  We agree that in order to sustain double-digit annualized returns, we need to read and listen to what the markets tell us vs. telling markets what they should do (buying stocks out of favor with hope).

That brings us to today.  A day like today with broad declines ACROSS THE BOARD - ALL INDICIES, ALL CAP SIZES, and MOST SECTORS based on an economic data release.  A perfect day to pull the curtain on the Wall Street wolves (INSIDERS) to see what they're hunting.

The INSIDERS typical feeding hours on any given day are simple:  9:30 am to 10:00 am EST and 3:30 pm to 4:00 pm EST.  It is in this combined 1-hour window each day that you will typically see big changes in price charts of any security. Check and prove it to yourself on any 1-day price chart for any security on any day.  WOLVES feeding time.  As we go forward, notice how often the market reverses its direction from out of the gate at market open and then changes course 30 minutes later.  INSIDERS just taking INVESTORS money. 

Keep in mind that when you run huge pooled accounts and funds and you loads of shares of a stock, the INSIDERS have to slowly accumulate under the cover of darkness - not to be detected. If discovered,  they will have to pay more per share if they REALLY want the stock.  So that's why you'll see big jumps/drops in share prices in the final 10 minutes of trading.  INSIDERS are accumulating/distributing shares. Again, trying their best not to be seen.  But you can't hide from the recorded ticker tape.  That's our deer stand!

So days like today are like panning for gold.  Shake the water & mud mixture in the screen and watch 95% of the muck fall through to the ground.  But what's left?  Those nuggets that remain are what the INSIDERS, who we know control 78% of the daily trading volume (KNS #11), are accumulating stocks/ETFs that meet this criteria:

(1)  CLOSE at (or practically at) their highest price for the day.

(2)  At least Average, but preferably Above Average or Heavy daily trading volume compared to its current daily average.

This is INSIDERS buying when everyone is supposed to be selling.  It's these stocks that the INSIDERS can't risk NOT to continue their accumulation plan.....because their conviction is so high.

Examples please?  You can run a stock screener with the above criteria, but I have stocks that I watchlist that popped up that you can check out for what I am talking about:

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #13- Macroeconomic Tool
caption:
We have come to accept that INSIDERS (as defined in KNS #11) command ~78% of the daily trading and thus control the market (reference KNS #11). And we have come to accept that the market's current prices discount future news by ~ 6 months - news that is both publicly available...and news that is NOT publicly available.

So how can we compete with INDISERS? We can't. Accept it. Nothing you can do gives you a computed first in advantage as a stock/ETF begins to rise. You can be first in.....but you have no control of TIME (KNS #10, #10A) - the INSIDERS control that and we've come to accept that. And TIME is critical to returns (e.g. 10% in 3 months 😃 versus 10% in 3 years 🤢).

So we aren't getting access to specific company news that INSIDERS get. BUT, we can tune in to current macroeconomic events that form their investment decisions in REAL-TIME. By doing this, we can understand the WHY in Wall Street activity as we read the ticker tape. It allows us to pull the trigger soon after we see the INSIDERS making moves. While it won't permit us to beat ALL INSIDERS, it will allow us to beat MANY INSIDERS and MOST ALL INVESTORS.

My recommendation for just 10 minutes per day to get this edge: A subscription to The Economist. As with 90% of news media, you will have to filter the liberal opinions from the facts - but you are good at that by now! The Economist has a daily 5 takeaways in world events delivered to your inbox (with vast resources of additional articles, links, etc. if your passion and interest extends beyond building your investment toolbox).

Keeping up with world economics is mandatory for consistent annualized double-digit returns. Economics influence supply chain, allow us to understand supply and demand relationships, wars, and how policies (stupid ones are best) are constantly creating investment ideas in, to some, as obscure stock picks.

Cost: $250 per year (used to be $55 at the turn of the century). You will easily recover this investment each year from just a single trade pairing as we cover how to read and TIME the markets.

In general, most economists are not wealthy but great at reading and explaining why in financial history - wheteher recent or historical. Traders make fun of them. And as a group they aren't the best forecasters and they have a terrible track record with markets. In fact, I only know a few extremely wealthy economists ( we will get to them later in KNS).

Recent examples that would have created wealth for you had you been tuned in AND with knowledge of reading the ticker tape, please? How about jumping on $VRT when it was in it's teens (now $80's) with insight that AI hardware would be first to leave the gate? Or how about when the price of oil was actually negative in March 2020 and $NOG 's stock's price was being accumulated around 50 cents per share? Imagine buying $XOM for $33 per share. I did with high confidence and low risk because I was tuned in to the macroeconomic environment. How about initiating a position in $DXJ and $DXJS, $MFG, $MUFG, and $SMFG in January 2023 when BOJ governor announced a plan to levy penalties on Japanese companies if they didn't start buying their shares back and dramatically increase their dividends? Warren and Charlie upped their stakes in Japanese trading companies 6 months later and announced their undervaluation to the world. These are just some examples that formed my current holdings but I have been routinely using this Macroeconomic tool for over 25 years.

How about some examples going forward? Did you know we are currently realizing and reversing the world's 17-year low in Copper inventories, which is critical to so many applications? So that allows us to jump on $TGB and $IVPAF and many more when we read the INSIDERS buying in their ticker tape. So many more coming....

Did you know natural gas has been negative since the beginning of March this year???? Did you know that the unofficial unemployment rate in China is 47% (likely due to single (spoiled) child policy? Did you know their population I'd falling off a cliff and they can't stabilize it? Did you know there is a vacancy glut of both residential and commercial real estate? Did you know that the stock:commodity ratio is at an ALL-TIME imbalance and that there's always a mean reversion? Look for my future trades to reflect when the INSIDERS start buying.

We are currently watching all of these developments WITH the INSIDERS. And while we accept we can't beat them all, we know we can beat most and definitely outperform our fellow INVESTORS.

Get yourself a solid macroeconomic tool. It will give you confidence and let you sleep peacefully at night when used in combination with reading markets for INSIDERS buying and selling (which we are in process of demonstating in KNS).

My aim is to help all.

This is the way.

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #12 - INSIDERS Tricks
caption:
Market Timing.

As you know by now from reading my Knowledge Transfer Series, I am a firm believer (in fact I have proven it to myself over and over again) that you can time markets. I am attempting to give you the HOW in this series of posts based on what I've compiled in my lifetime of learning and investing from the All-time greatest investors.....and they aren't all part of the regular crowd talked about with frequency.

So let's get to it. In KNS #11 (already referencing this one for its foundational basis), we come to accept that if we want superior double-digit annualized returns, we cannot be a market maker with hopes and wishes (which we will get slaughtered) and we must agree to be a subordinate INVESTOR that will listen and learn what the market (that we know and agree the INSIDERS control) is telling us by building our toolkit to read it correctly.

So let's jump right into it with 2 more (Remember RSI from the other day?) actionable and profitable MARKET TIMING tools developed by observing the INSIDERS chicanery over decades of data backtesting.

BUY SIGNAL INSIDER TIMING TOOL: When a company announces their quarterly earnings REPORT DATE at an EARLIER day then the past 2 years for same quarter, it is ALMOST ALWAYS a signal that the company will beat their quarterly earnings estimate, which is the strongest parameter correlation to an increasing stock price. This observation has been studied for years by others and I have personally observed and backtested to prove it to myself.

So let's look at some specific examples that are in the future ahead of us so you can see how timing can work!

First up is my largest holding $NOG. Two days ago, on April 4th, NOG issued their 1Q earnings release and conference call is scheduled for April 30th. Looking back at the past 2 years' 1Q earnings call dates: May 4th (2023) and May 5th (2022). Here's an example of a report date being bumped up earlier. Companies can anxious to do this to get their exciting news and financial position to the market sooner than later. So this tells us, based on reading the markets, very strong probability that NOG will handily beat earnings estimates and we should start BUYING THE STOCK RIGHT AWAY ahead of the announcement.

SELL SIGNAL INSIDER TIMING TOOLS: Conversely, when a company revises their earnings estimates for a particular quarter by pushing it to a later date (in comparisonto the same quarter over the past 2 years), we can expect bad news on earnings is coming. High probability that the stock's price will follow that earnings miss in decline. If you see a company revise the date and push it backward, that is not good as they likely need more time to financially engineer the books to present their best version of lipstick on a pig!

The SECOND SELL SIGNAL INSIDER TIMING TOOL is when you see a company release POSITIVE news a few days ahead of their earnings report date. This has been observed to be a common INSIDER trick to build up the stock price before they sell shares!!!!! Dirty dogs! 😡 Note: Don't confuse this tool with positive news announcements weeks ahead of an earnings announcement. Those types of news releases are part of the normal course of business and we expect a news flow like that for a company doing well.

Ok, that's it for today. Really just covered 3 MARKET TIMING TOOLS that you can start to test out for yourself and immediately take profitable actions!

Reminder to avoid the Conventionalists- they are out in full force with the recent market pullback. Stay the course.

This is the way.

title: Knowledge Transfer Series#11 Insiders vs Investors
caption:
This is the most important KNS information to date. Going forward, I will frequently reference KNS #11. So for those following closely, this is one to read and reread to understand the message. This is the base knowledge required to read and time the markets/securities. Not complicated by any means - but if you can't get there on the philosophy AND the psychology, then you need to find and follow your neighborhood Conventionalist for mediocre, general public annualized returns instead.

Ok, got the disclaimer out of the way. Here we go.

You are an Investor. You are NOT an Insider - and I'm estimating that 99% of us will NEVER be one. This is a foundational point that you MUST willingly accept. Reread the previous 3 sentences five times until it sets in. If you do not accept this fact, you will be saddled with investment strategies for the poor for your lifetime. This path will have you spending an enormous amount of your free time looking at stocks, ETFS, prospectuses, and funds with an annualized average return of ~7% over your lifetime. If this is acceptable, acknowledge your laziness or lack of interest in above average portfolio returns and jump in to your favorite S&P500 product.

Definitions. INSIDERS are all company officers and directors at all companies, Institutions (that pool other people's money together and make investment decisions with it), and some other privileged people (select sell-side analysts given favor by company officers when they cheer the company line or story).

INVESTORS on the other hand is us retail investors - or everyone else.

Fact #1: The INSIDERS command ~78% of the daily trading volume. So that leaves ~22% of the daily trading volume executed by us INVESTORS. And their impact is even less on price changes as they frequently debate amongst themselves, canceling strong buy or strong sell support.

Fact #2: CURRENT Market price charts and individual ETF/stock price charts discount future events and news by anywhere from 3 (1 quarter) to 9 months (3 quarters), but, on average 6 months (2 quarters). This is because INSIDERS know more about the company's forward prospects than news that is publicly available AND (they command the lion share in trading volume).

A price chart is simply a record of the supply and demand relationship of shares between the INSIDERS and the INVESTORS. When stocks are moving sideways,the spply-demand between INSIDERS and INVESTORS is BALANCED. INSIDERS are either ACCUMULATING shares from INVESTORS in anticipation of the next move up in stock price, or DISTRIBUTING shares to INVESTORS getting ready for the next drop in stock price. The key takeaway is that INSIDERS are the ones controlling the stock price movement. When a stock/ETF is rising, there are simply not enough shares to go around for all of the available demand for them. Conversely, when the stock/ETF price falls, there are more trying to exit a stock than willing buyers. Again, the key takeaway is that the INSIDERS control stock/ETF price movement.

In order to consistently realize double-digit, annualized returns, you must acknowledge that you do not have any power to be a market maker. You must resign yourself to read, listen, and follow the market makers. Much like a barnacle on a whale. Figure out how to tie yourself to a winning horse. This means checking your pride at the door and realize all the analysis and number crunching you do cannot influence the direction you think a stock will move IF the INSIDERS think otherwise. You simply cannot go head-tohead and beat them. There's no such David and Goliath stories on Wall Street. It is a shrewd place where INVESTORS are constantly having their capital transferring from INVESTORS to INSIDERS. But the advantage we have, there are no limitations to when, what and how much we can buy/sell. This is the huge advantage that Buffett and Lynch talk about frequently. But again, you must accept that you cannot move markets with hope - you will get absolutely slaughtered. You must learn to read and listen to the markets - to realize AMAZING annualized returns.

Going forward, we will touch on this frequently as we go into specific stocks, events, pullbacks, bounces, etc. It is all right there recorded in the ticker tape for all to read and see. Can't wait to share the specifics once this is digested carefully. Maybe give this another reread....to permanently ingrain in the brain. KNS #11.

This is the Way.

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #10 - TIME
caption:
Time.

It is the most forgotten, overlooked, and unloved investment parameter. The Conventionalists will surficially pay homage to it's existence with trite sayings like "TIME is Money!" or start investing now because it taks TIME. But then......their actions treat TIME like a throw-away parameter.

Have you ever noticed that most posts rarely reference TIME? People LOVE talking about stock prices and returns - but without TIME. How many talk about some stock or ETF going to some price level. $TSLA to $1,000+ . But isn't the biggest question - WHEN?

Here's why it matters. A 10% return can come in 3 months, or in 3 years. The TIME is the difference between an incredible investment (10% in 3 months annuallizes to 40% a year!) or a total waste of time (10% in 3 years annualizes ~3.5% per year - worse than money market right now or index fund).

Go check out the all-time stock price charts for 80-90% of all currently listed stocks that have a 20-year or more history. Guess what they have in common? THEY ALL GO UP. And this is where the Conventionalists get their fuel for their investment strategies......and their NEGLECT for TIME as an investment factor.

Yes, it's here, where their disdain for TIME allows the Conventionalists to blindly recommend you take your hard earned income and DCA (dollar cost averaging) into a stock in a long-term decling trend, diluting your returns even more - as if TIME was no issue!?!?!

Or how about the Conventionalists' "Word of the Lord" - "Buy Low, Sell High". Take your green wafer and repeat after me, "Buy Low, Sell, High". We all say it, right? Makes sense on the surface? But where is TIME in this sage advice?!?!?! The reality is that Conventionalists have no need for TIME...because, like we said above......80-90% of stocks all go up! And investing is a marathon. A complete disregard of TIME.

This Conventionalism is everywhere. Let's look to the Pros. They can be guilty too. Muppet Men (Charlie and Warren) are heroes of mine. I greatly appreciate their contributions to forming an investment thesis. I also recognize they get privileged opportunities to invest in securities that most don't because of their scale. And I also recognize that some of their advice can be questioned without context. I'm sure you are familiar with their quotes about buying when others are fearful or by when there's blood on the streets (after a stock gets "slaughtered ")? The other day I covered the stock history of $MSFT . Pull up the chart for reference. There was definitely "blood on the streets when the stock fell from ~$60 (late 1999) to ~$25 (late 2001). Conventionalists would have you come in and BUY $MSFT consistently via DCA all the way down from $60 because it was on "sale". Did you know that Microsoft went nowhere for a decade (2001: $25 per share, 2011: $25 per share). Imagine all of your investment capital just buying more and more shares amongst all the blood in the streets .....for a decade? Conventionalists would say, look at how much those shares are worth today as there measure for sound advice. ......as if TIME was no issue.

If you want outperformance with respect to TIME, you have got to overcome this thinking as acceptable. It is completely unacceptable and you need to put in some time to convince yourself of this. You cannot allow yourself to believe that buying a stock at the same price for a decade is successful investing. Instead, open your mind to the alternative and start talking about returns over TIME. No more saying something like "I got a 38% return on XYZ". Instead, we talk like this, "I got an 8% annualized return on XYZ."

Returning to our $MSFT example as it fell in 1999 until it started to rise again in 2012. Here are two approaches: (1) Conventionalists DCA: Buy 100 shares every year. Here are the price per share each year: 2000: $40, 2001: $25, 2002:$25, 2003: $25, 2004:$25, 2005:$25, 2006:$25, 2007:$25, 2008:$25, 2009:$25, 2010:$25, 2011:$25, 2012:$30. You would have spent $34,500 on $MSFT shares over those 13 years at a 2012 holding value of $39,000 for a return of 1% per year over that 13 year period.

Counter that with this approach I am asking you to consider: What if, instead of DCA $34,500 into $MSFT over 13 years, we take that $34,500 and buy $GOLD (Barrick Gold) in 2001 at $16 per share? We would have 2,156 shares. Over this time period we would have a 2012 holding value of $101,344 for a return of 17.6% PER YEAR, over this 11 year period.

Now, in 2012, you can sell all of your shares of $GOLD and use it all to BUY shares of $MSFT at $30 per share. Then, you would be holding 3,378 shares of Microsoft vs. The Conventionalist approach, holding 1,300 shares of Microsoft in 2012. Can you see the AMAZING difference? YOU WOULD HAVE 2.6X MORE SHARES IN $MSFT IN 2012 vs. Conventionalist DCA.

See KNS #10A for the conclusion.

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #9 - RSI
caption:
Before I lose the self-proclaimed Fundamental Investors who are longterm investors (of which is my core foundation as well - hey, I'm one of you!😂), there are some basic and easy Technical tools that I plan to work in to this Knowledge Transfer Series that we need to add to our toolkit to improve either short, mid, or longterm returns. So for those in that camp, try to stick it out!

RSI is the Relative Strength Index. For a trader, knowing a stock/ETF's current RSI is a must have puece of information before sending any buy/sell order. RSI is as common as a nail to a homebuilder. So let's acknowledge the Technicians can teach us something and that we aren't so arrogant to think we can't learn something from the Technicians,

The RSI is a measure of the speed and movement of a stock/ETF's price change. It moves constantly for every security within a range from 0 to 100. When the RSI is above 70, a security is generally considered overbought - and when a security is below 30, it is generally accepted that the security is oversold. Go ahead and pull up the RSI chart for your favorite 5 securities. Doesn't matter which ones. Look! They all look the same! For geeks, they look like sine waves, oscillating up and down constantly entering Overbought, reversing, going down to Oversold, reversing, going up to Overbought, reversing, going down to Oversold, reversing, going up to Overbought, on and on.....forever.... OK, you get it. Now, if you compare this RSI chart to the actual stock/ETF price chart, you'll see a fairly PREDICTABLE stock price direction that is well correlated to the RSI. In other words, the RSI tells us if the stock price is going up or down in the coming days or week ahead.

WOW! Wait a second, isn't that why we are here???? To make a "bet" on a stock price direction! Take the time to prove it to yourself with this exercise. Go look at the comparisons of the RSI charts to the stock/ETF price charts for each of your current holdings. FIND THE KEY INFLECTION POINTS - THERE ARE TWO PRIMARY POINTS: (1) The point where your stock's RSI just crosses above 30 and leaves Oversold territory, and (2) The point where your stock's RSI just crosses below 70 and leaves Overbought territory. In (1) above, this is an OPTIMAL point to buy. And (2) above is an OPTIMAL point to sell.

So whether you are a trader or a long-term investor, this is an easy tool to generally save an extra 1-3 % on your entry price by TIMING THE MARKET. Again, this works whether you plan to harvest those returns as a trader or a mid to longterm investor

Now this post is a baby step into RSI used in the most basic way. But this is a key one to reading what the ticker tape tells you - not what you tell the market. This basic application of this most common trader tool is just one MARKET TIMING TOOL that we have now. After we use it, analyze it, and prove to ourselves that it works as ot should (described above), we will start developing other tools that ALLOW US TO SUCCESSFULLY TIME MARKETS.

Watch out, here come the Fundamentalists in full force when they read this one! They will tell you that you can't time markets. They'll point to the ultra rich that said so, they'll point to their magazine articles, and 101 Investing for Dummies books. They'll spew the word of Certified Financial Planner....to keep a lid on your returns AND your dependency on their approach to the markets and their soliders. Ignore them. You can do this - just take the time and start really small to watch it work. YouMUST prove it to yourself first before we advance it's application to time markets in at a more macro level. Ask questions and share thoughts!

This is the way.

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #8 - Value or Growth?
caption:
Have you ever noticed how some investors are always seeking good growth stocks? Or how other investors dont dare to deviate from good value stocks? The big brokerages don't help either willingly offering growth-focused or value-focused investment vehicles.

What I think gets overlooked is the question why are investors hell bent on limiting their opportunities to either growth or value - especially when the classifications for individual stocks often change.

For example, leading up to the dotcom bust, $MSFT was climbing in stock price every day and peaked just shy of $60 a share. Soon thereafter, Microsoft's share price fell ..... and it took 17 YEARS for it to take out its previous high - that's right, October 2016 from October 1999. Had you bought into the frenzied peak in October 1999 and sold those shares 17 years later, you would have realized a gain of 0% over 17 years - not counting dividends. ZERO percent investing in the stock price appreciation in Microsoft after 17 years.

Even if you bought after the bubble burst, and collected shares at $20 per share, then sold after 10 years of frustration (~$25 per share) in going nowhere - when Wall Street was calling Microsoft a dividend-yielding value stock, you would have made 0% return, minus dividends.

And for younger investors, Microsoft was just as dominant in the Tech software space as they are today all through these time periods.

So Microsoft was LABELED as a value stock from 2001-2016. Their first dividend was initiated in 2003 and has been steadily increasing ever since. Growth rates were stodgy single digit percentages and Wall Street was talking like Microsoft was a dinosaur from another generation.

In 2016, the Cloud hype started to propel the stock again, and XBOX was a new business which led to the inevitable purchase of Activision. And now, ChatGPT. Since 2916, Microsoft has been humming with a bright future ahead as a GROWTH stock.

My point is that you won't find ANYONE labeling Microsoft as a Value stock anymore. But you also wouldn't find anyone o call them a Growth stock for the previous two decades either. As business changes, so can labels. Take Energy Transfer, $ET for example. Had you loaded up in December 2020 when it broke trend and started into accumulation phase around $6 per share, you would have realized 45% A YEAR (and that's NOT including their chunky ~10% dividend per year dividend over that period) over the past 4 YEARS!.......in a so-called VALUE stock. And ET's future earnings can't be classified as Value anymore with the projected growth of LNG. Once again, the classification will limit the investor who compartmentalized their investment strategy tool.

Right now, there is a flood of old, traditional "VALUE" stocks that will eventually be reclassified as GROWTH stocks by Wall Street. So why not broaden your screener and give these a look to boost your returns before Wall Street and the masses catch on?

Conversely, as we near the end of the cycle, we will see the transition of select growth stocks to value. Does Zuck's massive insider selling and Meta's dividend initiation show us something similar to MSFT's growth to value transition that happened in 2003......for a 16-year period? Time will tell.

But my point here is don't get caught up in labels (and growth vs. value is just one of them), and instead focus on companies with increasing earnings estimates that are able to achieve them, regardless of their "class". I did and found my portfolio made a dramatic shift into things I wouldn't buy IF I put rails on my investment strategy toolkit. Take a look at my core portfolio now and you'll see conservative "Value" companies in conservative industries on the verge of explosive growth.

Track my trades, portfolio, and posts to see how to continue to position within this final meltup before the cycle completes. title: Knowledge Transfer Series #7 - Use BOTH
caption:
Are you a Fundamental Investor or a Technical Trader? Most everyone is willing to take a classification badge and allow their approach to the markets be characterized into one of these compartments. If your answer to this question isn't BOTH, then you are leaving a lot of opportunity on the table.

Let's look ito why. A Fundamental Investor is one who digs into the company's finances, outlook, prospects, etc. to calculate valuation metrics for comparison to past calculations, peers within, industry, etc. They also want to know about debt levels and revenue growth and potential mergers/acquisitions, etc. It's when you RESEARCH a company for your perception of investibility.

Technical traders, on the other hand, don't really care about any of that. In fact, they mock "Funny Mentalists", and simply stay laser focused on a stock's price chart and it's slope. Technical traders believe the stock's current price reflects all news available at the moment, including news not publicly available - that would surely impact a Fundamentalists calculations.

Fundamentalists, also, have a disdain for Technicians, believing their interpretations is akin to reading tea leaves, astrology, Ouija boards, or just plain Voo Doo. Technicians have 50 different patterns and talk about how they are formed with human psychology constantly testing ceilings and floors with an explanation for either outcome of breaking through or holding strong.

They really mock each other and discount their approach to markets as just plain WRONG!

But the truth is.....they are BOTH exactly right. And I have learned that if you find investment ideas with strong Fundamentals and apply Technical interpretations to its stock price chart, you can identify a layered lower risk, higher return approach (support of strong buy indication from both a Fundamental and Technical point of view).

All will agree that a solid company can go through a stock price sideways movement or downward trend for an extended period of time. Fundamentalists will still ❤️ and dollar cost average thinking it is on "sale". Technicians will hate it because the stock price chart "trend is NOT your friend." Think $AAPL right now.

All will also agree that a stock chart can be on what seems like an everlasting rise (Technicians prize), but fundamentally unwarranted rise, which predictably will collapse because the fundamentals just aren't there. Think $AMC in meteoric rise.

I apply this dul-layered lower risk, higher return investment strategy as frequently as a plumber uses a wrench. As an example, I bought perennial Fundamental juggernaut $COST last year after a long period of decline as it's Technical chart pattern reversed above $500 per share. I sold out recently at $725 per share. Not because it isn't fundamentally strong. But because there are so many other dual-layered lower risk, higher return opportunities out there.

Learn both. Use both. Follow my posts, my portfolio, and my trades to see how it can work for you. And by all means Canadians, find a way to invest in U.S. stocks too!

This is the Way.

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #4 - DRIP
caption:
If you've been reading my series of posts, you know my intent is to help young investors avoid being coerced into conventional investing advice. Beware - it's everywhere......it's fed to the masses of us retail investors and regurgitated by many of us like programmed robots. Question everything. Backtest the advice. Challenge it, and if it passes the test, then subscribe. One thing to always keep in mind: In a relative measure, most all investors are underperformers and just a few investors are wealthy outperformers. So why would we just willing accept conventional advice delivered to the masses of underperformers. For now, we are born with brains, so let's use them!

Ok, let's get started. I frequently see posts of the benefits of DRIP (dividend reinvestment plans). This is where you buy a stock and it's quarterly dividend, instead of crediting cash into your account, automatically buys more shares of the stock. Over time, it juices the return of that stock investment. Sounds great, right? Maybe if you're lazy or you dont like tracking your investments - but you're not....because you're here on Blossom reading this in your precious time on Earth.

The conventionalists will have you believe this is a prudent thing to do. Why? There is nothing other than laziness that suggests you do this. Whoever told you.....do they get fees from such available DRIP investments? (I know not all stocks, ETFs have DRIP options - which is red flag #1). Or are those that tell you just repeating what they've been told because on the surface it sounds acceptable? A kind of sophisticated laziness?

Let's look into why DRIP is not a plan for us. First, you rceive dividends monthly, quarterly, or annually, but most stocks, it's quarterly. It is never true (that's right, I said NEVER), that buying your DRIP stock at the time you receive a dividend always a good time to buy that stock. You may have read my "Follow the $ector" post from the other day that can give you some insight on this.

A popular DRIP stock in the U.S. is WBA, Walgreen's pharmacy. Pull up a 10-year chart and imagine DRIPPING your quarterly dividends into that stock as it steadily dropped from $100 to $20 today. What a waste of dividends. Averaging is DIlution. You have a rationale, you do your homework, you make decisions at points in time to establish a position in a stock. So why then would you be willing to forgo your access to your quarterly cash reward and buy something that is currently overvalued? That makes zero sense - and dont let the conventionalists tell you otherwise.

Follow your current knowledge of the market and take that cask kick back and buy shares of a stock that has a likelihood to rise rather than fall. Test it out for yourself to convince yourself that there's always a better place to invest than your current DRIP stock. So put it there instead if your objective is to grow your nest egg.

I don't want to lose the point that it's fine to have a core holding that you plan to hold for a lifetime even when it's not in favor or it hits a rough patch in business (see TSLA). But when company's are in periods of declining profits, smarten up and don't take that monthly cash reward and try to catch a falling knife. Sometimes when you receive your dividend, buying more of the stock that gave it to you IS THE BEST place to put it. But the point is, not always. The pros know you can read cycles, markets, fundamentals (company earnings, debt, fcf, etc.), and technicals (charts). Ignore those that say you can't..........and I promise you the pros aren't in any kind of DRIP! Next time you get your dividend, ask yourself this question, "If you were given $10,000 right now but had to put it into one stock, which would it be?" Then do that with your (much smaller 😄) dividend instead.

Your not lazy when it comes to investing so stop diluting your performance and check out my previous post on "Follow the $ector" to learn how to better redirect your dividends in a systematic, profitable way.

Its to easy to just throw your hands up and say "nobody knows" But you can be a much smarter investor - reach for your full potential! Best of luck!

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #3- Don't Label Yourself
caption:
Pull up the Greatest Investors. They have labels. Benjamin Graham- The Value Inestor, Sir John Templeton - the Contrarian, Thomas Rowe Price, Jr. - The Stock Picker for the Long-Term, John Neff - The Value with High Dividend Yield Investor, Jesse Livermore - The Trader, Peter Lynch - The Observationalist Investor, George Soros - the Big Bet, Short Term Speculator, Warren Buffett - Value for the Long Term Investor, Jack Bogle - The Index Investor, Carl Icahn - The Activist Investor, Bill Gross - The Bond King Investor.

All of them have the performance to prove their greatness. But which one is the best? Which one is more in line with your investment strategy?

As a new investor, I would urge you not to run into any classification or investment style. Instead, learn from these greats and build your investment strategy toolkit so you can apply the best investment for the ever changing current market conditions.

I see posts of Blossomers that say everyone has their investment strategy that works for them. But why limit your potential to a defined investment strategy? The reality is that different market conditions warrant different investment strategies. New investors should be accumulating all of these different tools in their toolbox just as a new homeowner would. In the world of investing, there's times where the market favors value investing or growth investing, or income investing, or index investing, or market cap size investing, international investing, etc. Why not develop knowledge in all and expertly apply the right tool for the right market?

Have you ever had a need for a socket wrench but you grab the needle nose pliers (because that's what you have available) and try to work out the screw?

Back to the markets, there are both short and long periods of time (months or even decades) where one style can outperform another (e.g. growth vs. value, domestic vs. International, aggressive vs. Defensive, etc.). So suggest we start building the toolbox so we have the right tool for the right market. How did Bill Gross do with his bonds over the past decade of stock appreciation? How did investing in Japan over the past year do compared to investing in Japan over the prior 17 years?

So it's clear, the objective should be to learn all strategies. Later in this series, I plan to post how to develop an understanding of what kind of market you're in so you can apply these newly acquired tools.

But for now, quit calling yourself an ETF Investor, or a Momentum Investor, or a Tech Investor! You have the potential to be so much more than that.....so get busy and build your own toolbox by learning from the greats!

title: Knowledge Transfer Series #2 - Follow the Sector!
caption:
Convenentionalists will put you in an S&P500 and tell you to forget it. There's a statististic, often regurgitated by the masses that some 80-90% of investors can't beat the SPY. If you don't have the time or care to have your nest egg working for you - that can be a lazy plan. But for anyone willing to dig in and learn - realizing that routinely outperforming the SPY by just 1% outperformance has an incredibly profound impact on your portfolio.

Seems like a daunting task in a world of naysayers. But I am sharing a lower risk ETF strategy that is a relatively straightforward way to do just that.

After years of monitoring on a daily basis and decades of backtesting, this strategy works extremely well to outperform the S&P500 ....... by a lot more than 1% each year! Research shows that ~50% of a stock's return is attributed to the movement of the sector to which it belongs (the rising tide lifts all boats phenomenon). The other half of the stock's performance is related to the stock's fundamentals. Because this observation is constantly repeating itself on a daily basis, you will see times where companies with weak balance sheets rise when they shouldn't (sector in favor) and fall really hard when it should (sector out of favor, fundamentals are weak). Vice versa for a company whose sector/subsector is in favor that has strong fundamentals (e.g. NVDA).

So there are 11 sectors and 20 subsectors. Each one has an ETF. Adopting a systematic approach of rotation will allow you to routinely outperform the S&P500. Once you dig in on this, you will learn so much more on how to read the market and understand what the market is telling us - not what we want to tell the market. Here's just one gem of insight as an example. Pull up a year to date chart on SPY (S&P500 ETF), XITK (Innovative Technology ETF), KCE (Capital Markets ETF), and XLE (Energy ETF). Which one do you think was the best performer so far in 2024? You are probably surprised to learn that it's XLE, the energy ETF. Not many talking about it. This kind of advantaged insight presents itself persistently as sectors and subsectors fall in and out of favor with Wall Street - either by design or by fundamentals.....or both. But you don't need to know why, you just need to know how to read the markets instead to routinely get a leg up on the S&P500.

This is a key point. A lot of Blossomers are trying to be market makers with hope and buy stocks they WANT to reverse course and go up, regardless of sector favorability. When the simple approach is to "Follow the Sector!"

Once you gain confidence in this strategy, you can move on to a more advanced strategies that I use that work in all markets. Hint: Watch my portfolio and you'll find select stocks within the favored sectors......for even more outperformance.

Trying to really provide proven, actionable perspectives that run counter to the conventionalist propaganda fed to the masses of us retail investors. You CAN do it - Good Luck!

title: Knowledge Transfer Series - #1 Inflation
caption:
After reading posts in Blossom, I decided to initiate a series of sharing alternative perspectives that you won't hear in the popular media propaganda, but can be very informative for taking profitable actions.

I thought I'd kick it off with a discussion of inflation. Mainstream media (and a lot of Blossomers regurgitate what they hear from these talking heads) will talk about the decrease in inflation and we are in the final stages of reaching the inflation target of 2% per year. Sound familiar? Well, it's wrong....really wrong. And it's not hard to figure out why. Last December most were calling for rate cuts beginning in March 2024. No cuts. Now, there's a 50% chance of a rate cut in June. This Wednesday, we'll learn that it is a 0% chance of a rate cut in June. Most will be surprised but they don't have to be. Read the market cycle and you'll know that in the meltup phase that rate cuts don't come until the very end of the cycle. So ask yourself, do you really want rate cuts? Well the good news is they aren't coming anytime soon. The markets will do what they do after the reality sets in Wednesday when the Fed speaks. But then, the resumption of the stock market climb of select sectors will continue to March higher. Inflation control is beyond the Fed's toolkit.

Look around - do you see all the infrastructure spending going on? Do you see GDP rising along with companies earnings estimates? Do you read these posts of investors retiring early? What will that do to the unemployment rate? Do you know anyone traveling or buying something special this year? This economy is 🔥 and doesn't need further fuel to spend more. But it's human nature at this point in the cycle. Businesses, governments, and individuals that have invested will spend more and more. Do you think our fellow Blossomers will keep all their money invested after realizing 50% or even 100% or more on select investments? No way. The spending keeps going and increasing inflation is a byproduct of it.
This is just the start of re-inflation and the proxies for it are confirming it. Check out GCC, USO, and DBA. Check out the miners TGB, NGD, and WRN as just some examples. Check out oil and gas, CVE, XOM, FANG. What is the recent movement in these charts telling us about the future? The pros know....and they are buying when the masses are talking about Magnificent 7 or Fantastic 4 and when no one is looking. This is critical to knowing where the market is going vs. where the market has been.

These Legg Eggs really took off and started showing up everywhere in the 1980's. He dug in on the financials and boom - an investment idea became a core holding just like that. I think this is timely advice for this market and Blossom investors. We are in a stock pickers market with a long runway ahead of momentum buying of stocks that will continually make new 52-week highs on a daily basis. This will continue for years as select stocks are grossly undervalued. This is the final meltup phase of the cycle where you can realize a decade worth of gains in just two years, if you do some simple homework and invest in the right stocks. So consider putting your pride and hope aside and work FOR the health of your hard-earned life savings by picking stocks that satisfy BOTH criteria. You can always come back to the ones you cheerlead for when, and if, they satisfy both criteria. title: What did we learn from Chamath Palihapitiya?
caption:
Remember the self-proclaimed "King of SPACS" ? He had a Midas touch on a few early SPAC deals when the repackaged IPO vehicle was re-introduced to us retail investors (after more than a decade of mothballing). He would compare his results to Warren Buffett (Berkshire Hathaway) and even made a prediction that "Nobody's going to listen to Buffett. Buffett doesn't have the energy to say what he said 30 and 40 years ago in 2021."

But that all quickly changed when interest rates changed and the SPAC world fell apart. Chamath got a huge helping of humble pie......that he still hasnt finished yet, 3 years later. Now, nobody thinks he's genius, nobody is giving him a stage. Nobody listens to him. Investing in IPOs isn't brilliant - its more just a privilege of already being rich and just having access.

Reflecting on the Reddit frontman's fall from a brief stint of stardom, and that era of arrogant youthful swagger and ageist name calling, I am really encouraged by this Blossom community and the number of you that cite those that have been there before like Buffett, Munger, Lynch, and others as you begin on your investing journey. Those lessons have accumulated over 200 years of stock market activity. What hasn't ever changed is that the truly successful ones learn from those that came before and created this world for us and by studying the past. title: They are telling us so what are you waiting for?
caption:
This is the time to invest in commodities. Here's a laundry list of reasons why: (1) Ego-maniac Jamie Dimon just said this morning that he puts a hard recession at 65% while advising the Fed to hold off cutting rates until at least June. These statements may seem at odds - but he's telling us the economy is really humming and needs to slow rather than throwing more gasoline on the fire (easing monetary policy) that will make the inevitable recession that much harder. (2) Inflation has been re-accelerating and Dimon noted that as well calling for likely stagflation. (3) The markets are signaling the same as precious metals continue an everlasting climb. (4) Fuel prices are creeping up every week and the official "driving season" isn't even here yet. (5) Miners across the globe are issuing notes to increase capital for exploration projects. (6) History dating back to early 1800's show that History repeats - or at least rhymes for the final years of the meltup before the cycle ends. (7) Listen to the companies themselves on their earnings calls for their FY2024 outlook. I do across industries - they are all talking about how earnings will grow but be impacted (to various extents) b increasing inflation. (8) Most companies forwardearnings estimates are rising which is not a sign that the economy needs further boosting. (9) These Biden government moves to increase liquidity (homebuyer credits, credit card interest fee reduction, free refinance, etc.) are going to make the reinflation that much worse. and (10) The cycle always ends in collapse and will take the markets (as a whole) down with it. The great news is that it takes time to develop as the government will do all kinds of creative banking and financial engineering to delay and attempt to prevent it (which will make the inevitable recession that much worse). That means Blossomers have a ~2-year meltup that will continue to deliver phenomenal returns before the collapse.

Follow me and I will keep you updated as we progress through this final stage. This is going to be awful when it happens as the banks find exotic investments to package and transfer to the masses, who - as always - will be left holding the bag. Start counting the number of times you hear "CRE" every day and record it on paper. Watch this grow over time. CRE will eventually take it all down. Remember, these are just mortgages for empty office space, etc. whose owner hasn't been receiving any revenue to pay the monthly payments... for YEARS. They are going to do their absolute best to find ways to put some of this into all of our portfolios.

To avoid it, buy commodities now. Oil and gas, grains, metals, miners, precious metals, homebuilders, logistics, industrials banks (not regional or any that have CRE). Other speculative assets will also perform well. That's small/micro cap, crypto, AI, and biotech. But beware, you must monitor the exit point as it all goes higher and higher. I suspect it has a couple years left of absolutely amazing returns where you can realize a decade of returns in just 2 years. But then.........🤢😱😵😓😩😡

For young investors on this site, you will want to assess your true level of experience and pay attention to the experienced ones that care about this community. For now and the next couple of years, it's BUY, BUY, BUY.......until it's BYE, BYE, BYE. Follow me to stay tuned. Good Luck!
